title: Data Engineering Manager
description_text:
Are you passionate about Data & AI? 🤖 
At Satori Analytics, we aim to change the world one algorithm at a time by bringing clarity to global brands through Data & AI. From cloud-based ecosystems for fintech to predictive models for airlines, our cutting-edge solutions cover the entire data lifecycle—from ingestion to AI applications. 
As a fast-growing scale-up, our team of 100+ tech specialists—including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, and more—delivers innovative analytics solutions across industries like FMCG, retail, manufacturing and FSI. Join us as we lead the data revolution in South-Eastern Europe and beyond!
What Your Day Might Look Like:
Technical & Delivery Leadership
Own and evolve data engineering standards, best practices, and architectural principles across Satori projects
Act as a senior technical authority for complex data platforms (cloud data stacks, pipelines, streaming, orchestration)
Support project teams in solution design, risk management, and technical decision-making
Review and challenge designs to ensure scalability, performance, security, and cost efficiency
Partner with Tech Leads to ensure consistency and quality across projects
People Management & Leadership
Line-manage Senior Data Engineers and Tech Leads, supporting growth, performance, and career progression
Coach engineers on technical depth, ownership, communication, and leadership behaviors
Contribute to performance reviews, development plans, and promotion decisions in alignment with Satori’s competency framework
Actively role-model Satori’s values around collaboration, transparency, and accountability
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Work closely with POs to align technical solutions with client needs and delivery constraints
Partner with Data Science, AI, and Cloud teams to ensure smooth end-to-end solutions
Support presales and discovery phases by contributing technical input, estimations, and solution framing when needed
Organizational Impact
Identify gaps in skills, tooling, or processes and propose pragmatic improvements
Contribute to internal initiatives (guilds, playbooks, training, knowledge sharing)
Help scale data engineering capability as Satori grows, without losing quality or culture
Your Superpowers🚀:
8+ years of experience in Data Engineering, with exposure to complex, production-grade data platforms
Proven experience in leading senior engineers and/or technical teams
Strong expertise in modern data stacks (e.g. cloud platforms, data warehouses, ETL/ELT, orchestration, streaming)
Experience working in consulting or project-based environments is a strong plus
Ability to balance hands-on technical depth with strategic thinking
What Success Looks Like: 
Data engineering projects are delivered with high technical quality and predictability
Engineers feel supported, challenged, and clear about expectations and growth paths
Tech Leads and Product Owners trust you as a partner, not just a reviewer
Satori’s data engineering capability becomes more consistent, scalable, and resilient
Perks on Perks:
Competitive salary and hybrid work model – come hang out in our Athens office or work remotely from anywhere in European economic Area (EU, Switzerland etc.) or UK (up to 6 weeks per year).
Training budget to level up your skills from the top tech partners in the market (Microsoft, AWS, Salesforce, Databricks etc.) – whether it’s certifications or courses, we’ve got you covered.
Private insurance, top-tier tech gear, and the chance to work with a stellar crew.
Ready to create some data magic with us? Hit that apply button and let’s get started.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves owning and evolving data engineering standards and acting as a senior technical authority for complex data platforms, while also managing and coaching Senior Data Engineers and Tech Leads to ensure technical depth and career progression. This includes partnering with Product Owners and other technical teams to align solutions with client needs and delivery constraints.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess over 8 years of experience in Data Engineering, including proven leadership of senior engineers and strong expertise in modern data stacks across cloud platforms, data warehouses, and ETL/ELT processes. Experience in consulting or project-based environments is considered a strong advantage.
linkedin_org_slogan: Clarity in decision making through Data and AI.
linkedin_org_locations: 18 Miltiadou, Athens, Attiki 105 60, GR
New Union Square, Embassy Gardens, Ambassador Building, London, UK SW11 7BR, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Changing the world one algorithm at a time.

Satori is a term to describe “the moment of clarity”.

We are an Analytics Agency made with one simple vision: To give clarity in decision making, through data and AI.

With teams of certified expert architects, analysts, data and AI engineers, we have the depth and experience to deliver simpler and complex data-centric solutions reliably, efficiently and repeatably.

Over the past 10 years our people have been delivering innovative solutions to global brands across multiple industries in Financial Services, Retail, FMCG, Energy, Manufacturing, Health and others. Whether it’s a best practices cloud data estate design, a scalable and cost-efficient data warehouse, lake or lakehouse, intuitive and performing BI, optimisation and machine learning, generative (Open)AI and cognitive services, we’ve done it.

With a diverse client portfolio we are proud to say we have a >90% retention rate and long standing relationships as a trusted data and AI partner with some of the biggest brands in Europe and beyond.

If you want to know more about Satori's services, projects, and clients visit our website and feel free to get in touch directly with our people.

If you are a prospective Satorian and want to have a career in building advanced data and AI products for the best companies out there and be part of true innovation, visit our career page and send us your CV!

title: Unanet Staff Accountant
description_text:
Ready to Ignite your career and work alongside some of the most innovative and brightest professionals in innovative technology? Join us and unleash your potential working in an Agile environment while being part of one of the fastest growing government service providers in the country. 
As a Staff Accountant, you will be responsible for maintaining financial records, ensuring compliance with IRS rules and regulations, recording statements, and balancing ledgers for regular reports. The right candidate will be a proactive, knowledgeable 'doer' who is not afraid to roll up their sleeves and get to work as part of the team. 
Review financial statements for accuracy and legal compliance
Prepare and file tax returns
Enter accounting related information into business logs
Inspect account books for efficiency and accuracy
Organize and update financial records
Recommend ways to reduce costs and enhance revenue
Perform monthly bank settlements
Prepare documentation for external auditors
Demonstrated Experience with Unanet
Demonstrated Experience with QuickBooks Online
US Citizenship Required
Ignite IT offers a comprehensive salary and benefits package to include Medical, Dental, and Vision; Short Term/Long Term/Life Insurance; 401K with matching and fully Vested 100%; PTO; Holiday; Training; and much more…
Ignite IT is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, Veteran status, sexual orientation, or other protected characteristic. In accordance with EO 13665 Final Rule, Ignite IT will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant.
Benefits Include:
401(k)
401(k) matching
Dental insurance
Flexible schedule
Flexible spending account
Health insurance
Health savings account
Life insurance
Paid time off
Professional development assistance
Referral program
Retirement plan
Tuition reimbursement
Vision insurance
ai_core_responsibilities: The Staff Accountant will be responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, ensuring compliance with IRS rules and regulations, recording necessary statements, and balancing ledgers for regular reporting. Duties also include reviewing financial statements for accuracy and legal compliance, preparing and filing tax returns, and organizing financial records.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate must be a proactive, knowledgeable 'doer' comfortable working in an Agile environment within a fast-growing government service provider. Demonstrated experience with Unanet and QuickBooks Online is required, and US Citizenship is mandatory for this role.

title: Software Engineer: Full-Stack (Multiple Positions Available)
description_text:
Job Title: Software Engineer: Full-Stack or Software Engineer: Full-Stack (Data)
Salary: $100,000-$220,000 + equity options. 
Location: Remote with occasional in-person work in our co-working space in downtown Toronto.

About Us Fiscal.ai (formerly FinChat) is a leading research and data platform for capital markets. Combining a powerful research Terminal with modern APIs, Fiscal.ai is building the modern financial data company. The firm has raised $13M in venture backing and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada. With thousands of customers and millions of end-users through their API offerings, Fiscal.ai is building the premiere financial data company in the AI era.

Brief Description of the Role:
This role will be joining our growing and talented engineering Team. As a fast-growing company, you will be building at the intersection of the AI and finance. 

There are two main responsibilities of the engineering Team:
Building the Fiscal.ai Terminal product
Building the Fiscal.ai Financial Data infrastructure (and API product)
Both require deep understanding of the full stack, LLMs and finance. If you think this is the perfect fit for you, we'd love to specifically hear why!
You are a 10x engineer. If you're not sure, no need apply. You need to write good code, fast. 
Proficiency in TypeScript, React, NextJS. 
Experience working with LLMs.
Strong background working with and/or building APIs.
Comfortable developing across the entire stack and on multiple products. Strong ability to develop beautiful front end Terminal interface for users as well as support backend data engineering.
Experience with data parsing. 
Experience with large databases. 
Experience with python. 
Proven experience or strong interest in investing and finance. Our platform is built by investors, for investors, so a solid understanding of investment concepts is essential, especially when working with advanced data and financial concepts.
Bachelors or equivalent in Computer Science, Software Engineering. For some candidates, we may make exceptions ie) self-taught.
Experience or interest in AI technologies, specifically Large Language Models (LLMs). 
Keen eye for design, UI, UX. 
Based in Canada.

Tech Stack:
We work with a diverse set of tools and technologies, including:
React, Tailwind, Node, Next.js
Cloudflare, Firestore, Firebase, SQL.
Competitive salary
Equity options
Unlimited vacation
Benefits (dental, massage, prescriptions, vision, etc.)
Remote-first work with occasional in-person work in our co-working space in downtown Toronto.
Salary: $100,000-$220,000 + equity options. 
Why the huge range? Fiscal.ai hires engineers based on talent and fit with our company, not based on traditional years of experience. Our salary offer will be determined on these factors within that range. 
Number of Positions Available: Two (2) 
Type: Full-time
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves joining the engineering team to build at the intersection of AI and finance, focusing on either the Fiscal.ai Terminal product or the Financial Data infrastructure and API product.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be proficient in TypeScript, React, and NextJS, possess experience with LLMs, and have a strong background in building and working with APIs across the full stack.
linkedin_org_slogan: Fiscal.ai (formerly FinChat) is the modern financial data terminal.
linkedin_org_description:
The complete investment research platform for global equities.

FinChat.io is owned by Stratosphere Technology Inc.

title: Financial Operations Specialist (Payroll and Billing)
description_text:
Financial Operations Specialist (Payroll & Billing)
Location: Remote
Company: Maxana
About Us
Maxana is a fast-growing technology consulting and staffing firm that supports enterprise clients with engineering talent. Behind every successful placement is strong operational execution — and that’s where you come in.
We’re hiring a detail-obsessed Financial Operations Specialist to own payroll accuracy, invoicing integrity, and financial back-office processes that keep our consultants paid and our clients billed correctly.
If you love clean numbers, organized systems, and catching mistakes before anyone else sees them — you’ll thrive here.
What You’ll Do
You’ll be the backbone of our financial operations.
Payroll & Timekeeping
Run and manage payroll in ADP 
Collect, validate, and reconcile consultant timesheets 
Audit hours, pay rates, and approvals for 100% accuracy 
Ensure on-time payments for contractors and employees 
Resolve payroll discrepancies quickly and professionally 
Billing & Invoicing
Generate accurate customer invoices in QuickBooks 
Match timesheets to contracts and billing terms 
Verify rates, hours, and client-specific requirements 
Track invoice status and assist with follow-ups 
Prevent revenue leakage and billing errors 
Operations & Reporting
Maintain organized financial records and documentation 
Reconcile payroll and invoicing data weekly/monthly 
Create reports for leadership (hours, revenue, margins, etc.) 
Improve processes and automate wherever possible 
Serve as the go-to person for payroll or billing questions 
Must-Have (non-negotiable)
Deep, hands-on ADP payroll experience 
Expert-level QuickBooks (invoicing, reporting, reconciliation) 
Strong proficiency in Excel
2–5+ years in payroll, accounting ops, or financial operations 
Extreme attention to detail (you naturally double-check everything) 
Comfortable working with numbers all day 
Highly organized and process-driven 
Able to work independently with minimal oversight 
Fully Remote Work 
Competitive Pay 
Working for an industry leading, tech forward company
Flexible Hours
ai_core_responsibilities: You will manage payroll and timekeeping, ensuring accuracy and on-time payments for contractors and employees. Additionally, you will handle billing and invoicing, generating accurate customer invoices and tracking their status.
ai_requirements_summary: The role requires deep hands-on experience with ADP payroll and expert-level proficiency in QuickBooks. Candidates should have 2-5 years of experience in payroll, accounting operations, or financial operations, with a strong attention to detail.
linkedin_org_locations: 10175 W Twain Ave, Las Vegas, Nevada 89147, US
linkedin_org_description:
Maxana is a digital transformation partner that understands your objectives and requirements, quickly assesses your current technology ecosystem, and delivers your FinTech Digital Initiatives.  Maxana combines best in class client delivery with a focus on transparency and engineering competency enabling outcomes that deliver the highest possible return on your FinTech investment dollars.

Professional Services and Staffing
Maxana’s Professional services and staffing enables our clients to achieve their Fin Tech goals and objectives. Maxana provides CTO level oversight with quality delivery with a customer first mindset. Our staffing and services run the gamut from Enterprise and solution architecture, best in class engineering and development teams, or discrete individual contributor resources to support your programs and projects.

Custom Integration Solutions
Maxana’s Custom Integration Solutions Teams maximize business value by rapidly designing and implementing best in class integrations. Our custom integration solutions securely extend the functionality of your applications with new and existing third-party SaaS, open and proprietary platforms, and enterprise systems quickly driving both accelerated revenue and amazing customer outcomes.

Payment Processing
Not every company will need the same kind of online payment software, which is why Maxana designs systems for our clients from the ground up. Bespoke software, off-the shelf solutions..whatever your company needs, Maxana we’ll deliver it in a cost-effective, rapidly deployable manner.

title: AI Solutions Partner
description_text:
Want a 3D Career? Join Norgine.
At Norgine, our colleagues Dare themselves to be different and try new things, Drive to achieve their goals and beyond, and Develop themselves and their community. 
We call it the 3D career at Norgine and it offers you a fully-rounded experience with no limits. 
Bring everything about yourself that you’re proud of, whether that’s your passion for making a difference, focus on others’ well-being, or intellectual curiosity to unleash in a fast-paced environment and supportive community. 
In return, get a sense of belonging, a long-term career with ongoing development and upskilling, and a company that cares about people’s wellness as much as you do. 
Norgine: Transforming Healthcare, Backed by Over a Century of Expertise.
We have an exciting opportunity for an AI Solutions Partner to join Norgine.
The person holding this position will report to the Senior Manager Data & AI Enablement and be a member of the IT Operations team.
The core responsibility of the AI Solutions Partner is to act as a strategic advisor and orchestrator, working across business functions to identify, design, and implement AI solutions that meet business needs. The role selects appropriate AI frameworks aligned to Norgine’s infrastructure and future scalability, and leads the end-to-end journey from opportunity identification to business value realisation, coordinating vendors and internal teams for safe, compliant, and high-impact delivery.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:
• Explore and understand external technology developments and assess their relevance for Norgine.
• Collect business requirements using interviews, workflow analysis and workshops, expressing these in terms of user needs and goals.
• Support documentation of “as is” and “to be” processes and define required changes.
• Review existing operations and implement innovation processes to drive continuous improvement.
• Deliver digital solution architecture including conceptual and logical diagrams aligned with future-state design.
• Consult stakeholders on data standardisation and recording processes to ensure quality.
• Coordinate subcontractors and suppliers, acting as a key point of contact within established contract plans.
• Support implementation of quality assurance initiatives and drive operational improvements.
• Contribute to security implementation through monitoring and escalation of risks.
• Plan and manage delivery of AI projects using appropriate project management methods.
• Develop stakeholder engagement approaches by identifying needs, issues, and concerns to support effective communication and decision-making.
• Build personal capability through ongoing training, coaching, accreditation, and continuous learning.
• Comply with all rules, regulations, and guidance relevant to the role.
Tasks include:
• Maintain an enterprise AI use case backlog and prioritise initiatives based on value, feasibility, risk, learning, and opportunity cost.
• Prepare lean business cases with Finance to secure sponsorship and funding.
• Define governance guardrails for each initiative, embedding Responsible AI, privacy, security and regulatory compliance from the outset.
• Experience sufficient to handle a wide range of situations and advise others, ideally in business analysis, product/portfolio management, consulting, or digital transformation.
• Proven experience in vendor orchestration and delivery oversight in a regulated environment.
• Strong facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills, with familiarity in AI tools, governance frameworks, and compliance standards.
• Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional initiatives from discovery to value realisation.
Managerial Experience
• Experience supervising or guiding more junior colleagues.
This job description is a summary of the typical functions of the job; not an exhaustive or comprehensive list of all possible responsibilities, tasks and duties. Norgine reserves the right to change responsibilities to meet business and organisational needs as necessary.
Our benefits may vary per location. Please liaise with the Norgine TA representative to obtain more information.
If you want a multi-dimensional 3D career in a leading healthcare organisation, join us.
ai_core_responsibilities: The AI Solutions Partner acts as a strategic advisor and orchestrator, identifying, designing, and implementing AI solutions across business functions while selecting appropriate AI frameworks. This role leads the entire process from opportunity identification to business value realization, coordinating internal teams and vendors for compliant and high-impact delivery.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates need sufficient experience to advise others, ideally in business analysis, product management, consulting, or digital transformation, along with proven experience in vendor orchestration and delivery oversight within a regulated environment. Strong facilitation, stakeholder engagement skills, and familiarity with AI tools, governance, and compliance standards are essential.

Today’s Norgine is a nimble, innovative, and high-performing company that has been transformed by a relentless focus on operational excellence to do the right thing by patients, push boundaries, and take strides into new therapeutic areas. The company’s integrated approach – strong commercial capabilities, deep medical, regulatory and clinical expertise, in-house manufacturing, robust supply networks, and best-in-class enabling functions – ensures delivery of high-quality, transformative medicines quickly and effectively to more than 25 million patients annually.

title: Sales Representative
description_text:
Our client in the UK is seeking a proactive Sales Representative to help qualify incoming leads as the business scales. The ideal candidate will be confident, “on the ball,” and able to manage basic lead qualification tasks independently. 
Key Responsibilities
Respond to incoming leads via phone or other communication channels. 
Qualify leads using straightforward, structured questions. 
Log lead interactions and meeting setups accurately in a tracking system or spreadsheet. 
Communicate effectively with the sales team regarding lead status and follow-ups. 
Support the sales team in maintaining an organized and up-to-date lead pipeline. 
Strong communication and listening skills. 
Self-motivated, proactive, and able to work independently. 
Highly organized with attention to detail. 
Comfortable using spreadsheets or basic CRM tools to log activities. 
“Switched on” and able to think quickly when handling incoming leads. 
Previous experience in sales in a marketing environment. 
Why Join:
Opportunity to play a key role in scaling the business. 
Flexible, remote-friendly work environment. 
Gain hands-on experience in sales processes and lead management.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Representative will be responsible for responding to incoming leads via various channels and qualifying them using structured questions. Key duties also involve accurately logging interactions and meeting setups in tracking systems and communicating lead status updates to the sales team.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate must be confident, proactive, and capable of working independently to manage basic lead qualification tasks. Essential requirements include strong communication and listening skills, high organization, and comfort using spreadsheets or basic CRM tools, along with previous sales experience in a marketing environment.

title: Project Manager Co-op - Ireland (2026)
description_text:
SRA's mission is to level up every day to protect our clients and their customers. This begins with our team members and their experience. SRA prides itself on maintaining a culture where team members have a shared sense of support and belonging, consistent with our It's Personal company value. At SRA, we prioritize transparent career pathing, varied DEI programming and community groups, competitive benefits including mental health support, and an emphasis on a sustainable, healthy, and engaging work culture. SRA has twice been named a Best Place to Work by the Philadelphia Business Journal. 
These Essential Functions, Requirements, and Skills are guidelines. If you are a candidate who does not meet this exact job description but can demonstrate excellent organization, attention to detail, professionalism, flexibility, and self-direction in your professional background, we hope you apply. 
Summary/Objective 
The Project Manager Co-op position will be part of Security Risk Advisors, Technical Project Management team. Our style of project management is dynamic, innovative, fast- paced, and highly rewarding for both our clients and our team. This is an outstanding opportunity to work with a wide variety of project management tools and process across various well-known client organizations. 
Essential Functions 
Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 
Ensure that all aspects of a project are organized and in conformance with timeline and deliverables requirement. 
Facilitate cross-team and external client collaboration, ensuring alignment of project objectives with organizational goals and fostering a cohesive team environment. 
Engage with stakeholders to manage expectations, define project scope, and secure commitment and resources for successful project execution. 
Encourage a proactive approach to problem-solving, empowering team members to address day-to-day operational issues and scope changes effectively. 
Ensure comprehensive documentation and reporting of project progress, adhering to organizational policies and procedures, and facilitating informed data driven decision-making. 
Must possess the aptitude to rapidly learn and become proficient in the use of information security-specific jargon and acronyms, effectively assimilating, and applying the specialized terminology used daily in InfoSec 
Eventually drive continuous improvement initiatives by critically evaluating existing project management processes and introducing scalable solutions to enhance efficiency. 
ON THE JOB TRAINING IS PROVIDED FOR ALL OF THE ABOVE 
Position Type/Expected Hours of Work 
This job operates in a professional office environment, and all co-ops are expected to work three days per week in the office in Kilkenny. This role routinely uses standard office equipment. The role is Monday to Friday 8:30am – 5:00pm 
Preferred Qualifications and Experience 
Experience or coursework utilising project management techniques. 
Experience in coordinating teams and clients. 
Creative problem solving, attention to detail, coordination, teamwork, decision making. 
An interest in learning about cybersecurity technology and information security. 
Physical Demands 
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk and hear; use hands to finger, handle, or feel; and reach with hands and arms. The employee frequently is required to stand and walk. This is a largely sedentary role. 
Candidates with disabilities are encouraged to apply and email careers@sra.io with any questions. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable disabled individuals to perform the essential functions of this role. 
Other Duties 
Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice. 
Professional Development: 
At SRA, we are committed to the continuous professional growth of our employees. The successful candidate will not only have the chance to hone their project management abilities but will also be encouraged to expand their technical knowledge within the information security domain. 
We support our team members in building upon their existing skills and in acquiring new competencies through on-the-job experience and formal training initiatives. 
As part of our commitment to fostering talent, we offer access to a range of professional development opportunities, ensuring that our staff remain at the forefront of industry advancements and best practices. 
Work with Experts: Robust internal training program, plus Company-paid external training. SRA recognizes the value of professional development for employees. Therefore, we encourage our employees to pursue continuing education and role-specific training.
Mental Health Services: SRA has partnered with BetterHelp to provide SRA employees with free mental health support. BetterHelp connects individuals with licensed therapists for chat, video, and phone sessions.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Project Manager Co-op will ensure that all aspects of a project are organized and in conformance with timeline and deliverables requirements. They will facilitate collaboration among teams and clients, manage stakeholder expectations, and drive continuous improvement initiatives.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience or coursework in project management techniques and an interest in cybersecurity technology. Skills in creative problem solving, attention to detail, and teamwork are essential.

title: Direct Support Professional
description_text:
We are hiring flexible, reliable, and attentive applicants to provide assistive services to our clients. We seek full- and part-time team members for weekly staffing starting at as little as 1 hour shifts. Schedules are built around your availability and desired hours.
DSP's work one-on-one to support the needs of our clients in their homes through services and training such as:
companionship and interpersonal connection
meal preparation, such as help with cooking skills or grocery shopping
light housekeeping and laundry
transportation for doctor's visits and errands
other independent living skills
This position offers a fulfilling opportunity to provide Individualized home support (IHS), In-Home Family Support Services (IHFS), and Supportive Employment Services (SES) to individuals in the comfort of their own residences. You will be working with individuals who have varying disabilities and types of needs.
If you are someone with a flexible weekday schedule and a desire to connect with your community, this position is perfect for you! It allows you to go out and build meaningful relationships while making a difference.
We are looking for candidates who possess important qualities such as reliability, compassion, patience, engagement, and attention to detail. As a caregiver, you will need to effectively manage your time and be capable of working independently.
High school diploma or equivalent (Required)
Driver's license (Required)
Reliable and insured vehicle
Forward facing customer service experience preferred
Caregiving experience preferred
$18-20 per hour
Paid training and mileage reimbursement for client transport
Sick and Safe leave
Paid time off
PPE provided
ai_core_responsibilities: Direct Support Professionals provide assistive services to clients in their homes, including companionship, meal preparation, and transportation. They support individuals with varying disabilities and needs through individualized home support and training.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a high school diploma or equivalent and a valid driver's license with a reliable vehicle. Preferred qualifications include customer service and caregiving experience.
linkedin_org_locations: 1018 Main Street, Worcester, MA 01608, US
linkedin_org_description:
Quality Care Providers LLC is steadfastly committed to providing kind, complete and responsive assistance for clients who have health issues that affects their activities of daily living. We are a team of nurses, aides, community health workers and case managers who are united with the goal to improve lives for the disabled, elderly and ailing in our community.

Our mission is to maintain and improve the quality of life for our patients. Whether in the home or in the community, our employees are proficient and dedicated to provide the highest standard of care with a sense of warmth, kindness and individual pride. We partner with families, primary care physicians and community resource centers to ensure our patient’s services are well coordinated. Living by our mission is what makes us a top choice for an excellent Group Adult Foster Care program (GAFC).

We proudly serve residents all over Massachusetts. We are privately-owned and operated. This means we have greater control over the quality of care that is provided to clients. Consequently, clients can voice out their care preferences and such will be considered during the development of their care plan. With Quality Care Providers LLC, you receive care that is designed for you.

title: Nurse Practitioner or Physician Assistant - Tucson AZ
description_text:
Nurse Practitioner (NP) or Physician Assistant (PA) – Veteran Disability Assessments
We are seeking a dedicated Nurse Practitioner (NP) or Physician Assistant (PA) to join our team in performing medical assessments for U.S. Veterans as part of the VA's disability benefits process. This role involves conducting focused, in-person physical exams and reviewing medical records electronically to complete the required documentation for the VA.
Key Responsibilities:
· Conduct in-person disability assessments for U.S. Veterans.
· Review and analyze electronic medical records via a secure web portal.
· Accurately document exam findings and complete the required VA forms.
· Provide medical opinions based on your assessment findings.
Note: This role does not involve treatment or prescribing medication. Disability determinations are made by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), not by the provider.
Position Details:
· Schedule: Part-Time, 3 days/week (8:00 AM - 4:00 PM).
· Location: In-person role.
· Caseload: 3-5 Veterans per day.
· Equipment Provided: Computer and all necessary tools for documentation.
What We’re Looking For:
· A compassionate provider dedicated to supporting the Veteran community.
· Strong analytical skills to review medical records and assessment data.
· Excellent time management and ability to meet deadlines.
· Proficient in electronic documentation and web-based portals.
· A professional and empathetic demeanor when interacting with Veterans.
Requirements:
· Active, unrestricted Nurse Practitioner (NP) or Physician Assistant (PA) license.
· Comfortable using technology to review records and complete documentation.
Compensation & Benefits:
· Competitive salary (based on experience). 
· Health insurance & 401K benefits available.
· Malpractice Insurance covered by MRG.
· Training and ongoing support provided.
· Predictable schedule with no on-call duties.
Is This Role Right for You?
· Do you find it rewarding to serve and support Veterans?
· Are you looking for a structured role with no on-call shifts?
· Would you enjoy performing focused medical assessments without treatment responsibilities?
If so, apply today and become part of a meaningful mission to support those who served our country!
ai_core_responsibilities: The Nurse Practitioner or Physician Assistant will conduct in-person disability assessments for U.S. Veterans and review electronic medical records. They will document exam findings and provide medical opinions based on their assessments.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have an active, unrestricted Nurse Practitioner or Physician Assistant license. They should be comfortable using technology for reviewing records and completing documentation.

title: Program Analyst (DTRA)
description_text:
Location: Arlington, VA 
Category: Contingent
Schedule (FT/PT): FT
Travel Required: NA
Shift: 1st shift 
Remote Type: On-Site
Clearance required: Secret Clearance
Division: Aviation 
Who is ACT1 Federal? ACT1 Federal LLC is a 100% employee-owned company. We’ve served the Department of Defense (DoD) for nearly thirty years. Our core missions include weapon systems engineering, logistics, space domain expertise, global defense and security, business and financial management for security assistance and major defense articles, as well as military training and arctic security. Join us! 
***THIS POSITION IS CONTINGENT UPON CONTRACT AWARD***
As a Program Analyst, supporting a Department of Defense customer, you will be trusted to review and analyze data provide high-quality mission and decision-making support on budgetary, financial, and civilian pay issues while continuously improving the quality, efficiency, and focus of the customer’s financial services and operations. 
Responsibilities: 
Support the Chemical and Biological Defense Program (CBDP) for all aspects of accounting; financial statements; Internal Controls Enterprise Risk Management (ERM); Information System Controls including Complementary User Entity Controls (CUEC); reconciliation of budgetary and proprietary accounts; vendor pay operations; processing and maintenance of commitments and obligations; financial and accounting compliance; funds management and control; financial systems integration and coordination; and establishment of an effective Manager’s Internal Control (MIC) Program across CBDP components.
Assist the DoD customer to prepare, promulgate, and maintain a comprehensive directive for the Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) to identify and comply with requirements of Federal Managers’ Financial Integrity Act (FMFIA) OMB Circular A-123, including appendices as issued, and DoD guidance, as well as other pertinent statutes, standards, and directives on establishment and effective assessment of programs for internal control.
Provide support in distributing funds received from OSD by appropriation. Accomplish funds distribution through the DoD Program and Budget Allocation System (PBAS) and the Enterprise Fund Distribution (EFD) to designated executing agencies.
Provide support in managing and maintaining the Joint Integrated Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear Defense (CBRN) Analytics Platform (JICAP).
Ten (10) years of work experience in supporting the Department of Defense or similar agency.
Experience with DTRA (Defense Threat Reduction Agency) is strongly preferred, especially with Chemical and Biological Defense Program.
Experience with Quantitative/Qualitative Analysis and Enterprise Risk Management (ERM).
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite products (Word, Excel, Access, Project, SharePoint, and PowerPoint) and Visio.
Active SECRET Clearance is required.
· Medical/Dental/Vision Insurance
· ACT1 Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP)
· Company Paid Life and AD&D Insurance
· Company Paid Short-Term Disability 
· Voluntary Long-Term Disability
· Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA)
· Health Savings Account (HSA)
· 401K with employer match
· Paid Time Off 
· Paid Holidays
· Parental Leave
· Military Leave 
· Education, Training & Professional Development 
· Voluntary Accidental Injury/Critical Illness/Hospital Care 
· Voluntary Pet Insurance, Legal Resources, and Identity Protection
https://act1federal.com/careers/
Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits of flexibility and collaboration, whether that happens in person or remotely.
If this position is listed as remote or hybrid, you’ll periodically work from a ACT1 Federal or client site facility. 
If this position is listed as onsite, you’ll work with colleagues and clients in person, as needed for the specific role.
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local or international law.

title: Creative Strategist
description_text:
About Us
NALA is building Payments for the Next Billion. Faster, smarter, and fairer transfers for everyone. Since 2022, we've grown our business 120x, grown the team from 9 to 150+, raised $50M+ from top-tier investors, and were named to the Forbes Fintech 50 in 2025.
We operate two core products:
NALA, our consumer app makes cross-border payments cheaper, faster and more reliable for the global diaspora. Allowing users to send money from the UK, US and EU to Africa and Asia.
Rafiki, our B2B payments infrastructure, is powering global payments.
Our team includes alumni from Wise, Stripe, Monzo, Revolut, and CashApp — operators who’ve scaled world-class products. We act with urgency, think deeply, and put our customers first always.
At NALA, this isn’t just a job. It’s ownership, impact, and the chance to change global payments forever.
Join us in building Payments for the Next Billion
🙌 Your Mission 
Your mission is to define and execute NALA’s creative strategy, turning our mission, products, and growth priorities into compelling narratives that build brand love and drive measurable business impact.
The Role Purpose
The Creative Strategist serves as a driver for NALA’s brand identity, bridging the gap between high-level strategy and world-class execution. You will be the guardian of the NALA Gold Standard, responsible for producing and directing beautiful content that resonates emotionally with our customers. By utilising a Test and Learn approach, you will ensure our storytelling remains agile, impactful, and always puts the Customer First.
🎯 Your Responsibilities in this Role 
Set the Creative Standard: Own NALA’s end-to-end creative strategy across brand, product, growth and campaigns, ensuring a consistent and distinctive voice. 
Master the Brief: Translate complex brand strategies into actionable, punchy briefs for influencers and agencies.
Drive the Narrative: Partner directly with the CEO and leadership team to translate company strategy into clear creative direction and storytelling. 
Multi-channel campaigns: Develop and launch high-impact campaigns that drive brand awareness, user growth, and engagement across key markets. 
Be the brand guardian: Set the creative bar across copy, visuals, and concepts, reviewing and improving work to world-class standards. 
Collaborate: Work closely with Growth, Product, and Marketing teams to ensure creativity directly supports commercial outcomes. 
Iterate with Speed: Launch creative "tests’ to gather feedback and make rapid adjustments—because at NALA, Speed Wins.
Direct PR & Brand Strategy: Manage our public narrative and blog strategy, ensuring we communicate with a friendly, approachable, and honest tone.
Who You Are (EKBS Specification) 🧪 Experience
You have a proven track record of setting a Gold Standard in a specialised creative discipline.
You have experience moving projects through the 0-1 MVP phase into scaled, high-utility execution.
You have successfully led agencies or influencers as a DRI (Directly Responsible Individual).
Proven experience leading creative strategy at a high-growth tech, fintech, or consumer brand. 
A strong portfolio demonstrating strategic thinking, storytelling, and execution across multiple channels. 
Experience working closely with senior leadership and influencing company-level decisions through creative work. 
🧠 Knowledge
You deeply Understand Why—you don’t just create; you dig for the root cause of customer problems.
You have a firm grasp of the competitive landscape and how to differentiate NALA’s brand and positioning across all markets.
You understand that the Experience is as important as the functionality.
Ability to connect brand creativity to measurable outcomes such as growth, engagement, or conversion. 
✍️ Behaviours 
Sense of Urgency: You attack every morning with hunger and a desire to get your full to-do list done.
Solutions, not problems: You are proactive in addressing issues or problems with actionable solutions, and don’t wait to be told what needs to be done.
Point of View: You have the courage to ask "Why not?" and push boundaries when a decision isn't ambitious enough.
⚽ Skills
Creative Specialism: Mastery in at least one core creative discipline—such as high-impact copywriting, professional videography, or short-form video production—to lead by example and set the "Gold Standard".
Briefing & Creative Direction: Expert ability to translate brand strategy into actionable briefs for influencers and agencies, ensuring the "NALA tone of voice" is consistently delivered.
Visual & Narrative Storytelling: Skilled in combining "art and science" to create beautiful product experiences that resonate emotionally with customers.
Rapid Iteration & Prototyping: Ability to launch creative "MVPs" to gather immediate customer feedback and make micro-adjustments to improve utility.
✅ Success in the role looks like 
3-Month Metrics 
Clear, creative strategy defined and aligned with CEO and leadership team. 
Improved consistency and quality across NALA’s brand, campaigns, and messaging. 
6-Month Metrics 
One or more major campaigns launched that drive measurable uplift in brand awareness or user growth. 
Creative outputs are recognised internally as a key driver of NALA’s growth and market positioning. 
⭐️ Benefits 
27 Days Off Plus UK Bank Holidays: Take the time to decompress. Working at a startup is hard! 
Birthday Leave: Celebrate your special day with a bonus day off to take off in that month. 
Enhanced Parental Leave: We offer 16 weeks of full pay for the primary caregiver and 4 weeks of full pay for the secondary caregiver (after a 6-month probationary period). 
Enhanced Pension: Salary sacrifice pension scheme via Penfold giving you flexibility and control on how you save for your future. 
Global Workspace: Get access to WeWork locations worldwide. 
Learning Budget: Fuel your growth with $1000 annually for learning and development. 
Sarabi: Themed snacks and Friday lunch focused on building great working relationships with the team. 
Monthly Socials: Join fun social events every month for great times. 
Free Coffee: Enjoy barista-style coffee at your fingertips.
ai_benefits: 27 Days Off Plus UK Bank Holidays

They will collaborate with leadership to translate company strategy into compelling narratives and manage multi-channel campaigns.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a proven track record in creative strategy within high-growth tech or fintech environments, along with experience leading agencies. A strong portfolio demonstrating strategic thinking and storytelling across multiple channels is essential.

In just 28 months since re-launch in 2022, NALA grew from $0 to $24M in revenue, turned profitable once, and built a moat through an integrated B2B (rafiki.com) infrastructure powering stablecoin payments between the US, UK, EU, Africa and Asia.

Raised over $50m - our investors include Accel, Acrew, Y Combinator, Bessemer, and DST Global Partners, alongside awesome founders Alex (Deel), Tarek (Kalshi), Jonas (Monzo), Ryan (Chime), Laura (Alloy) and Vlad (Robinhood).

title: Product Manager, Insights (AI/ML)
description_text:
Irth Solutions is building Insights—a cross‑product intelligence layer that powers Damage Prevention, Asset Integrity, Land Management, and Stakeholder Engagement with predictive and probabilistic risk analytics for critical network infrastructure.
Insights blends generative AI, machine learning, vision modeling, risk data, workflow models, industry trends, and customer‑specific datasets to identify and quantify opportunities and risk with exceptional accuracy. Because Insights integrates directly with 811 call centers, contract locators, energy companies, utilities, and integrity / engagement / land teams, its models are trained on the industry’s most operationally relevant data.
Irth delivers comprehensive risk scoring that combines predictive AI/ML with actuarial analysis—turning data into deeper, more actionable intelligence.
Role summary
As Product Manager, Insights (AI/ML), you will own the horizontal roadmap and make Insights a seamlessly integrated intelligence layer across Irth’s product portfolio. You’ll bridge data science, platform engineering, and real‑world customer workflows to ship trustworthy models and Insight‑powered features that drive measurable outcomes.
What you’ll do
Product strategy & roadmap
Own the cross‑portfolio Insights roadmap; set vision, goals, and success metrics in partnership with vertical PMs.
Evaluate and prioritize AI/ML capabilities and data science investments aligned to company strategy, ARR goals, and technical constraints.
Translate market and customer needs into clear epics, features, and user stories; manage dependencies across teams.
AI/ML product leadership
Define requirements for predictive models, image analysis, and probabilistic scoring; partner with data science on training needs, calibration, and accuracy improvements.
Guide model performance evaluation and risk calibration; understand the AI/ML lifecycle from training through monitoring. 
Champion transparency and operational confidence in AI‑driven decisions with customers and stakeholders. 
Execution & delivery
Partner with UX and engineering to ship intuitive, Insight‑powered workflows that fit into customer operations. 
Support release readiness, documentation, and enablement for Insights features; function as the escalation point for complex issues and serve as domain SME through build, launch, and adoption. 
Customer & market
Engage customers and industry experts for discovery, feedback, and validation; conduct competitive research to improve experience and win share. 
Collaborate with product marketing, sales, and enablement on go‑to‑market for Insight‑powered capabilities. 
How we’ll measure success (first 12 months)
Adoption: % of customers and users engaging with Insights‑powered features across product lines.
Model impact: Measurable improvements in precision/recall, calibration, lift, and reduction in false positives/negatives.
Commercial outcomes: Attach rate for Insight‑powered modules and ARR influenced by Insights capabilities.
Operational confidence: Reduction in escalations related to risk scoring; improved user satisfaction (CSAT/NPS) for Insight‑powered workflows.
Delivery: On‑time release of prioritized roadmap items with clear enablement and GTM readiness.
How you’ll work
Cross‑functional leadership: Influence without authority across product, engineering, data science, UX, marketing, sales, and engagement / delivery. 
Customer‑back approach: Balance AI/ML innovation with field‑tested operational reality; ensure features are intuitive and trustworthy in high‑stakes environments. 
What you’ll bring (Requirements)
5+ years of product management or technical product leadership in AI/ML, LLMs, data platforms, analytics, or workflow‑heavy enterprise software. 
Strong technical fluency with AI/ML concepts, data modeling, APIs, system architecture, and platform dependencies; proven track record partnering with data science across the ML lifecycle. 
Demonstrated ability to turn complex, data‑driven customer problems into shipped features with measurable impact. 
Excellent written and verbal communication; able to simplify technical AI/ML topics for business users. 
Bachelor’s in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Statistics—or equivalent experience (advanced degree a plus). Relevant Agile/Scrum or PM certifications are a bonus. 
Familiarity with the Microsoft stack (e.g., Teams, Azure DevOps/Jira) and agile software development practices. 
Domain experience (Preferred)
Background in Damage Prevention/One Call, utilities, energy, asset integrity, and/or land management. 
Working knowledge of standards such as ASME B31, PHMSA 49 CFR 192/195, CSA, and CGA/CCGA—and how they influence compliance workflows and risk models. 
Join a dynamic, growing company that is well respected in its industry. 
Competitive salary
Health plans options including medical, dental, & vision 
401k (US), RSP (Canada) + company match 
Flexible PTO policy plus company-paid holidays 
Benefits options such as health insurance, life insurance, discounts and perks programs
Generous “work from home” stipend to get you started
Team events including monthly lunches for everyone, volunteer outings, and quarterly gatherings
Hybrid employees have access to snacks, beverages and coffee at our Columbus office
ai_core_responsibilities: As Product Manager, you will own the Insights roadmap and integrate it across Irth’s product portfolio. You will bridge data science and engineering to deliver AI-powered features that drive measurable outcomes.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5+ years of experience in product management or technical leadership in AI/ML and related fields. A strong technical background and the ability to translate complex problems into actionable features are essential.

linkedin_org_description: Companies in the energy, gas utility, electric utility, municipality, contract locator, telecom and media industries have trusted Irth to enhance the resilience of their critical network infrastructure with our market-leading SaaS cloud-based technology for managing 811 tickets, land rights, and risk. We’ve accumulated best practices, data, and experience over nearly three decades serving the needs of this market, and we continue to build additional solutions, offerings, and apps to help our customers maintain and manage risks on their network infrastructure. By taking advantage of the best technology available, we help our customers address the expanding threats they face today.

title: Business Development Representative
description_text:
About Flinks 🚀
Flinks is the embedded finance platform that brings together connectivity, intelligence, and payments — giving businesses the infrastructure they need to build and deliver seamless financial experiences at scale.
As a leader in Open Finance in Canada, we’ve grown since 2016 into one of North America’s most trusted platforms for financial data access, enrichment, and money movement. We work with innovators across many industries, including lending, fintech, banking, insurance, and wealth management.
Today, our platform connects to 15,000+ financial institutions across North America and powers over 1M monthly connections. We also give our customers unprecedented visibility into 4,500+ real-time financial insights to support smarter decisioning. Companies rely on Flinks to streamline onboarding, verify income, assess credit risk, and power faster payment experiences.
We’re on a mission to drive financial innovation and help businesses build financial experiences that feel effortless, connected, and customer-first. That’s where you come in.

About the Team 💰
Have you ever wanted to explore a career in sales, but weren’t sure where to start? If so, then the Flinks Sales Team could be the perfect place for you. We’re looking for several Business Development Representatives who will contribute to the continued growth of our North American operations by creating top of the funnel opportunities for our Sales Executives.

What You'll Be Doing 🔥
Clearly communicate the value of our software platform by showcasing its new and existing capabilities, addressing customer needs, and sparking interest.
Build and maintain a robust pipeline of qualified leads using a strategic, multi-channel approach, including cold calls, email campaigns, LinkedIn outreach, and more.
Qualify inbound leads generated through marketing efforts, while proactively sourcing and nurturing outbound leads, converting them into high-potential opportunities for Sales Specialists.
Use advanced tools and creative tactics to identify and engage companies and contacts that align with the ideal customer profile.
Establish strong connections with prospective clients through daily research, personalized communications, and consistent outreach efforts via email, calls, and social media.
Meet and surpass daily activity metrics such as cold calls, emails, and LinkedIn messages to ensure a healthy sales pipeline.
Stay organized by consistently updating and maintaining CRM records in HubSpot, adhering to established processes and best practices.
Partner closely with Marketing and Sales Specialists to align strategies, share insights, and deepen product knowledge for more effective prospecting.
Who You Are 💪
1-2 years of experience in a B2B sales role, preferably in SaaS.
Eager to learn and passionate about pursuing a career in tech sales.
Proven ability to meet or exceed sales targets and KPIs in previous roles.
Exceptional communication, active listening, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage in consultative selling and build productive relationships with diverse groups.
Self-starter mindset who isn’t afraid to overcome objections and challenges.
Creative strategist with a propensity for creating compelling messaging and content.
Experience using CRMs like HubSpot or Salesforce, with an ability to quickly adapt to new sales tools and technologies. 
Nice to Haves
Experience in the fintech industry. 
Knowledge of financial services, open banking, and fintech companies in Canada and the United States.

The Interview Process 🏗 
Head of People Ops
Hiring Manager
Sales Role Play Panel Interview
Compensation Range
For experience and qualified hires, the base compensation range is $52,000 to $78,000 CAD annually. As a part of the total rewards package for this role, Flinks offers BDRs a competitive commission structure in addition to Health & Dental coverage as of Day 1, Flexible Paid Time Off (PTO), a hybrid work environment from our offices in the heart of the financial district at King & Yonge, and much more.
We are committed to providing accommodations for persons with disabilities. If you require accommodation, we will work with you to meet your needs.
Please note that Flinks uses artificial intelligence (AI) during the recruitment process to assist in screening, assessing, or selecting applicants.

linkedin_org_description:
Flinks is the embedded finance platform that brings together connectivity, intelligence, and payments — giving businesses the infrastructure they need to build and deliver seamless financial experiences at scale.

As a leader in Open Finance in Canada, we’ve grown since 2016 into one of North America’s most trusted platforms for financial data access, enrichment, and money movement. We work with innovators across many industries, including lending, fintech, banking, insurance, and wealth management.

Today, our platform connects to 15,000+ financial institutions across North America and powers over 1M monthly connections. We’ve securely processed 100,000+ financial documents to date. We also give our customers unprecedented visibility into 4,500+ real-time financial insights to support smarter decisioning. Companies rely on Flinks to streamline onboarding, verify income, assess credit risk, and power faster payment experiences.
We’re on a mission to drive financial innovation and help businesses build financial experiences that feel effortless, connected, and customer-first. That’s where you come in.

Learn more at www.flinks.com

title: Product Owner
description_text:
With Intelligence is now a part of S&P Global, creating one of the most comprehensive data offerings for alternatives and private markets participants. We are now part of a larger organisation with more than 35,000 staff worldwide, so we're able to understand nuances while having a broad perspective. From helping our customers assess new investments across the capital and commodities markets to guiding them through the energy expansion, acceleration of artificial intelligence, and evolution of public and private markets, we enable the world’s leading organisations to unlock opportunities, solve challenges, and plan for tomorrow – today. We’re Advancing Essential Intelligence.
You will own and be accountable for the delivery of With Intelligence’s data automation team, ensuring consistent execution, clear alignment and effective performance across the team.
Work closely with stakeholders across Product Strategy, Platform and Data Operations acting as the single source of truth for priorities and work intake for the team.
Balance and prioritise competing business needs to ensure focus on the highest-value outcomes.
Provide clear, timely, and accurate visibility into the status of initiatives, risks, and delivery timelines.
Maintain a deep understanding of the team’s tooling and systems, using this knowledge to shape scope, priorities, and delivery decisions. 
Writes clear, unambiguous requirements for automation workflows (acceptance criteria, edge cases, human-in-the-loop needs, escalation paths)
Understands process flows deeply enough to challenge assumptions and validate scope
Defines automation success metrics (accuracy, throughput, time saved, etc..)
Maintains and actively uses technical debt registers, backlog hygiene and dependency tracking to assess true team status
Identifies gaps and can reliably reproduce issues to support effective resolution before delivery risk emerges (e.g. missing logic, unmet dependencies, etc)
Executes robust UAT to validate ACs end-to-end, systematically identifying defects and minimising the introduction of regressions or downstream bugs.
Communicates clearly, consistently, and proactively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Accountability & Documentation
Owns backlog: prioritisation, clarity, readiness, alignment to outcomes.
Produces and maintains high-quality documentation (problem statements, data definitions, assumptions, decisions, constraints).
Ensures decisions are recorded and traceable, reducing rework and ambiguity
Takes responsibility for missed expectations and course-corrects decisively.
Automation & AI Product knowledge
Understands automation system design well enough to guide technical discussions and validate implementation approaches
Comfortable working with data to inform decisions (basic SQL, Tableau/Looker, operational metrics)
Brings an automation-first, creative mindset by proactively identifying manual or repetitive data processes and reimagines them as scalable automated workflows using AI/LLMs, data science, and engineering best practices.
Demonstrates strong understanding of modern automation practices, including:
AI-assisted workflow design
LLM prompt + evaluation patterns
Human-in-the-loop review systems
Workflow orchestration and monitoring
Automation quality controls and fallbacks
Experience working alongside engineering and data science teams delivering production automation systems
24 days annual leave rising to 29 days
Enhanced parental leave
Medicash (Health Cash Plans)
Wellness Days
Flexible Fridays (Opportunity to finish early)
Birthday day off
Employee assistance program
Travel loan scheme
Charity days
Breakfast provided
Social Events throughout the year
Hybrid Working
Our Company:
With Intelligence is based at One London Wall, London EC2Y 5EA. We offer amazing benefits, free breakfast daily and drinks provided all day, every day. We actively encourage social networks that oversee activities from sports, book reading to rock climbing, that you are free to join.
As part of our company, you will enjoy the benefits of an open plan office and working with a social and energetic team. With Intelligence provides exclusive editorial, research, data and events for senior executives within the asset management industry. These include hedge funds, private credit, private equity, real estate and traditional asset management, and our editorial brands are seen as market leaders in providing asset manager sales and IR execs with the actionable information they require to help them raise and retain assets. To maintain and grow our position in the market we need to continue to hire highly motivated, thoughtful and to ensure our subscribers are getting the exclusive intelligence they need first, and most comprehensively, through our range of services. If you are interested so far in what you have read, please apply, we look forward to hearing from you.
We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. Our policy is not to discriminate against any applicant or employee based on actual or perceived race, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy), marital status, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, mental or physical disability, religion, creed, colour, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression (including transgender status), veteran status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Area Coach
description_text:
About the company
Our client is the European subsidiary of a leading KFC franchisee that operates one of the fastest-growing international-format restaurant chains.
Our client's goal is to scale KFC’s presence in Germany, open modern restaurants, and build a strong operational team working in accordance with international QSR standards.
Shape the future of a dynamic restaurant brand from the ground up! Our client is seeking a visionary Area Coach to lead their expansion across Thuringia, Saxony, and Bavaria—starting with the launch of 1–2 new restaurants in 2026 and building a robust regional network. This is a rare opportunity to design operational excellence from scratch: define processes, implement digital tools, cultivate franchise partnerships, and craft the guest experience without legacy constraints. Enjoy entrepreneurial freedom with corporate backing, extensive travel across three diverse German states, and the satisfaction of building a thriving business where your strategic decisions directly shape growth and culture from day one.
The current work arrangement is remote/travel-based.
The future office will be located in one of the cities in Thuringia, Saxony, or Bavaria.
Your Responsibilities
Launch of the First Restaurants:
Support the opening of 1–2 KFC restaurants in 2026, covering the full cycle from site handover to the first day of operations
Ensure full compliance with KFC operational standards during the launch phase
Finance & P&L
Responsibility for restaurant P&L during the opening phase
Control operational costs and achieve planned profitability targets
Participate in the preparation of opening budgets and budgets for the first months of operation
Optimize staffing levels and shift schedules in compliance with German labour law
Team
Recruit and train the initial restaurant teams (cashiers, cooks, cleaning staff)
Establish service, hygiene, and safety standards
Provide coaching and mentorship for future Restaurant Managers
External Communication
Liaise with the KFC brand owner regarding standards and audits
Work with trade unions, health and safety authorities, and local government bodies
Prepare restaurants for inspections by regulatory and supervisory authorities
Operational Support
Conduct weekly on-site work in restaurants to ensure quality control and team support
Analyse key performance indicators, including service speed, guest satisfaction, and compliance with standards
Implement digital management tools in accordance with GDPR requirements
Contractors & Opening Readiness
Coordinate with contractors (construction, equipment, cleaning, service providers)
Monitor restaurant readiness for opening and formally accept key works and systems
Fluent German (C1/C2) — mandatory for working with trade unions and public authorities
At least 2 years of experience in the restaurant or retail sector (e.g. Restaurant Manager, Supervisor, Trainer)
Understanding of German labour law is an advantage
Willingness to travel frequently within the region (30–50% of working time)
Higher education is preferred but not mandatory — hands-on experience is highly valued
What Our Client Offers
Official employment under German labour law with a full social benefits package
A unique professional opportunity: you are not “a manager in a system” — you are building the system from the ground up
Career growth opportunities: following a successful launch, regional network expansion and internal career progression
Support: you will not be alone — our client's expert team brings 15+ years of experience working with the KFC brand
ai_core_responsibilities: The Area Coach will support the opening of 1–2 KFC restaurants in 2026, ensuring compliance with operational standards and managing P&L responsibilities. They will also recruit and train restaurant teams, establish service standards, and conduct operational support.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least 2 years of experience in the restaurant or retail sector and fluent German (C1/C2) for communication with trade unions and authorities. Understanding of German labour law is advantageous, and a willingness to travel frequently within the region is required.

title: 1st & 2nd Shift Direct Care
description_text:
About MHA
MHA – Mental Health Association is a nonprofit organization dedicated to helping people live their best life. We provide a wide range of services across Western Massachusetts, including outpatient therapy, substance use recovery, supports for individuals with developmental disabilities or brain injuries, and residential and housing programs. MHA serves individuals impacted by mental illness, substance use, developmental disabilities, brain injuries, and homelessness. Our mission is rooted in inclusion, dignity, and empowering people to lead fulfilling, independent lives.
About Integration & Community Living (ICL)
MHA’s Integration & Community Living (ICL) program supports individuals with developmental disabilities and brain injuries in living independently and meaningfully in the community. In partnership with the Department of Developmental Services (DDS), ICL provides person-centered residential and outreach services throughout Western Massachusetts.
With over 60 years of experience, MHA has been a leader in helping individuals transition from institutional settings into homes where they can grow, make choices, and fully participate in community life. Services range from supported and shared living to emergency respite and privately funded care, all tailored to promote independence, dignity, and connection.
_______________________________________________________________________________________________
Position Summary
As a Residential Support Specialist, you will provide a supportive and safe environment in community residential settings within MHA’s ICL (Integrated Community Living) department. This role involves traveling to different program locations based on staffing needs, offering flexibility and consistent support across the department. You will help foster growth, independence, and community engagement for individuals who have experienced a brain injury resulting in physical limitations, mental health, and/or cognitive challenges. 
Pay Rate: $20 an hour
Open Shift: Tuesday through Thursday & Saturday 9am-3pm, Friday 2pm-8pm (30h)
Key Responsibilities
Provide respectful, person-centered support to participants in daily living activities, personal care, and skill development.
Promote community integration by facilitating access to transportation, social activities, and external resources.
Support participants' health needs, including medication administration (per MAP standards), appointment scheduling, and monitoring overall wellbeing.
Assist with financial skills and money management, maintaining accurate documentation of all expenditures.
Respond to emergencies and crisis situations appropriately, following agency protocols and communicating with supervisors.
Advocate for participants and help them build self-advocacy skills; serve as a liaison with families, providers, and community supports.
Contribute to the development and implementation of Individual Service Plans (ISPs), documenting progress and participation.
Maintain accurate and timely documentation, including daily logs, incident reports, and health records.
Promote a safe environment by following all safety procedures, assisting with emergency drills, and identifying potential hazards.
Collaborate effectively with team members, attend training, maintain certifications (MAP, CPR, First Aid), and actively participate in meetings and supervision.
Equal Opportunity Statement
The Mental Health Association is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
Valid driver’s license, sufficient automobile insurance, an acceptable driving record, and access to a reliable vehicle during working hours.
Strong communication, organizational, and computer skills.
Adaptability to participants' changing needs.
Ability to pass and maintain certifications in medication administration (MAP), CPR, and First Aid.
Must be at least 18 years old.
High school diploma or GED required; college degree in human services or a related field preferred.
Minimum of six months of relevant experience; experience with individuals with brain injuries, mental health challenges, or developmental disabilities is preferred.
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Residential Support Specialist, you will provide a supportive and safe environment in community residential settings. You will help foster growth, independence, and community engagement for individuals who have experienced a brain injury resulting in physical limitations, mental health, and/or cognitive challenges.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be at least 18 years old and have a high school diploma or GED, with a preference for a college degree in human services or a related field. A minimum of six months of relevant experience, particularly with individuals with brain injuries or developmental disabilities, is preferred.

linkedin_org_description: MHA is committed to building an agency of excellence for its clients, staff and community. We will continue to strive to be the premier provider of residential and support services, noted for innovation, commitment and dedication. We conceptualize a community where all individuals have the skills and opportunities to achieve their aspirations. Our aim is to create an atmosphere where dignity, respect and equality are commonplace, where all individuals can reach their maximum potential. Through flexibility, continuous self-examination and knowledge of current and effective practices, MHA will remain on the cutting edge of service delivery. For those areas of an individual’s life that can be improved, MHA will seek to do so. For those areas in which answers are yet to come, MHA will bring hope. Respect, Integrity, Compassion!

title: Bingo Trivia and Karaoke Host
description_text:
Music Bingo, Trivia, and Karaoke Host
Applicants must complete ALL screener questions to be considered for this position.
Are you outgoing, energetic, and reliable? Are you looking for a great part-time job that allows you to interact with people, and is something that you WANT to do? Then you should apply to work for Top Shelf Trivia!
We conduct hundreds of shows each week in cities across the country, and we are looking for qualified individuals to join our growing team.
YOU NEED TO HAVE:
An engaging personality
A laptop or tablet
Reliable transportation
A consistent night and time available each week
Attention to detail and a track record of dependability
No prior experience is required.
Responsibilities and Details
Host live entertainment
Engage with customers
Entertain the crowd
Each show is 2 hours, and generally begins between 6PM and 9PM. We are looking for responsible, self-motivated, loyal, and outgoing people who are driven to ONLY provide "Top Shelf" level entertainment and performances!
If this sounds like the right fit for you, APPLY NOW!
An engaging personality
A laptop or tablet
Reliable transportation
A consistent night and time available each week
Attention to detail and a track record of dependability
The starting pay is $20/hour with the opportunity for an increase with experience.

We offer the highest quality Trivia, Music Bingo, and Karaoke shows in the industry. We pride ourselves on employing the most energetic, personable, and talented entertainers - which is why we believe our shows to be truly unparalleled! 

Whether you want to rock out and try your and hand at naming the top hit songs throughout the decades, or you'd like to gather your friends for a round of brain-busting trivia - Top Shelf shows are where it's at! Join the party now for a chance to win some amazing prizes, while playing the most fun live games! 

Top Shelf Trivia was founded in NC and has been rapidly expanding across the east coast since 2016, in it's quest to provide the most excellent Trivia and Music Bingo shows our country has to offer!

title: Water Delivery Technician
description_text:
We're excited to welcome a motivated Water Delivery Technician to join our team at Culligan in our Service Centre in Wolverhampton. 
In this role, you'll have the opportunity to make a real difference by providing excellent service to our valued customers whilst ensuring our bottles are delivered on time, bottled water coolers are installed & sanitized correctly and collecting equipment & bottles from customer sites.
This is a great opportunity to join our field operations team and start a career at Culligan. We offer ongoing training and development, providing opportunities for progression with structured training programmes. 
Support our mission to give people instant access to cleaner, safer, better-tasting water through our unparalleled expertise in sustainability, science, and service.
Hours of Work: Monday-Friday 7:00am-5:00pm
What this role involves: 
Complete daily vehicle checks.
Multi drop of (20kg) water containers at a variety of customer locations.
Sanitize and exchange Bottles Water Coolers 
Engage with customers to grasp their needs whilst delivering outstanding customer service
Playing a vital role in ensuring the smooth running of our Service Centre
Documenting all sanitization & delivery activities via handheld device 
Full and valid driving license with a maximum of 6 points (none of which are for dangerous or careless driving)
Minimum of 2 years' driving experience
Excellent communication skills and the ability to build rapport with customers. 
Ability to be able to follow timed delivery schedules
Monday - Friday Days working pattern 
23 days' holiday + Bank Holidays - increasing with long service
Enhanced Maternity, Paternity, Shared Parental and Grandparent leave 
Company Pension scheme 
Company Sick Pay 
Shopping discounts with Perks at Work 
24 hours access to Employee Assistance Programme 
Volunteering Day 
Employee support network and mental health assistance
Opportunity for Career Progression
