title: Credit Control Assistant
description_text:
Are you interested in working with a leading education technology player, the global leader in the assessment and certification of professional skills industry with presence in more than 200 countries worldwide? If so, this is the chance to apply now! 📥
PeopleCert is currently seeking a Credit Control Assistant to join our dynamic Finance team. In this role, you will play a key part in managing the company’s credit control operations, ensuring timely collection of receivables, and maintaining healthy customer relationships. You will collaborate closely with internal departments and external partners to support effective cash flow management and contribute to the company’s financial stability and growth.

As a Credit Control Assistant, your tasks will include the following: 
Follow and enhance internal credit control processes and company credit policy, ensuring compliance and best practices across all transactions. 
Monitor and review aged debt reports regularly, identifying opportunities to maximize cash collection and minimize overdue balances. 
Ensure timely debt payments while maintaining positive, long-term relationships with customers—balancing firmness with professionalism. 
Perform accurate account reconciliations, keeping customer ledgers up to date and resolving discrepancies efficiently. 
Manage and maintain the corporate credit insurance policy to safeguard company receivables and reduce financial exposure. 
Negotiate repayment plans with clients in a proactive and solution-oriented manner. 
Assess new credit applications, review customer creditworthiness, and provide recommendations to support sound financial decisions. 
Build and nurture strong relationships with internal teams and external clients, acting as a trusted partner in all credit-related matters.
What we look for:
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or Business (or a related discipline). 
1-2 years’ experience in Credit Control or a similar finance role—experience in a multinational or fast-paced corporate environment will be considered a strong advantage. 
Excellent command of English (C2 level)—a LanguageCert C2 LTE or C2 IESOL certification will be considered a plus. 
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, with the ability to create pivot tables, use complex formulas, and perform data analysis to support decision-making. 
Strong computer literacy, with ECDL Advanced level certification highly desirable. 
Exceptional communication and customer service skills, with a confident, professional approach to internal and external stakeholders. 
Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities, able to identify discrepancies and propose effective solutions. 
Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines, while maintaining high accuracy and attention to detail.
What we offer:
Competitive remuneration package 💲
Work in an international, dynamic and fun atmosphere 💯
Two free vouchers for all certifications from PeopleCert's Portfolio per year for all employees 🎓
Huge learning experience in using best practices and global environment 📈
Constant personal and professional development 🥇
100% Virtual Hiring Process 💻

If you want to become a member of our international, dynamic and agile team that creates world leading software products, then we should certainly like to hear from you!

About PeopleCert
PeopleCert is a global leader in assessment and certification of professional skills, partnering with multi-national organizations and government bodies for the development & delivery of standardized exams. Delivering exams across 200 countries and in 25 languages over its state-of-the-art assessment technology, PeopleCert enables professionals to boost their careers and realize their life ambitions.
Quality, Innovation, Passion, Integrity, Clarity, Velocity are the core values which guide everything we do.
Our offices in UK, Greece, and Cyprus boast a culture of diversity, where everyone is different, yet everyone fits in. All of us at PeopleCert are committed to the reflection of the diversity and inclusion of our customers and the communities in which we do business.

Working on Home Office (HO) Secure English Language Tests (SELTs)
Any person who is engaged by PeopleCert to work on the SELT service must undergo a Background Check (the results of which must be acceptable to PeopleCert and the HO) prior to commencing their SELT duties. All SELT personnel will be required to complete a declaration (provided by PeopleCert) where the existence of any criminal record and/or bankruptcy must be declared.
If working on the SELT service in the UK, background checks will include:
A basic or enhanced Disclosure Barring Service (DBS) check
Right to Work in the UK check (including nationality, identity and place of residence)
HO security check (Baseline Personnel Security Standard (BPSS) or Counter Terrorist Check (CTC)
Financial background check
Employment reference check.
If working on the SELT service anywhere in the world (outside of the UK) personnel will undergo background checks that are equivalent to those stated for the UK.
In addition, if personnel are required to speak to SELT candidates they must be appropriately skilled in English language and, where SELT services are provided anywhere in the world (outside of the UK), the official language of the relevant country.
All applications will be treated with strict confidentiality.

This role involves monitoring aged debt reports, performing account reconciliations, and negotiating repayment plans with clients.

title: Менеджер з обслуговування преміум клієнтів дистанційними каналами
description_text:
Райффайзен Банк — найбільший український банк з іноземним капіталом. Більше 30 років ми створюємо та вибудовуємо банківську систему нашої держави.
У Райфі працює понад 5 500 співробітників, серед них одна із найбільших продуктових ІТ-команд, що налічує понад 800 фахівців. Щодня пліч-о-пліч ми працюємо, щоб більш ніж 2,7 мільйона наших клієнтів могли отримати якісне обслуговування, користуватися продуктами і сервісами банку, розвивати бізнес, адже ми #Разом_з_Україною. 
Твої майбутні обов’язки: 
Забезпечення продажу продуктів та сервісу Преміум клієнтам Банку за допомогою телефонних дзвінків та інших дистанційних каналів.
Забезпечення продажу простих продуктів та сервісу суб‘єктів підприємницької діяльності.
Забезпечення підтримки відносин з існуючими Преміум клієнтами та залучення на обслуговування нових Преміум клієнтів роздрібного бізнесу (приватні особи та бізнес банкінгу) з метою забезпечення виконання планових показників.
Відображення операцій в операційному дні Банку та їх контроль.
Побудова та управління клієнтським портфелем сегменту Преміум.
Забезпечення якості обслуговування клієнтів Банку для забезпечення конкурентних переваг Банку, підтримання та зміцнення позитивного іміджу Банку та Групи Raiffeisen Bank International.
Виконання вимог у сфері запобігання та протидії легалізації (відмиванню) доходів, одержаних злочинним шляхом, фінансуванню тероризму та фінансуванню розповсюдження зброї масового знищення.
Дотримання та виконання вимог Політики комплаєнсу в Банку та Групі та Політики запобігання та управління конфліктами інтересів в Банку та Групі.
Недопущення ситуацій, що становлять конфлікт інтересів.
Твій досвід та навички:
Досвід роботи з преміум клієнтами від 1 року
Досвід роботи з продажами продуктів
Високий рівень комунікації

Пропонуємо те, що має значення саме для тебе: 
Конкурентна заробітна плата: гарантуємо стабільний дохід та річні бонуси за твій особистий внесок. Додатково, у нас діє реферальна програма винагороди за залучення нових колег до Райффайзен Банку. 
Соціальний пакет: офіційне працевлаштування, 28 днів оплачуваної відпустки, додатковий «декрет» для татусів, та матеріальна допомога для батьків при народженні дітей. 
Комфортні умови праці: можливість гібридного формату роботи, офіси забезпеченні укриттями та генераторами, забезпечення сучасною технікою.
Wellbeing програма: для всіх співробітників доступні медичне страхування з першого робочого дня; консультації психолога, нутриціолога чи юриста; дисконт програми на спорт та покупки; family days для дітей та дорослих; масаж в офісі. 
Навчання та розвиток: доступ до понад 130 навчальних онлайн-ресурсів; корпоративні навчальні програми з CX, Data, IT Security, Лідерства, Agile. Корпоративна бібліотека та уроки англійської. 
Крута команда: наші колеги – це спільнота, де вітаються допитливість, талант та інновації. Ми підтримуємо один одного, вчимося разом та зростаємо. Ти можеш знайти однодумців у понад 15-ти професійних ком’юніті, читацькому чи спортивному клубах.
Кар'єрні можливості: ми заохочуємо просування всередині банку між функціями.
Програма підтримки захисників і захисниць: ми зберігаємо робочі місця та виплачуємо середню заробітну плату мобілізованим. Для ветеранів та ветеранок у нас діє програма підтримки, розвивається ветеранська спільнота Банку. Ми працюємо над підвищенням обізнаності керівників та команд з питань повернення ветеранів до цивільного життя. Райффайзен Банк відзначений як один з найкращих роботодавців для ветеранів (Forbes)
Чому Райффайзен Банк? 
Наша головна цінність - люди і ми даємо їм підтримку і визнання, навчаємо, залучаємо до змін. Приєднуйся до команди Райфу, адже для нас ТИ маєш значення! 
Один із найбільших кредиторів економіки та аграрного бізнесу серед приватних банків 
Визнаний найкращим працедавцем за версіями EY, Forbes, Randstad, Franklin Covey, Delo.UA 
Найбільший донор гуманітарної допомогисеред банків (Червоний Хрест України, UNITED24, Superhumans, СМІЛИВІ) 
Одна з найбільших ІТ-продуктових команд серед банків країни 
Один із найбільших платників податків в Україні, за 2023 рік було сплачено 6,6 млрд гривень
Можливості для всіх: 
Райф керується принципами, що фокусуються на людині та її розвитку, у центрі уваги 5 500 співробітників та понад 2,7 мільйони клієнтів 
Підтримуємо принципи різноманіття, рівності та інклюзивності
Ми відкриті до найму ветеранів і людей з інвалідністю та готові адаптувати робоче середовище під ваші особливі потреби
Співпрацюємо зі студентами та людьми старшого віку, створюючи умови для зростання на будь-якому етапі кар’єри
В Райфі ти маєш значення! 
Бажаєш дізнатися більше? – Підписуйся на нас у соц.мережах: Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn
Рекрутер Балабан Ольга olha.balaban@raiffeisen.ua

Additionally, it includes ensuring compliance with anti-money laundering regulations and managing the client portfolio.

linkedin_org_description:
Raiffeisen Bank Public Joint Stock Company was registered on March 27, 1992 (operated under the brand name of Joint Stock Postal Pension Bank Aval until September 25, 2006). Since October 2005, the Bank has been part of the Raiffeisen International Bank Holding AG Group, Austria (since October 2010 — Raiffeisen Bank International AG, as of September 30, 2015 the Group held 96.46% of shares in the Bank).

The Bank provides a broad range of standard and innovative banking services through its nationwide network which comprised, as of September 30, 2015, 617 outlets located in big cities, provincial and community centers throughout Ukraine.

The Bank has an effective management structure, based on a clear division into business lines and supporting verticals (information technologies, operation support, controlling, security and other) both in the Bank’s Head-office and regional directorates. As a truly international bank, Raiffeisen Bank clearly separates front-office functions from back-office ones to achieve higher quality of client service and enhance risk mitigation.

Traditionally, Raiffeisen Bank is ranked among top Ukrainian banks by trust and recognition of clients, partners and experts, both Ukrainian and international.

The Bank strives to improve its customer service quality through using its own long-term experience, the valuable expertise of Raiffeisen Bank International network banks and the newest technologies.

title: Lead Support Specialist
description_text:
About MHA
MHA – Mental Health Association is a nonprofit organization dedicated to helping people live their best life. We provide a wide range of services across Western Massachusetts, including outpatient therapy, substance use recovery, supports for individuals with developmental disabilities or brain injuries, and residential and housing programs. MHA serves individuals impacted by mental illness, substance use, developmental disabilities, brain injuries, and homelessness. Our mission is rooted in inclusion, dignity, and empowering people to lead fulfilling, independent lives.
About Integration & Community Living (ICL)
MHA’s Integration & Community Living (ICL) program supports individuals with developmental disabilities and brain injuries in living independently and meaningfully in the community. In partnership with the Department of Developmental Services (DDS), ICL provides person-centered residential and outreach services throughout Western Massachusetts.
With over 60 years of experience, MHA has been a leader in helping individuals transition from institutional settings into homes where they can grow, make choices, and fully participate in community life. Services range from supported and shared living to emergency respite and privately funded care, all tailored to promote independence, dignity, and connection.
_______________________________________________________________________________________________
Position Summary
As a Lead Support Specialist, you will provide a supportive and safe environment in community residential settings within MHA’s ICL (Integrated Community Living) department. You will help foster growth, independence, and community engagement for individuals who have experienced a brain injury resulting in physical limitations, mental health, and/or cognitive challenges. 
Pay Rate: $21 an hour
Open Shift: Tuesday through Friday 1pm-9pm, Saturday9am-5pm (40h)
Key Responsibilities
Provide respectful, person-centered support to participants in daily living activities, personal care, and skill development.
Promote community integration by facilitating access to transportation, social activities, and external resources.
Support participants' health needs, including medication administration (per MAP standards), appointment scheduling, and monitoring overall wellbeing.
Assist with financial skills and money management, maintaining accurate documentation of all expenditures.
Respond to emergencies and crisis situations appropriately, following agency protocols and communicating with supervisors.
Advocate for participants and help them build self-advocacy skills; serve as a liaison with families, providers, and community supports.
Contribute to the development and implementation of Individual Service Plans (ISPs), documenting progress and participation.
Maintain accurate and timely documentation, including daily logs, incident reports, and health records.
Promote a safe environment by following all safety procedures, assisting with emergency drills, and identifying potential hazards.
Collaborate effectively with team members, attend training, maintain certifications (MAP, CPR, First Aid), and actively participate in meetings and supervision.
Equal Opportunity Statement
The Mental Health Association is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
Valid driver’s license, sufficient automobile insurance, an acceptable driving record, and access to a reliable vehicle during working hours.
Strong communication, organizational, and computer skills.
Adaptability to participants' changing needs.
Ability to pass and maintain certifications in medication administration (MAP), CPR, and First Aid.
Must be at least 18 years old.
High school diploma or GED required; college degree in human services or a related field preferred.
Minimum of six months of relevant experience; experience with individuals with brain injuries, mental health challenges, or developmental disabilities is preferred.
Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision)
Retirement Plan (403B, IRA)
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick & Public Holidays)
Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity)
Long Term Disability
$21 an hour
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Lead Support Specialist, you will provide supportive and safe environments for individuals with brain injuries, promoting their growth and community engagement. Key tasks include assisting with daily living activities, health needs, and advocating for participants.

title: Cowpea Breeding Lead
description_text:
The International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA) invites applications for the internationally recruited position of Cowpea Breeding Lead. 
The International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA) is a not-for-profit institution that generates agricultural innovations to meet Africa’s most pressing challenges of hunger, malnutrition, poverty, and natural resource degradation. Working with various partners across sub-Saharan Africa, we improve livelihoods, enhance food and nutrition security, increase employment, and preserve natural resource integrity. IITA is a member of the One CGIAR, a global agriculture research partnership for a food-secure future. Please visit http://www.iita.org/ for more information on IITA. 
The Cowpea Breeding Lead will lead, coordinate and oversee breeding activities majorly in West Africa to ensure alignment with IIITA strategy and operational plans. More importantly, the role entails the development and delivery of highly productive, climate-resilient, and nutritionally enhanced farmer and market preferred cowpea varieties. Additionally, the role includes setting breeding priorities with stakeholders, improving populations, advancing varieties through multi-stage testing, supporting variety release and breeder seed production, and contributing to regional breeding networks, modernization efforts, and resource mobilization aligned with the new Gates Foundation project (Breakthrough Cowpea Products for the West African Drylands) and CGIAR Breeding for Tomorrow (B4T).
Position Responsibilities 
· Breeding Strategy: Formulating cowpea breeding strategies aimed at achieving specific goals, such as increasing yield, improving stress tolerance, or enhancing nutritional content.
· Product Design and Development: Implement an effective breeding strategy to develop elite breeding material and a product advancement process to identify new candidate varieties for targeted market segments and product profiles.
· Demand-led Breeding: Support and contribute to revision of market segment definition and target product profiles for the 4 breeding pipelines and seed system activities in the region.
· Germplasm Development: Working with NARS and selected partners, identify, obtain and develop appropriate germplasm to sustain the breeding program.
· Coordination: Coordinate the development of stage gate multilocation product performance testing strategy, data analysis and advancement process, which is aligned with target market segments, TPEs or Agro-ecologies.
· Optimization of Breeding Processes and Operations: Ensure cost effective breeding operations, and standard operating procedures that deliver agreed quality and performance indicators.
· Collaboration and Partnerships: 
o Develop strong partnerships with national public breeding programs to collaboratively identify critical germplasm, product needs and define complementary breeding objectives.
o Work in partnership with scientists from other disciplines and interact with industry partners and end users (farmers, extension agents, traders, consumers, etc.) from different organizations to design well defined and clearly prioritized Target Product Profiles in the Target Population of Environments. 
o Enable and actively support the regional crop network to collaboratively plan and implement breeding activities that optimize the development of individual and shared network germplasm products and builds capacity to share knowledge, best practices, training etc.
· Enterprise Breeding Systems (EBS) and Breeding Analytics: Use EBS for management of pedigree, nurseries, yield and disease trials, experimental data and seed inventory along with Bioflow to analyze the raw data.
· Capacity Building: Provide training and mentorship to breeding staff, partners and scholars on breeding techniques, methodologies, data analysis and scientific communication.
· Resource Mobilisation: Contribute to or lead the development of research proposals and other fundraising activities.
· Publication: Lead or contribute to publication of research results in international, peer reviewed journals (Thompson-indexed journals with impact factor).
· Reporting: Develop and deliver high-quality monthly and annual reports that track progress, analyze outcomes, and document achievements against set targets.
Educational Qualifications and Experience 
• PhD in plant breeding, genetics breeding, genetics, or related field 
Core Competencies 
· At least 10 years of experience in plant breeding programs
· Evidence of significant management skills including change management and management of successful breeding teams
· Ability to work well in a diverse multicultural environment
· Excellent interpersonal and organizational skills
· Evidence of experience in resource mobilization
· Excellent written and oral communication skills
· Ability to build consensus/function as part of a team
· Problem solving and decision-making skills
· Resilience under pressure; sound risk judgment 
· Experience of managing staff and budgets
Duty Station: Kano, Nigeria. 
General information: The contract will be for a period of three years, with the possibility of renewal contingent upon the availability of funds and the candidate's performance. IITA offers an internationally competitive remuneration package paid in U.S. Dollars. 
Applications: Applications must include covering letter which should address how the candidate’s background/experience relates to the specific duties of the position applied for, curriculum vitae, names and addresses of three professional referees (which must include either the Head of the applicant’s current or previous organization or applicant’s direct Supervisor/Superior at his/her present or former place of work). The application should be sent online using this link: https://apply.workable.com/iita/#jobs 
Closing Date: 28th February 2026 
IITA is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to building a diverse workforce. 
While all applications will be acknowledged, please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Cowpea Breeding Lead will lead and oversee breeding activities in West Africa, focusing on developing climate-resilient and nutritionally enhanced cowpea varieties. Responsibilities include formulating breeding strategies, coordinating testing, and collaborating with partners to optimize breeding processes.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a PhD in plant breeding or a related field and at least 10 years of experience in plant breeding programs. Strong management skills, excellent communication abilities, and experience in resource mobilization are also required.

title: Junior Operations & Customer Support
description_text:
Operations & Customer Support Coordinator
ERG Media is a publishing house and creative agency specialising in publishing and automotive content. We produce premium books, CGI, film, and creative work for prestigious global brands such as Porsche, operating at the intersection of publishing craft and high-end brand storytelling.
We're a lean, international team with offices in Milan and Japan. Our work demands precision, creativity, and an unwavering commitment to quality.
The Role
We're looking for an Operations & Customer Support Coordinator to keep our business running smoothly across both B2B client operations and B2C retail. You'll be the person who ensures our customers receive exceptional service, our supply chain operates efficiently, and our products reach their destinations on time.
Day-to-day, you'll handle customer enquiries across our retail channels, coordinate with warehouses and logistics partners on order fulfilment, and support the procurement of books and merchandise from our international supplier network. You'll also support project delivery across our creative agency, helping to keep work moving between teams and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.
You'll work closely with our Senior Account Manager and Operations Manager, acting as the connective tissue between our customers, suppliers, and internal teams.
This is a junior role with real scope to grow. You'll start by supporting our operations and customer service functions administratively, with a clear path toward owning supplier relationships and taking on greater responsibility as you develop.
The role is open to candidates who are fully remote and able to work CET hours, with a preference for those located closer to Milan who can spend time at our HQ.
What You'll DoOperations & Customer Support
Respond to customer enquiries across our B2C retail channels, ensuring timely and helpful communication
Process B2B and B2C orders, invoices, and related documentation
Coordinate with warehouse and logistics partners on fulfilment, shipping, and delivery
Track shipments and proactively resolve any issues that arise
Keep customer and order data accurate across our systems
Support returns, exchanges, and customer service escalations
Supply Chain & Procurement
Coordinate with suppliers on timelines, quotes, and deliverables for books and merchandise
Support the Print Production Manager and Sourcing Manager with vendor communications
Keep product information and adminstrative data up to date across systems
Help maintain supplier records and track procurement activity
Project Coordination
Act as the link between creative, account management, and production teams, ensuring smooth handoffs and on-track timelines
Chase progress, gather updates, and keep stakeholders aligned on deliverables
Maintain accurate project data in Asana so the team has a clear picture of status
Spot bottlenecks and flag risks before they become problems
Prepare status updates and keep project documentation organised
Essential
Experience in operations, customer service, logistics, or a production/agency environment
Comfortable working with project management and CRM tools (Asana, Gorgias, Shopify, or similar)
Exposure to e-commerce, retail, or supply chain operations
Highly organised with strong attention to detail
Proactive communicator who follows up without being asked
Clear written and verbal English
Available to work CET business hours
Nice to Have
Publishing or print production experience
Experience working with international suppliers or logistics partners
Familiarity with Shopify or similar e-commerce platforms
Essential:
Experience in an e-commerce envrioment both B2C and B2B
Experience in an agency, project delivery, or production environment
Comfortable working with project management tools (Asana or similar)
Some exposure to logistics, retail, or e-commerce operations
Highly organised with strong attention to detail
Proactive communicator — you follow up without being asked
Clear written and verbal English
Available to work CET business hours
The role is open to those who are fully remote and able to work on CET hours, with a preference for candidates located closer to Milan who can spend time in our HQ.
Nice to have:
Publishing or print production experience
Experience working with international suppliers or logistics partners
ai_core_responsibilities: The Operations & Customer Support Coordinator will handle customer inquiries, coordinate with warehouses and logistics partners, and support project delivery across the creative agency. This role ensures exceptional service and efficient supply chain operations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience in operations, customer service, or logistics, and be comfortable with project management tools. Strong organizational skills and proactive communication are essential.

linkedin_org_locations: Via Privata Maria Teresa, 7, Milan, Lombardy 20123, IT
Oude Oeverstraat 120, Arnhem, Gelderland 6811JZ, NL

title: Junior Operations & Customer Support
description_text:
Operations & Customer Support Coordinator
ERG Media is a publishing house and creative agency specialising in publishing and automotive content. We produce premium books, CGI, film, and creative work for prestigious global brands such as Porsche, operating at the intersection of publishing craft and high-end brand storytelling.
We're a lean, international team with offices in Milan and Japan. Our work demands precision, creativity, and an unwavering commitment to quality.
The Role
We're looking for an Operations & Customer Support Coordinator to keep our business running smoothly across both B2B client operations and B2C retail. You'll be the person who ensures our customers receive exceptional service, our supply chain operates efficiently, and our products reach their destinations on time.
Day-to-day, you'll handle customer enquiries across our retail channels, coordinate with warehouses and logistics partners on order fulfilment, and support the procurement of books and merchandise from our international supplier network. You'll also support project delivery across our creative agency, helping to keep work moving between teams and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.
You'll work closely with our Senior Account Manager and Operations Manager, acting as the connective tissue between our customers, suppliers, and internal teams.
This is a junior role with real scope to grow. You'll start by supporting our operations and customer service functions administratively, with a clear path toward owning supplier relationships and taking on greater responsibility as you develop.
The role is open to candidates who are fully remote and able to work CET hours, with a preference for those located closer to Milan who can spend time at our HQ.
What You'll DoOperations & Customer Support
Respond to customer enquiries across our B2C retail channels, ensuring timely and helpful communication
Process B2B and B2C orders, invoices, and related documentation
Coordinate with warehouse and logistics partners on fulfilment, shipping, and delivery
Track shipments and proactively resolve any issues that arise
Keep customer and order data accurate across our systems
Support returns, exchanges, and customer service escalations
Supply Chain & Procurement
Coordinate with suppliers on timelines, quotes, and deliverables for books and merchandise
Support the Print Production Manager and Sourcing Manager with vendor communications
Keep product information and adminstrative data up to date across systems
Help maintain supplier records and track procurement activity
Project Coordination
Act as the link between creative, account management, and production teams, ensuring smooth handoffs and on-track timelines
Chase progress, gather updates, and keep stakeholders aligned on deliverables
Maintain accurate project data in Asana so the team has a clear picture of status
Spot bottlenecks and flag risks before they become problems
Prepare status updates and keep project documentation organised
Essential
Experience in operations, customer service, logistics, or a production/agency environment
Comfortable working with project management and CRM tools (Asana, Gorgias, Shopify, or similar)
Exposure to e-commerce, retail, or supply chain operations
Highly organised with strong attention to detail
Proactive communicator who follows up without being asked
Clear written and verbal English
Available to work CET business hours
Nice to Have
Publishing or print production experience
Experience working with international suppliers or logistics partners
Familiarity with Shopify or similar e-commerce platforms

title: Senior Inbound Travel Consultant
description_text:
Planet Africa Tours’ Inbound Division offers destination management services to the Japanese market, operating tours across Southern and East Africa. Our expert team has a wealth of knowledge and the connections to deliver an exceptional experience for any travelers visiting from Japan. A combination of local knowledge and an understanding and appreciation of the requirements of the Japanese traveler has made Planet Africa Tours the largest Southern Africa operator in the Japanese market. 
The Senior Reservations Consultant is responsible for proactively designing, operating, and managing bespoke FIT and group bookings, providing a high level of service to customers. 
Key Tasks include, but are not limited to:
Plan, design, and prepare travel itineraries, providing travel advice and recommendations that will improve the guest experience.
Manage bookings from quote to finalizing, invoicing, and travel documentation.
Sourcing and securing alternatives if any availability challenges arise.
Convert quotes into bookings, making every effort to close the sale, and utilizing opportunities to increase margin.
Conduct availability checks, book all accommodation and services, and reconfirm all bookings.
Provisionally book services and manage their release dates.
Compile documentation for tour guides and tour leaders, and documents pertaining to group operations such as rooming list, invoices, etc.
Quality control of all bookings.
Resolve account queries within set deadlines.
Deliver professional, solutions-focused communication to customers.
Assist with training, mentoring, or guidance to junior team members when required. 
EDUCATION: Grade 12 essential. A related tertiary qualification in Travel and Tourism is highly preferable.
EXPERIENCE: Minimum 5 years’ related experience in inbound reservations (preferably at a senior level); well-versed in designing and operating bespoke itineraries for both group and individual travelers; relevant experience working with the Japanese market will be highly beneficial. 
KNOWLEDGE: Strong knowledge of travel products, destinations, and logistics in South and Southern Africa.
SKILLS: Strong computer and systems Skills (UDMS, Microsoft Office/Word/Excel/Outlook/SharePoint; excellent written and verbal communication skills; excellent interpersonal skills; high level of organizational ability and time management; strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills; strong administrative and organizational skills.
ATTRIBUTES: Professional, calm, and solutions-driven approach; ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities; demonstrates accountability and ownership of outcomes; adaptable and enjoys working in a changing environment.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Reservations Consultant is responsible for designing, operating, and managing bespoke travel bookings while providing high-level service to customers. Key tasks include managing bookings, conducting availability checks, and compiling documentation for tours.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a minimum of 5 years of related experience in inbound reservations, preferably at a senior level, with strong knowledge of travel products and logistics in Southern Africa. A related tertiary qualification in Travel and Tourism is highly preferable.
linkedin_org_slogan: We pride ourselves in being South Africa’s most reputable family leisure travel brand with a history of excellence.
linkedin_org_locations: Chiapinni Square, Mechau Street, Cape Town, Western Cape 8001, ZA
linkedin_org_description:
Pentravel was established in 1983 and has for several years been part of the Cullinan Holdings group, a listed company specialising in travel and tourism companies in such areas as hospitality, tour operating, corporate retail travel and leisure travel.

With 20 shops in SA and expert product and destination knowledge, Pentravel is your trusted leisure travel partner. In addition to expertise in selling local, regional and international airfares, the retail travel group has a strong land product strategy in conjunction with key preferred suppliers worldwide, as well as the ability and expertise to package tailor-made breaks based on client preference and budget. From hotel accommodation and resort packages, to coach tours, ski and cruises, Pentravel offers more than a travel consulting service. Pentravel are the leisure travel experts. Foreign exchange, travel insurance and visa services are also provided.

At Pentravel we like to sell the difference; focusing on personal service, great advice and excellent value. We have a high rate of repeat and referral business and believe that knowledge and personal experience add value to your own experience. And it would be our privilege to guide them. Your holiday begins with us!

title: CRM Specialist
locations_alt_raw: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
locations_derived: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
description_text:
KingMakers is a proudly African company shaping the future of sports and iGaming entertainment across the continent.
Since 2018, we’ve been building platforms, including BetKing and SuperSportBet, that bring millions of fans closer to the excitement of play through technology, integrity, and innovation.
Operating across markets such as Nigeria and South Africa, we’re part of a new generation of African businesses redefining what quality, trust, and entertainment look like in our industry.
Our vision is clear: to be the home where Africa plays. 
At KingMakers, we move fast, stay curious, and hold ourselves to high standards of performance and accountability. We believe great products don’t just entertain — they build connection, opportunity, and pride across the communities we serve.
About the Role
As a CRM Specialist at KingMakers, you will play a pivotal role in executing and optimizing CRM campaigns, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs. 
This role involves collaborating cross-functionally to develop effective customer journeys and targeting criteria to meet activation, retention, and reactivation targets. Therefore, a customer first mindset is imperative. 
You will be responsible for crafting personalized cross-channel marketing strategies utilizing onsite, email, SMS, push messaging and more to improve the customer experience, using data to enhance user experiences and grow brand loyalty.
Key Responsibilities
Retention Strategies: Design and implement customer retention initiatives, using data-driven insights to inform strategies that enhance customer loyalty. 
Campaign Management: Manage the execution of CRM campaigns across various channels, ensuring that our messaging is relevant, timely, and resonates with our user base.
Data Analysis: Analyze customer behaviour, preferences, and feedback to inform campaign strategies and optimize engagement efforts.
Segmentation: Develop and manage customer segmentation strategies to personalize messaging and experiences effectively. 
Collaboration: Work closely with retention, marketing, product, and customer service teams to ensure that our CRM initiatives align with business goals. 
Performance Measurement: Track and report on CRM campaign performance, making necessary adjustments to strategies based on results. 
Best Practices: Stay up-to-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in CRM and customer engagement. 
In 6 months, you will:
Have implemented new CRM campaigns that increase customer engagement and improve retention.
Developed a comprehensive understanding of our customer segments.
Established key metrics for measuring the success of CRM initiatives.
Collaborated effectively with cross-functional teams to integrate CRM strategies into broader marketing objectives.
2+ years of experience in CRM B2C, digital marketing, or a related field.
Proven experience with CRM platforms for customer management, segmentation, and campaign automation.
Knowledge of marketing automation and customer engagement tools for personalized campaigns and communications.
Strong analytical skills, able to interpret data and draw actionable insights.
Understanding of customer segmentation and targeting strategies to optimize marketing efforts.
Excellent communication skills to collaborate across teams, thinking creatively to improve customer engagement.
Ability to manage multiple projects, meeting deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Nice to Have:
Industry experience in Sportsbook and/or iGaming.
Experience with Optimove platform.
Experience in A/B testing and marketing campaign optimization.
Health Support: Employer contribution toward your monthly medical aid, helping you invest in your health and wellbeing.
Comprehensive Insurance: Group risk cover providing additional protection and peace of mind for you and your loved ones.
Future Planning: Employer contributions to a provident fund to support your long-term financial security and retirement planning.
Performance Bonus: discretionary rewards that celebrate your impact*
Convenience at Work: free reserved parking at the office
Hybrid Work: a flexible arrangement with 2 days in the office and 3 days remote each week (Update this according to department)
Work with global teams across Nigeria, Spain, Malta & the UK.
*Eligibility for the discretionary bonus depends on the onboarding cycle and start date; employees joining after a certain point in the year may not be eligible for the 2026 cycle.

Key duties include designing retention initiatives, managing campaign execution, analyzing customer behavior, developing segmentation, and collaborating with various internal teams.

title: Plant Movement Operative
description_text:
We are seeking 4x Plant Movement Operatives with backgrounds in heavy-load movement, crane operation, and large-scale forklift work. You will support the offloading, manoeuvring, and positioning of PowerPods/E‑pods, and standby generators from delivery wagons into designated shop-floor fit‑out areas.
Key Responsibilities:
Offloading and internal movement of large/heavy industrial units, including: 
PowerPods
E‑Pods
Standby generators
Manoeuvring loads safely from wagons to specified fit‑out locations.
Operating a variety of movement equipment, including: 
Electric and manual skate movers
Toe jacks
Large‑scale forklifts (20 tonnes and above)
Gantry cranes (experience with up to 64‑tonne systems preferred)
Ensuring all movements are completed safely, efficiently, and in line with site regulations.
Working collaboratively with site teams during critical lift and placement operations.
Experience desired:
Ideally you possess experience in all of the responsibilities mentioned above but if not, do not worry we would still be interested in your profile. This is a brand-new project and we need a variety of skills / experience. 
Private Health Insurance
Performance Bonus
25 days annual leave + bank holidays
Birthday Off
Pension Plan
Salaries are being determined and will depend on experience. 
AVK|SEG does not discriminate on the race, colour, religion, sex, age, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other reason prohibited by law in provision of employment opportunities and benefits. We will endeavour to respond to all applicants however due to the volume of responses, we can only guarantee that candidates who have been shortlisted will be contacted.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves offloading and internal movement of large industrial units, ensuring safe manoeuvring from delivery wagons to designated areas. Operatives will also operate various movement equipment and collaborate with site teams during critical operations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should ideally have experience in heavy-load movement and equipment operation, though the company is open to a variety of skills and experiences. The position is part of a new project, indicating flexibility in candidate profiles.

AVK is the largest and fastest-growing supplier of innovative power solutions for data centres, AI infrastructure and leading organisations in the UK and Europe. We specialise in all aspects of design, planning, implementation, and continuing maintenance.

At AVK, we power tomorrow’s data. As a team, we are transforming the way the data industry connects to and consumes energy by developing innovative and sustainable solutions across the globe. We work together, across disciplines, to tackle complex, large-scale, and ground-breaking engineering and energy projects. 

With offices and hubs across the UK and Europe, AVK is in a unique position, in terms of operational scale and delivery capability. We deploy leading-edge solutions for our clients, helping them to meet their power demands alongside their sustainability goals.

To learn more, visit www.avk-seg.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

title: Relationship Manager
description_text:
Title Relationship Manager 
Desired Location Sofia, Bulgaria
Hybrid
The world of payment processing is rapidly evolving, and businesses are looking for loyal and strategic partners, to help them grow.
WE ARE NUVEI. Nuvei is the Canadian fintech company accelerating the business of clients around the world. Nuvei’s modular, flexible and scalable technology allows leading companies to accept next-gen payments, offer all payout options and benefit from card issuing, banking, risk and fraud management services. Connecting businesses to their customers in more than 200 markets, with local acquiring in 45+ markets, 150 currencies and more than 600 alternative payment methods, Nuvei provides the technology and insights for customers and partners to succeed locally and globally with one integration.
At Nuvei, we live our core values, and we thrive on solving complex problems. We’re dedicated to continually improving our product and providing relentless customer service. We are always looking for exceptional talent to join us on the journey!
Your Mission
We are looking for a Relationship Manager to join our fast-growing Digital Assets team. Reporting to our Senior Relationship Manager, you will be responsible for developing strong relationships with some of our most important customers.
The Relationship Manager is responsible for managing a portfolio of crypto and digital assets merhcnats with the goal of maintaining and developing strong customer relationships. The main aims are to act as a trusted advisor and primary point of contact on existing, new and evolving methods of payment, while also ensuring consistent and reliable revenue growth.
Responsibilities
Be the main point of contact for the businesses you manage and build strong, long-lasting customer relationships
Use your understanding of your customers, our products and industry to proactively go to customers with recommendations that will help them grow
Use transactional data to provide insights to your customers on how they can improve performance across all areas of their business
Work closely with our Product teams to help influence our new product developments based on customer needs
Identify and close new opportunities to grow business within your portfolio of customers
Act as a central point of contact to a wide range of internal functions.
Travel to visit customers as required
3+ years of experience in a relationship management/account management role preferably within enterprise software or other technology related industries.
Self motivated and a wide systemic view of the industry, company and it’s products
Be genuinely curious to learn – both about our dynamic industry and our customers.
Comfortable working with data and a skilled enough communicator to be able to use that data to make new product recommendations in a commercial setting.
A desire to progress and develop yourself personally and professionally
Relevant payments industry and digital assets/crypto experience – either from another payments provider or from the merchant side – is a definite plus, but not required
Nuvei is an equal-opportunity employer that celebrates collaboration and innovation and is committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. The team at Nuvei is comprised of a wealth of talent, skill, and ambition. We believe that employees are happiest when they’re empowered to be their true, authentic selves. So, please come as you are. We can’t wait to meet you.
A challenging job in a fast-developing, international company.
A friendly work environment where you can thrive and develop your skills.
Career advancement possibilities.
Competitive remuneration package.
Additional Health insurance incl. Dentist.
Fully covered MultiSport card.
Food vouchers.
Employee discounts card, Seminars and conference tickets, Playroom, and other additional perks.
Please send your resume in English.

title: Google Ads Specialist (DTC eCommerce)
description_text:
About Huzzle
At Huzzle, we connect high-performing professionals with global companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include startups, digital agencies, and tech platforms in industries like SaaS, MarTech, FinTech, and EdTech. We match top sales talent to full-time remote roles where they’re hired directly into client teams and provided ongoing support by Huzzle.
Role Type: Full-time
Engagement: Independent Contractor 
Key Responsibilities
Own and scale Google Ads and/or Bing Ads accounts for DTC eCommerce campaigns 
Build, optimise, and iterate on Performance Max (P-Max) campaigns with clear ROAS targets 
Launch and manage Demand Gen and YouTube campaigns to support upper and mid-funnel growth 
Monitor and optimize performance against CAC and ROAS benchmarks 
Analyze attribution and performance using tools such as Northbeam, Triple Whale, Rockerbox, and GA4 
Translate performance data into actionable insights using Excel or Google Sheets 
Collaborate with creative, CRO, and growth teams to improve ad performance 
Create clear reporting and data visualizations for stakeholders 
Continuously test audiences, creatives, bidding strategies, and funnel structures
Must Haves:
3+ years of hands-on Google Ads experience for DTC eCommerce brands 
Proven experience managing Performance Max (P-Max) campaigns 
Strong understanding of CAC, ROAS, and profitability-focused growth 
Experience with Demand Gen, YouTube, or full-funnel paid media strategies 
Working knowledge of Northbeam, Triple Whale, Rockerbox, and/or GA4 
Advanced proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets 
Strong analytical mindset with experience in data analysis and visualization 
Comfortable working in fast-paced, performance-driven environments 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Nice to Have: 
Experience with Bing Ads 
Background working with high-spend DTC ad accounts ($50k+/month) 
CRO or landing page optimisation exposure 
💻 Fully Remote: Work from anywhere with international teams
🧭 Ongoing Support: Receive guidance from Huzzle before and after placement
💰 Competitive Compensation: Salaries vary by client and candidate preference — we’ll match you with options that fit your goals
ai_core_responsibilities: The Google Ads Specialist will own and scale Google Ads and/or Bing Ads accounts for DTC eCommerce campaigns, focusing on building and optimizing Performance Max campaigns. They will also launch and manage Demand Gen and YouTube campaigns while analyzing performance metrics to drive actionable insights.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have 3+ years of hands-on Google Ads experience specifically for DTC eCommerce brands and a strong understanding of key performance metrics like CAC and ROAS. Experience with Demand Gen and YouTube campaigns is also required, along with proficiency in data analysis tools.
linkedin_org_slogan: ChatGPT for Jobs. 100k+ people use Huzzle monthly to find jobs, internships and advance their careers.
linkedin_org_locations: London, England, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Huzzle is an early careers platform connecting students, societies, and employers. Starting out as a CRM for pre-professional student societies, Huzzle has evolved into the university career companion used by over 100,000 talents in the UK.

Research shows that students involved in student initiatives are 3X more hireable, and are rated 18% more career-ready by employers. 

On Huzzle, companies can access these talents by co-hosting events with over 1,000 diverse societies, promoting their roles, and through personalised direct messaging campaigns.

title: Manager, Client Experience Technical
locations_alt_raw: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
locations_derived: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
description_text:
A LITTLE BIT ABOUT Boldr
Boldr is the first global B-Corp dedicated to delivering world-class Client experiences while creating access to dignified, meaningful work in communities around the world.
We are a global team, united by our desire to connect diverse people with common values for bolder impact. 
We employ just over a thousand team members across five countries and we want to employ over 5,000 people by 2027, if not sooner.
LET’S START WITH OUR VALUES
Meaningful connections start with AUTHENTICITY
We do our best work by being CURIOUS
We grow by remaining DYNAMIC
Our success combines AMBITIOUS VISION with OPERATIONAL EXCELLENCE 
At the heart of great partnerships we’ll always find EMPATHY
WHAT IS YOUR ROLE
We’re looking for a self-starter, hard-working, and dedicated team member to jump in and be a key part of our Client Experience team. 

In this role, you are responsible for maintaining and nurturing Boldr’s Client relationships as well as developing and empowering Boldr’s Team Captains to be successful in their role. You will act as a business owner taking full accountability for the success of your Client and teams, ensuring they are fully equipped to meet our commitments to our Clients and doing so with the highest level of quality. 

You will help foster a healthy working environment for our team members by working with other functions and internal stakeholders (such as People Experience and Operations). You will represent Boldr to our Clients and act as our internal Client across various departments. You will need to be laser-focused on our operational performance and comfortable with the management of global teams on a daily basis. You will be responsible for day-to-day Client communication, performance reporting, training, and escalation management. Having an eye for details will be important as you’ll be maintaining all Client Success tools and procedures. 

WHAT WILL YOU DO
Processes
Develop and maintain an authentic relationship with all the Client and internal stakeholders.
Act as a strategic advisor to our Clients and internal stakeholders by managing key points of contact and updating key challenges and opportunities.
Execute daily and weekly reviews of our operational performance with Client stakeholders and Boldr leadership.
Ability to work seamlessly with different departments namely: Implementation, Client Solutions, Finance, IT, People Experience and Office Operations.
Communicating and resolving basic to complex inquiries and concerns in an accurate, valid, complete, and timely manner. 
Create and manage Client contracts such as MSAs and SLAs. 
Manage a team of Team Captains and monitor compliance against SLAs and achievement of Client KPIs.
Partner with Team Captains to ensure documentation is constantly updated and identification of process improvements are sourced with the TC and their team.
Facilitate Client Business Reviews in partnership with your SBU Head and organize the Boldr team to create these reviews.
Develop, maintain, and leverage relationships and networks within the Client organization to identify and develop business opportunities.
Update Hubspot deal cards and ensure all necessary items are appropriately invoiced.
Follow up with clients on invoicing, pricing discrepancies, and other billing-related issues. 
People Management
Plan and oversee hiring, onboarding, training, quality assurance, tool optimization, and team building.
Supervise and develop Team Captains through guidance, observation, and performance management activities. 
As needed, joins the Team Captain on calls with the Client, team huddles or coaching sessions to stay up to date with all Client and team developments. 
This also provides an opportunity to observe the Team Captain’s presentation, Client communication and coaching skills. 
Taking steps to ensure learning and growth through development plans and, when necessary, progressive counseling. 
Promote and foster a culture of continuous improvement by providing positive and constructive feedback to team members.
Establish, promote, and maintain the Boldr culture within the organization. 
Understand employee career growth aspirations to enable matching Boldr’s needs with employee abilities.
Act as the escalation point for the Team Captains.
Responsible for upholding our organization’s values throughout the workplace.
Monitor achievement of internal and external KPI’s and ensure Operational Excellence.
Identify performance issues, conduct root cause analysis, and work with operations to improve performance on KPIs through motivating, guiding, and holding local operations managers accountable for following SLA. 
Tools, Administration, and Reporting
Familiarity with Google Suite of Services is a must for this role.
Gmail, GDrive, Sheets, Slides, and Docs are used heavily in Boldr
Experience with Hubspot is a plus.
Experience optimizing platforms such as ZenDesk, TalkDesk, Shopify applications is also a plus.
Know how to use data to generate reports and assess the health and efficacy of a program, and how to take action to mitigate or improve.
Maintain Client schedule (Weekly Updates, Monthly Business Reviews &, Quarterly Business.
Reviews, Implementations) and collect feedback and drive necessary actions to completion.
Training
Coordinate necessary training and best practices related to Tools and Processes.
Onboard new hires to ensure they are fully ready to succeed in their role.
Ongoing support and knowledge sharing
Obtain feedback from Clients and team members on processes and tools to ensure continuous improvement.
Provides input and communication to drive Client Success improvement, for all Clients, enterprise-wide.
WHAT WE’LL LIKE ABOUT YOU
YOU ARE…
Curious and authentic, just like us! #beboldr 
An analytical and critical thinker, with an eye for even the most minute of details
Passionate about client satisfaction
YOU HAVE…
Bachelor's/College Degree of any related field you’re passionate about!
5+ years in a Client facing role.
2+ years knowledge of BPO, Staffing, Tech, or other Business Service Industries 
2+ years people management experience.
Manipulate Excel or Google Sheets, and Google Slides to be helpful with the reporting work you’ll be responsible for. 
Focus on listening and understanding what other people are saying, ask questions as appropriate, and not interrupt at inappropriate times.
Maintain an open dialogue with the organization's external and internal Client partners in both positive and negative situations.
Read, research, and draft letters, emails, and documents.
Identify complex problems and review related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions.
Speaking in a public setting and delivering presentations to individuals and groups.
Cope effectively with losses or rejections by moving on to other challenges and learning from past experiences.

title: OME Senior Safety Consultant
description_text:
Salary range: £65,000 per annum - £80,000 depending on skills and experience. 
Location: Remote / Hybrid - Full or part time.
Working Pattern: We operate a flexible hybrid working pattern at Occam Group, with time split between the office, client sites and working from home. 
Job Description
Day to day, you will work with clients providing specialist consultancy services and support business development activities. You will use your engineering knowledge to influence the design, operation and maintenance of complex systems to ensure that technical risks are identified, communicated and managed; and that the safety of those using or relying on the system is not compromised.
You will join our Ordnance, Munitions and Explosives (OME) consultants who are currently delivering on the most cutting edge OME programmes covering directed energy weapons, hypersonics, autonomous ordnance clearance, conventional weapons and the deterrent. 
Duties to include 
Providing hazard and risk analysis technical leadership to OME projects and junior staff.
Conducting Independent Safety Audits for OME programmes.
Setting and implementing safety strategies for complex and novel OME systems.
Leading OME integration studies to air platforms, ships, submarines and land systems.
Setting OME certification and qualification strategies and programmes.
Essential Requirements 
Educated to degree level (or equivalent) preferably in a related technical discipline.
Desirable Requirements 
Chartered Status with UK Engineering Council (desirable).
Consultancy experience (desirable).
This is a fantastic opportunity for an experienced OME professional. If you're ready to apply your skills and develop in a fast-paced, supportive environment, we'd love to hear from you! 
Due to the nature of the work that the Occam Group undertake, you must be a UK national in order to apply. 
You will be required to undergo pre-employment screening and must be able to satisfy clearance criteria for UK National Security Vetting. 
We provide our people with excellent benefits package that comprises of: 
Annual bonus linked to company and individual performance 
25 days holiday + bank holidays 
Loyalty Leave 
Attractive pension scheme - matched pension contributions, up to 8% from day one 
Family Friendly Policies 
Private healthcare 
Targeted professional development 
Life Assurance (4x salary) and Income Protection (75% of salary) 
Paid membership fees to a professional institution 
Cycle to work salary sacrifice scheme 
EV lease salary sacrifice scheme 
Individuals from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply, as we believe that diversity and inclusion are fundamental to creating a dynamic and thriving workplace culture.
ai_core_responsibilities: You will provide specialist consultancy services and support business development activities, ensuring technical risks are identified and managed. You will lead OME projects and provide technical leadership to junior staff.
ai_requirements_summary: A degree in a related technical discipline is required, with chartered status and consultancy experience being desirable. Candidates must be UK nationals and able to satisfy clearance criteria for UK National Security Vetting.

title: Sales Development Representative
description_text:
About Us
We are Up. 
We design and deliver digital-first employee benefits that transform the workplace experience.
We believe that happy and healthy employees work better and smarter. Our solutions help you achieve this by improving productivity and reducing costs, while consistently delivering an exceptional user experience.

Today, we empower tens of thousands of leading companies across Greece to engage and retain their people. We continuously refine the Up experience based on insights from our users, always looking ahead to the future of work.

Up Hellas is a wholly owned subsidiary of Up Group, present in 34 countries worldwide.
www.uphellas.gr
www.up-group.coop
We are seeking a talented Sales Development Representative to join our team!
The ideal candidate should be someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and is passionate about connecting with potential clients, identifying new business opportunities, and supporting our Key Accounts team through lead generation and outreach. Your Main Missions:

Manage and distribute incoming leads
Execute LinkedIn activities (e.g. article sharing, content engagement)
Identify new business opportunities and potential customers
Maintain a high level of engagement with prospects and proactively identify their needs and challenges
Contribute to the development of new products and services, including market strategies and pricing models
Present products and services to potential clients and introduce new offerings to existing customers
Researching new markets

Your Profile:
Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Management, Economics, or Social Sciences
Minimum 6 months of proven experience in a telephone-based role (e.g., call center, telesales, or similar)

Good understanding of market and business trends
Advanced user of MS Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a fast-paced environment
We offer:
A dynamic work environment with a brand-new office space!
A suite of benefits: FlexOne, Fitpass, Private medical plan, Public Transportation Card, and even breakfast!
A full-time, indefinite contract
Location: Thessaloniki
All information received will be treated with strict confidentiality.

title: Director of After School Programming and Enrichment for Lower School
description_text:
POSITION DESCRIPTION
St. Catherine's School is an Episcopal day school in Richmond, Virginia, founded in 1890 enrolling roughly 950 girls age 3 through grade 12. Located on a 16-acre campus in the western part of the city, St. Catherine's core values include pursuing academic excellence, developing character, nurturing spiritual growth, celebrating individuals, and building community. 
St. Catherine's School provides a rigorous education that prepares girls with diverse perspectives for a lifetime of learning, leadership, and service in a global community.
POSITION OVERVIEW
The Director is responsible for the overall planning, staffing, and management of the After School Program serving St. Catherine’s students in grades JK-4. This program connects girls with a variety of enrichment classes and activities.
Supervisor: Head of Lower School and Early Childhood
Supervisory Responsibilities: Instructors, staff and vendors
Position Status: Full time, exempt, 10-month schedule
Hours of Work: Standard Work Week, Hours 10:30am - 6:30pm
ESSENTIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
Manage the program registration process
Develop, monitor, and maintain the program budget
Create, update, and manage marketing and informational materials for push pages, flyers, Papers for Parents, and the public SaintsNet page
Maintain and regularly update the SaintsNet web page
Ensure timely publication of current information regarding projects, classes, and special events
Oversee student activities content, including photos and highlights
Maintain accurate operating hours and essential program information
Update and manage faculty biographies
Prepare and distribute a monthly communication email to parents
Develop, implement, and maintain program policies and procedures, including safety and health guidelines and parent communication protocols
Create and maintain an annual enrichment schedule organized by trimester
Supervise instructors and staff; coordinate enrichment class teachers and external vendors
Provide administrative support, including sign-out procedures, attendance tracking, and enrichment class rosters
Research and develop age-appropriate daily activities for each student group
Identify and research opportunities to expand and enhance the program
Assist with program setup and cleanup as needed
Order and manage supplies necessary for students and staff
Coordinate with Assistant Head of Early Childhood to ensure all religiously-exempt childcare program requirements are met and maintained consistently (for example, Department of Social Services)
PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORK ENVIRONMENT
While performing the duties of this position, the employee is required to sit; stand; walk; use hands to manipulate, handle and feel objects, tools, or controls; reach with hands and arms; climb stairs; balance; stoop, kneel, crouch and crawl; talk and hear; engage in moderately strenuous physical activity both indoors and out; and smell. The employee may occasionally lift or move up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this position include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. 
TRAVEL
Travel is not a requirement for this role.
EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT
St. Catherine’s School does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, or national and ethnic origin in the administration of its hiring practices. St. Catherine’s School is committed to providing an inclusive educational environment. We actively engage students, families, faculty and staff from diverse backgrounds and experiences. Our School community respects the differences in ethnicity, race, religion, sexual orientation and socioeconomic status. Consistent with the School’s mission, St. Catherine’s fosters in our students, and experts from all others, respect for the dignity and wholeness of every person. For more information on St. Catherine’s Access, Equity and Belonging please follow this link https://www.st.catherines.org/about/access-equity-and-belonging
CONDITIONAL OFFERS
An offer of employment for this position is contingent upon the applicant’s right to work in the United States, authorization for and completion of successful background checks as may be requested by the school or required by law.
BENEFITS
St. Catherine’s School offers a highly competitive compensation package for this full-time position with benefits to include excellent health plan options, retirement benefits, exhaustive professional development and networking opportunities as well as state-of-the art facilities.
Note: This position description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work to be performed by employees within this classification. This position description should not be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties and responsibilities required or assigned to an employee in this position. Nothing contained in this position description restricts St. Catherine’s School’s right to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this position at any time.
REQUIREMENTS AND QUALIFICATIONS
Bachelor’s degree in Education or equivalent work experience required; Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in after-school programming, enrichment programs, or Lower School education, including leadership or supervisory experience
Strong understanding of child development, age-appropriate enrichment, and best practices for student safety and well-being
Proven ability to manage budgets, registration processes, and operational logistics
Experience supervising, mentoring, and evaluating faculty, staff, and enrichment instructors
Excellent organizational and project management skills with strong attention to detail
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including experience communicating effectively with parents and school leadership
Proficiency with educational technology platforms, websites, and content management systems (e.g., school portals, registration software)
Demonstrated ability to plan and execute engaging enrichment opportunities across academic, athletic, artistic, and social-emotional domains
Commitment to the school’s mission, values, and community culture
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment while maintaining a high level of professionalism
Current CPR/First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain upon hire)
ai_core_responsibilities: The Director is responsible for planning, staffing, and managing the After School Program for students in grades JK-4, ensuring a variety of enrichment classes and activities. This includes overseeing program registration, budget management, and communication with parents.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a bachelor's degree in Education or equivalent experience, along with 3-5 years in after-school programming or Lower School education. Strong organizational, communication, and supervisory skills are essential, along with a commitment to the school's mission.
linkedin_org_locations: RR 8 Box 600, Du Bois, Pennsylvania 15801-8728, US
linkedin_org_description: All Girls since 1890. Experts in the way girls learn best, St. Catherine’s offers Chinese and Spanish (K-12), a laptop program, STEM and more than 30 honors and 20 AP classes.

title: Area Special Events Sales Agent - Italy & Spain
locations_alt_raw: Milan, Metropolitan City of Milan, Italy
description_text:
A fabulous opportunity to join an incredible luxury hospitality brand and an amazing Sales Team representing the most stunning properties located in Southern Europe. As part of the Special Events Sales Office (SESO), you will be the first point of contact for the MICE, Celebration, and Special Interest segments for our 10 iconic hotels across Italy and Spain.
Scope and Purpose
The Area Special Events Sales Agent is responsible for delivering an impeccable first impression, providing expert destination advice, and managing the end-to-end reservation process. You will ensure that every client request is handled with the highest level of service from initial inquiry through to the final confirmation of the program.
Key Responsibilities
Handle group requests across all segments, including qualifying, negotiating, and contracting using sophisticated sales techniques.
Proactively develop and maintain strong relationships with clients to ensure Belmond remains their preferred choice.
Liaise daily with on-property teams, Regional Sales & Marketing staff, and Belmond Worldwide Sales Offices to align on strategy and execution.
Maintain deep knowledge of our hotels, local destinations, and the competitive landscape to provide superior client advice.
Perform general office duties and ensure all business processes strictly adhere to company guidelines.
Minimum 1 year of sales, events, or reservations experience within the luxury hotel or travel industry. Previous experience in group reservations is a significant plus.
College degree in Business Management, Hotel Management, or Marketing.
Perfect command of Italian and excellent English (both written and spoken). Knowledge of Spanish is considered a strong advantage.
High competence in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and proficiency in hotel PMS systems such as Opera.
Strong organizational and multi-tasking abilities, high attention to detail, and a customer-focused, problem-solving attitude.
Must hold an EU passport or a valid legal permit to work in Italy.
What We Offer
At Belmond we pride ourselves on fostering a culture built on kindness, active listening, and genuine connections with our colleagues and guests. We offer a comprehensive range of compensation, perks and benefits including:
Permanent contract within CCNL Turismo Federalberghi 
Competitive salary and life insurance plan 
Complimentary and preferred rate experiences at our iconic destinations 
Harvard Management Online Training 
Corporate Belmond and LVMH Benefits 
Meals vouchers 
We strive to create an environment where our employees can thrive both personally and professionally. With a commitment to your ongoing development, you'll have the opportunity to learn and grow alongside some of the most talented individuals in the industry. Join us and be part of creating unforgettable moments for our guests, communities, and each other.
The Belmond & LVMH Family
Belmond is proud to be part of LVMH, and the Belmond global collection of iconic hotels, trains and river cruises, all creating exceptional experiences worldwide. When you join one of our properties, you join a global family of history-rich hotels and breathtaking destinations, where our employees are at the heart of the Belmond experience.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Area Special Events Sales Agent is responsible for managing group requests and ensuring high-level service from inquiry to confirmation. This includes developing client relationships and collaborating with on-property teams and sales staff.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a minimum of 1 year of experience in sales, events, or reservations within the luxury hotel or travel industry, with a college degree in a relevant field. Proficiency in Italian and English is required, with Spanish as a plus.
linkedin_org_locations: London, W1W 7NY, GB
linkedin_org_description: Belmond was born from a passion for connecting discerning travellers with the world’s most remarkable properties, locations and journeys. From hotels and trains to river cruises and safaris, Belmond curates incomparable experiences and crafts unforgettable stories. With a legacy spanning over 45 years, since the acquisition of Hotel Cipriani in Venice in 1976, Belmond has grown into a global collection of 43 properties spread across 24 countries and territories. Exceptional destinations connected by legendary journeys are the very soul of Belmond, where the path that brings you to a place is as important as the destination itself. A pioneer of slow travel, Belmond has been operating the Venice Simplon-Orient-Express since 1982. The group later expanded to include pristine beach resorts, such as Maroma on the Riviera Maya, historic hideaways such as Villa San Michele in the Florentine foothills, urbane icons, such as Copacabana Palace in Rio de Janeiro, and gateways to UNESCO world heritage sites, such as Hotel das Cataratas in Brazil’s Iguassu National Park. As proud custodians of storied properties, Belmond is committed to building on the past to create a new legacy: the heritage of the future. Working with communities and local talents, together with the world's most respected chefs, designers and artists, Belmond continues its purpose of perpetuating the legendary art of travel. Since 2019, Belmond has been part of the world’s leading luxury group, LVMH (Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton).
