title: Electrical Project Manager
description_text:
The Electrical Project Manager oversees all aspects of electrical projects within TEiC, ensuring successful execution from initiation to completion. Reporting to the ES Operation Manager, this role involves coordinating with internal teams, clients, and subcontractors to deliver projects on time, within budget, and according to quality standards. 
Position requires travel of 50% to 75% with the possibility of 100% based on project demand. 
Project Execution:
Develop comprehensive project plans, including scope, schedule, and budget.
Coordinate with internal teams to ensure resources are allocated effectively.
Oversee project execution, ensuring adherence to quality standards and safety regulations. 
Identify potential risks and issues early in the project lifecycle and develop mitigation strategies.
Proactively address project issues to minimize impact on schedule and budget.
Escalate unresolved issues to senior management as needed for resolution.
Prepare and distribute project status reports to stakeholders, highlighting key milestones, risks, and issues.
Maintain accurate project documentation, including contracts, change orders, and progress reports.
Archive project documents and deliverables upon project completion for future reference.
Customer Focus:
Serve as the primary point of contact for clients throughout the project lifecycle.
Manage client expectations and address concerns in a timely and professional manner.
Build and maintain strong client relationships to foster repeat business and referrals.
Collaboration:
Lead and motivate project team members, including engineers, technicians, and subcontractors.
Coordinate with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment of project goals and objectives.
Provide guidance and support to team members to overcome challenges and achieve project milestones.
Financial Management:
Monitor project budgets and expenditures, identifying cost-saving opportunities and mitigating risks.
Ensure accurate and timely invoicing and billing to clients and vendors.
Implement cost control measures to maximize project profitability.
Quality Control:
Ensure compliance with industry standards, codes, and regulations throughout the project.
Conduct regular inspections and audits to verify adherence to quality requirements.
Implement corrective actions as necessary to maintain project quality standards.
Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering, Construction Management, or related field experience.
Project Management Professional (PMP) certification preferred.
Minimum of 5 years of experience in electrical project management, preferably in industrial construction.
Completion of OSHA 30-Hour Construction Safety Training (or equivalent) required prior to hire or within 30 days of onboarding.
Strong project management skills, with the ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and deliver complex projects on time and within budget.
Excellent organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks and manage competing priorities effectively.
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively convey technical information to diverse audiences.
Strong negotiation and conflict resolution skills, with the ability to resolve issues and reach consensus among stakeholders.
Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision) Effective on your first day! 
Wellness Programs and Awards Get healthier and earn premium discounts! 
Gym Reimbursement and Weight Loss Benefit
Retirement Plan (401k, IRA) Company match! 
Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D)
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick & Holidays)
Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity)
Short Term & Long Term Disability
Training & Development
Employee Assistance Program
Flexible Spending Accounts
Duncan, SC Location Onsite Gym

With a unified network of specialized subsidiaries, BPI provides a comprehensive portfolio of energy expertise under one roof. Our collaborative approach removes complexity, ensures consistency, and drives innovation across every engagement.

title: Data Engineer
description_text:
About UBDS
UBDS Group helps organisations deliver meaningful digital transformations through modern technology solutions. We work with clients across the public and private sector to design and build products, platforms and services that genuinely make a difference to users.
We’re looking for a Data Engineer who enjoys working with data, technology and users to solve real problems. Someone who is comfortable working in delivery teams, curious about how things work, and keen to build reliable, well-designed data solutions that get used.
The Role
As a Data Engineer at UBDS, you’ll work as part of multidisciplinary teams alongside clients, designers, analysts and engineers to deliver modern data solutions. You’ll contribute to the design and build of data pipelines, models and analytics products, learning from experienced colleagues and applying best practices along the way.
This is a hands-on role where you’ll develop your technical skills, collaborate closely with others, and gain experience delivering solutions that operate in real-world environments.
What You’ll Do
Help translate business problems into data, analytical and technical tasks with support from senior colleagues. 
Design, build and maintain data pipelines, models and products using SQL, Python and modern data engineering tools. 
Work with data warehouses, lakehouse platforms and event-driven patterns, including building semantic models for analytics and visualisation. 
Contribute to solutions built on cloud data and AI platforms (AWS/Azure), following good software engineering, testing and CI/CD practices. 
Apply analytics, experimentation, AI/ML integration, governance, security and data quality practices as part of delivery teams. 
Create clear data visualisations and explanations that help users understand and act on insights. 
Work closely with users and stakeholders in Agile, client-driven environments, learning from live services and user feedback. 
Follow relevant industry and government standards and contribute to solutions that are well documented, maintainable and easy to hand over. 
What We’re Looking ForCore Data & Technical Skills
Experience translating business questions into data or analytical requirements, with guidance where needed. 
Good SQL and Python skills for data modelling, transformation, automation and analysis. 
Experience working with data models in data warehouses, lakehouse environments or analytics tools such as Power BI. 
Familiarity with modern data engineering frameworks and tools (e.g. Spark / PySpark, Pandas, orchestration tools). 
Experience working with cloud data platforms on AWS or Azure (e.g. Databricks, Redshift, Microsoft Fabric). 
Understanding of software engineering fundamentals, including Git, testing, CI/CD and modular design. 
Basic analytics and statistical understanding, including metrics and simple experimentation. 
Awareness of AI/ML concepts, such as using model APIs or prompts, with a desire to build experience in this area. 
Understanding of data governance, security and quality concepts, including access control and GDPR considerations. 
Experience communicating insights through data visualisation using tools like Power BI, Tableau or Python charts. 
Consulting & Delivery Mindset
A user-focused approach and curiosity about who will use the solution and why it matters. 
Ability to communicate clearly and work collaboratively in client-driven, sometimes ambiguous environments. 
Willingness to contribute to clear slides and written materials with support and guidance. 
A logical, thoughtful approach to problem solving and delivery. 
Experience contributing to production systems or live services, with an interest in learning from user feedback. 
Attention to detail in code, documentation and naming conventions. 
A product mindset, with experience working in Agile teams delivering features and outcomes iteratively. 
Awareness of industry and government standards, and an openness to learning and applying them in delivery.

Why people choose to grow their careers at UBDS Group
Professionals choose to grow their careers at UBDS Group for its reputation as a dynamic and forward-thinking organisation that is deeply committed to both innovation and employee development. At UBDS Group, employees are given unique opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects across a diverse range of industries, exposing them to new challenges and learning opportunities that are pivotal for professional growth. The Group’s culture emphasises continuous improvement, offering ample training programs, mentorship, and the chance to gain certifications that enhance their skills and marketability. 
UBDS Group fosters a collaborative environment where creativity and innovation are encouraged, allowing employees to contribute ideas and solutions that have a tangible impact on the company and its clients. This combination of professional development, a culture of innovation, and the opportunity to make meaningful contributions makes UBDS Group an attractive place for those looking to advance their careers and be at the forefront of technological and operational excellence.
Employee Benefits
Training – All team members are offered a number of options in terms of personal development, whether it is technical led, business acumen or methodologies. We want you to grow with us and to help us achieve more
Private medical cover for you and your spouse/partner, offered via Vitality
Discretionary bonus based on a blend of personal and company performance
Holiday – You will receive 25 Days holiday, plus 1 day for Birthday and 1 day for your work anniversary in addition to UK bank holidays
Electric Vehicle leasing with salary sacrifice
Contributed Pension Scheme
Death in service cover
About UBDS Group
At UBDS Group our mission is to support entrepreneurs who are setting new standards with technology solutions across cloud services, cybersecurity, data and AI, ensuring that every investment advances our commitment to innovation, making a difference, and creating impactful solutions for organisations and society. 
Equal Opportunities
We are an equal opportunities employer and do not discriminate on the grounds of gender, sexual orientation, marital or civil partner status, pregnancy or maternity, gender reassignment, race, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, disability or age.

Responsibilities also involve working with cloud platforms, applying software engineering best practices, and creating data visualizations to help users derive insights.

A consulting mindset, user focus, clear communication, and understanding of software engineering fundamentals are also essential.

title: Casual Night Receptionist
description_text:
Here at Millennium Hotels UK, we value your skills, encourage growth by nurturing your personality, and reward your dedication. You'll learn not only from fellow colleagues, but also through our M Academy, where you'll be able to advance your career with apprenticeship and develop your career within our Brands.
The Copthorne Tara Hotel London Kensington is seeking Casual Night Receptionist who will be responsible for:
To accurately register guests into the hotel, as well as check guests out of the hotel, making provisions for when the system is down for night audit, maintaining records of transactions to be posted onto the live system.
To efficiently respond to any guest enquiries and by communicating effectively to colleagues and superiors.
To assist the Night Shift Leader by undertaking miscellaneous additional tasks, for example, checking market codes, passport numbers and nationality
Provide excellent customer service by ensuring that guests’ first impressions are of a courteous and professional Reception team.
To be fully cognisant with the hotel computer system and apply the knowledge in the most appropriate manner according to the guest requirements.
To have full knowledge of Hotel room rates and policies as applied to guest, and to actively earn revenue for the hotel
To process non-arrival reservations, researching the booking to make sure it is accurate in all respect.
To be familiar with the Hotel door key system, to issue accurately to guests, ensuring keys are cancelled where necessary; and to liaise with Security if guests have problems when accessing rooms.
Key Information:
Role: Casual Night Receptionist
Salary: £12.21 per hour
Working hours; Flexible
Address: Copthorne Tara Hotel, Kensington Hight Street, London, W8 5SY
Requirements:
Experience with Opera Cloud/ Opera preferred.
Confidence in guest interactions
Large-scale hotel experience is advantageous.
Strong communication skills, flexibility, and ability to remain calm under pressure.
Capacity to complete tasks within deadlines and excellent organizational skills
Excellent organisational skills and the ability to multitask and work under pressure
We Offer:
Social Events, Wellbeing and Team Activities
Training and development
Cash Health Plan cover option available
Apprenticeship/Placement opportunities
Career development and salary reviews
Interest free Season Ticket Loan Scheme
Uniform (Laundry / Dry Cleaning for Uniform)
Meals Provided on Duty
Length of Service related holiday scheme
Eye Care Vouchers
Employee Discounted Accommodation and F&B
Life Assurance
About the Hotel
The Copthorne Tara Hotel London Kensington is an elegant contemporary 4-star hotel in prestigious Kensington, located two minutes’ walk to High Street Kensington Underground station, making exploring easy. The hotel offers well-appointed and comfortable guest rooms combining Standard, Superior and Club accommodation. Club rooms offer iconic views over the city and include Club Lounge access for complimentary breakfast and refreshments.
Guests can sample the authentic Singaporean, Malaysian and Chinese cuisine at Bugis Street, traditional pub fare at the Brasserie Restaurant & Bar or relax in our tea lounge.
About the Group:
Millennium Hotels and Resorts owns and operates a portfolio of over 120 hotels worldwide. Properties under the group are placed into four collections: Leng’s Collection, M Social Collection, Millennium Collection, Copthorne Collection and The Biltmore Mayfair. Each collection has its own distinct personality, character and clearly defined hotel brands within it; tailored to suit different types of guests.

title: Supplier Quality Engineer
description_text:
Job Title: Group Supplier Quality EngineerLocation: Farnborough
Our Vision & Why It Matters
At Alexander Dennis, our vision is to lead the evolution of sustainable on-road mass transportation and mobility.
We design, deliver, and support market-leading buses and coaches that help reduce emissions, improve air quality, and keep communities connected. Every role contributes to that mission, whether you’re engineering, driving sales, improving production, or supporting our customers.
What You’ll Do
As Group Supplier Quality Engineer, you’ll lead supplier concern investigations, manage supplier meetings, and validate supplier parts to ensure quality standards are met. You’ll qualify new suppliers, conduct audits, and drive continuous improvement initiatives across the supply base. You’ll be a key player in crisis management, supporting in-service and in-factory quality incidents, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to resolve issues and implement corrective actions.
You’ll also support reliability investigations, prepare performance data for supplier reviews, and lead RCCA and 8D activities. Your role will be instrumental in improving supplier process capability and ensuring consistent quality across all supplied components.
What We’re Looking For
We’re looking for a quality-focused engineer with experience in QA auditing within an automotive environment. You’ll have a strong understanding of ISO9001, IATF 16949, and supplier quality tools such as SPC, RCCA, PPAP, 8D, 5S, and FMEA. You’ll bring excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills, along with the ability to write clear and concise reports.
Bonus if you have:
HND/Degree in Mechanical, Electrical, or Quality Engineering
CQI certifications
Experience with APQP and QlikSense reporting tools
What We Offer
Because we know that to deliver on our vision, people have to feel supported and inspired:
Impact: Be part of something that’s changing cities, improving air quality, helping public transport evolve.
Development & Growth: Training programmes, mentorship, opportunities to take on new roles, broaden skills.
Culture & Belonging: An inclusive environment; your voice matters; we treat people fairly.
Perks & Benefits: Competitive salary, pension, wellbeing support, cycle to work scheme, enhanced maternity and paternity benefits.
Our Values – What Guides Us
These are more than words on a page, they shape how we work, what we stand for, and how we build trust with our customers, communities, and each other.
Safety: The health and wellbeing of our team members, and the safety of our products, are our top priority.
Quality: We strive for excellence in our products, services, and all that we do.
Integrity: We act with honesty, transparency and integrity, treating each other with respect in a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace.
Accountability: We take responsibility for our actions, seeking to build trust and earn a reputation for excellence and reliability.
Teamwork: We work with our team members, our supplier partners, and our customers to pursue mutual benefits.
Sustainability: We seek long-term success for our business, our communities, and the environment through responsible sourcing, lean manufacturing, and sustainable operations.
How to Apply
If you see yourself growing with Alexander Dennis and contributing to our vision, we’d love to hear from you.
Please complete our online application form and attach your CV!
We care deeply about being inclusive and we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
If you are an internal applicant, you have a responsibility to inform your current line/department manager and local human resources representative before applying for the role.
Due to the volume of vacancies and applications, we would prefer that you submit your application online. If however, you require an alternative method of applying, please give us a call on +44 1324 621 672 or send an email to careers@alexander-dennis.com

title: VP of HR, Compliance & Organizational Development
counties_derived: Lower Connecticut River Valley Planning Region
description_text:
POSITION SUMMARY:
The Vice President of Human Resources, Compliance and Organizational Development provides leadership and direction for human resources, compliance, organizational development and risk management functions. As a key member of the senior leadership team, the VP ensures that people practices, policies, and compliance frameworks align with the organization’s mission, values, and compliance obligations. The VP leads enterprise frameworks, monitoring, and escalation for HR and compliance matters, partnering with executive leadership to support timely decision-making and organizational accountability.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
Oversee HR functions, including:
Talent acquisition and retention
Performance management and succession planning
Compensation and benefits
Employee relations and engagement
Learning, leadership development, and training
Ensure HR policies and practices promote employee well-being and align with applicable laws, regulations, and internal standards.
Foster a positive, professional, mission-driven workplace culture
Lead the organization’s compliance program, by establishing oversight frameworks, monitoring, and escalation processes to support adherence to employment and labor laws, nonprofit regulatory requirements (including federal and state funding), ethical standards, and internal policies. Oversee compliance investigations and related reporting in partnership with executive leadership and legal counsel as appropriate; escalate findings and recommended corrective actions for review and decision-making. Partner with legal counsel, auditors, and regulators as needed, and advise the President & CEO on risk-based priorities and response strategy.
Identify, assess, and escalate organizational risk trends; recommend mitigation strategies and strengthen controls in partnership with operational leaders. Co-chair the Employee Engagement, Compliance and Safety Committees
Prepare quarterly reports on workforce metrics, compliance status, and risk trends for the President & CEO and senior leadership, and support Board reporting as requested.
Lead and mentor the HR and compliance teams, and provide strategic oversight and operational support to the Solutions training business as service lines evolve, in alignment with organizational priorities. Promote cross-functional collaboration with finance, programs, and operations
EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

Qualifications Required
Bachelor’s degree in human resources, Business Administration
Minimum of 10 years of progressive HR and Compliance leadership experience
Demonstrated expertise in HR, compliance, organizational development and risk management
Strong knowledge of employment law, nonprofit regulatory requirements, federal and state funding requirements, compliance and practice
Proven ability to lead complex organizations through change with a positive, approachable leadership style
Experience in the nonprofit, public, and mission-driven sector

Preferred
Master’s degree 
HR and/or compliance certifications (SHRM-SCP, SPHR, CCP)

Core Competencies 
Ethical judgment and integrity
Strategic thinking and execution
Strong interpersonal and communication skills
Positive and approachable leadership style
Ability to balance mission impact with regulatory responsibility
Professional with excellent customer service skills
ai_core_responsibilities: The VP of HR oversees HR functions and leads the organization's compliance program, ensuring alignment with laws and internal standards. They also prepare reports on workforce metrics and partner with executive leadership on risk management.
ai_requirements_summary: A bachelor's degree in human resources or business administration is required, along with a minimum of 10 years of progressive HR and compliance leadership experience. Preferred qualifications include a master's degree and HR/compliance certifications.

Advanced Behavioral Health (ABH) is a private, non-profit organization driven by our mission to advance the recovery, health, and well-being of individuals, families and communities.  Our vision is to be a trusted partner to state and federal agencies, providers, and payers, utilizing cutting-edge technology and clinical “best practices” to achieve excellence in program operations and outcomes.  We are guided by our values of teamwork with inclusion, integrity with respect, passion with accountability, and stewardship with excellence. 
  
Among our comprehensive range of services and programs, ABH, through its affiliate Solutions EAP, is a leading provider of employee assistance programs, services, trainings, leadership development, and crisis management throughout the Northeast and beyond.  We are addressing the opioid epidemic with innovative opioid-sparing strategies and services. title: SALES EXECUTIVE • NETWORKING SOLUTIONS
description_text:
Teltonika’s networking solutions business unit develops and manufactures industrial and enterprise connectivity equipment, primarily routers, switches, gateways, and modems. Our networking devices serve clients in the most complex areas of Industry 4.0, Smart City, and Green Energy. 
We are looking to recruit talented SALES EXECUTIVES to join our newly formed local German sales team, operating out of our new office in Munich. We seek to expand our already significant German market traction by growing our existing customer base and attracting new clients and channels into this market. 
In this role, you will:
Contribute to market research, including identifying market potential and finding opportunities for growth.
Generate sales with new clients through cold calling and networking.
Build and maintain long-term relationships with new and existing customers through daily contact, presentations, and frequent visits.
Prepare commercial offers and ensure contracts to achieve assigned sales goals.
Attend exhibitions/meetings to represent Teltonika Networks and its products with solutions.
Building and maintaining a CRM database.
Collaborate with the technical support department and product specialists to address customer requirements.
What we expect of you:
Confident B2B sales presence in Germany's market.
It’s not essential but having some strong connections in a relevant industry already allows you to quickly develop potential interest, with the confidence that you have an exceptional product behind you.
Strong, professional communication and presentation skills, with excellent German and English language skills (both written and spoken).
A consultative approach to doing business, understanding the clients’ needs and pain points, with an ability to show how our solution will resolve both.
Full German Driving license.
What we offer:
A very competitive salary which is based on experience. We will pay the right salary for the right individual.
A competitive commission structure, which is uncapped and based on deal income.
Professional development through our career pathways.
Place where curiosity is key: here, we share knowledge and learn in training courses.
Dynamic, challenging work environment and cooperation-based internal culture.
Salary: 
We appreciate everyone's efforts and experiences, so we offer an attractive base salary that will depend on your skillset and expertise in the field. We also have a competitive commission structure.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Executive will contribute to market research, generate sales with new clients, and build long-term relationships with customers. They will also prepare commercial offers and represent the company at exhibitions and meetings.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a confident B2B sales presence in the German market and strong communication skills in both German and English. A consultative approach to understanding clients' needs and a full German driving license are also expected.

Through long-term experience and research and development of industrial network devices for IoT and M2M communication, we have developed a wide portfolio of products for the most complex areas of Industry 4.0, Smart City, and Green Energy.

title: Freelance Statistics Expert with Python Expertise - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, Statsmodels, and Scikit-learn); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for statisticians with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Statistics or related fields, e.g. Probability Theory, Mathematical Statistics, Applied Statistics, etc. 
3+ years of professional mathematics experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PStat, CAP, SAS Certifications) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $11/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice and evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints. They will also validate calculations using Python libraries and improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be statisticians with professional experience in Python, ideally holding a degree in Statistics or related quantitative fields, and possessing 3+ years of professional mathematics experience. Strong written English proficiency (C1/C2) and a stable internet connection are also required.

title: Marketing Manager - Southern Europe (MATERNITY COVER)
locations_alt_raw: Milan, Metropolitan City of Milan, Italy
description_text:
A fabulous opportunity to join an incredible luxury hospitality brand and an amazing Marketing Team representing the most stunning properties located in Southern Europe (Italy, Spain, and Portugal).
Scope and Purpose
As Marketing Manager for Southern Europe, you will contribute to implement the marketing strategy for Belmond properties across the division. Working alongside the Divisional Brand & Marketing Director, you will focus on brand awareness and excellence, ensuring that all campaigns, editorial projects, and partnerships are executed with precision and drive engagement and conversion.
Key Responsibilities
Execute the annual marketing plan, ensuring all projects remain on track, within budget, and aligned with luxury standards.
Support gold, silver, and local campaigns, ensuring seamless alignment between all internal and external stakeholders.
Oversee the creation of new brand collateral and assist in the production of photo and video shoots while managing regional image libraries.
Update hotel websites with relevant content and monitor performance to suggest actions that enhance visibility and conversion.
Develop innovative ideas and content for electronic and direct mailings targeting both consumers and the travel trade.
Ensure a consistent brand voice and aesthetic across all marketing materials, social media messages, and editorial projects.
Proactively develop new projects and partnerships that elevate brand awareness and excellence.
Maintain regular communication with General Managers, PR Managers, and operational teams to support overall business strategies.
Collaborate with the head office to roll out global brand programs and initiatives at a local or regional level.
Work closely with Sales, Groups, and other departments in Milan to align on divisional projects and initiatives.
Previous experience managing a brand marketing strategy within the luxury sector, including graphic and visual oversight.
Strong visual and creative skills with the ability to understand art direction and evaluate on-brand content.
A deep understanding of luxury brand standards in terms of visuals, copy, and the overall client experience.
Knowledge of CMS, email marketing systems, website management, and online digital channels.
Exceptional organizational skills with the ability to manage complex details and meet concurrent deadlines under pressure.
Effective interpersonal and communication skills to influence stakeholders and clearly express ideas in English and Italian.
Competency in the Microsoft Office suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word).
What We Offer 
At Belmond we pride ourselves on fostering a culture built on kindness, active listening, and genuine connections with our colleagues and guests. We offer a comprehensive range of compensation, perks and benefits including: 
Temporary (Maternity cover) contract within CCNL Turismo Federalberghi
Competitive salary and life insurance plan.
Complimentary and preferred rate experiences at our iconic destinations.
Harvard Management Online Training.
Corporate Belmond and LVMH Benefits.
Meals vouchers. 
We strive to create an environment where our employees can thrive both personally and professionally. With a commitment to your ongoing development, you'll have the opportunity to learn and grow alongside some of the most talented individuals in the industry. Join us and be part of creating unforgettable moments for our guests, communities, and each other. 
The Belmond & LVMH Family 
Belmond is proud to be part of LVMH, and the Belmond global collection of iconic hotels, trains and river cruises, all creating exceptional experiences worldwide. When you join one of our properties, you join a global family of history-rich hotels and breathtaking destinations, where our employees are at the heart of the Belmond experience.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Marketing Manager will execute the annual marketing plan, ensuring projects are on track and aligned with luxury standards. They will also oversee the creation of brand collateral and develop innovative marketing content.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have previous experience managing brand marketing strategies in the luxury sector and possess strong visual and creative skills. A deep understanding of luxury brand standards and effective communication skills in English and Italian are also required.
linkedin_org_locations: London, W1W 7NY, GB
linkedin_org_description: Belmond was born from a passion for connecting discerning travellers with the world’s most remarkable properties, locations and journeys. From hotels and trains to river cruises and safaris, Belmond curates incomparable experiences and crafts unforgettable stories. With a legacy spanning over 45 years, since the acquisition of Hotel Cipriani in Venice in 1976, Belmond has grown into a global collection of 43 properties spread across 24 countries and territories. Exceptional destinations connected by legendary journeys are the very soul of Belmond, where the path that brings you to a place is as important as the destination itself. A pioneer of slow travel, Belmond has been operating the Venice Simplon-Orient-Express since 1982. The group later expanded to include pristine beach resorts, such as Maroma on the Riviera Maya, historic hideaways such as Villa San Michele in the Florentine foothills, urbane icons, such as Copacabana Palace in Rio de Janeiro, and gateways to UNESCO world heritage sites, such as Hotel das Cataratas in Brazil’s Iguassu National Park. As proud custodians of storied properties, Belmond is committed to building on the past to create a new legacy: the heritage of the future. Working with communities and local talents, together with the world's most respected chefs, designers and artists, Belmond continues its purpose of perpetuating the legendary art of travel. Since 2019, Belmond has been part of the world’s leading luxury group, LVMH (Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton).

title: Junior Inbound Travel Consultant
description_text:
Planet Africa Tours’ Inbound Division offers destination management services to the Japanese market, operating tours across Southern and East Africa. Our expert team has a wealth of knowledge and the connections to deliver an exceptional experience for any travelers visiting from Japan. A combination of local knowledge and an understanding and appreciation of the requirements of the Japanese traveler has made Planet Africa Tours the largest Southern African operator in the Japanese market. 
The Junior Reservations Consultant is responsible for processing FIT and small group enquiries from quotation to finalization, invoicing, and preparing travel documentation, whilst gaining the required knowledge and skills in the field of Inbound Travel. 
Key Tasks include, but are not limited to:
Assist with preparing travel quotations and itineraries.
Load and update bookings accurately and promptly in the UDMS.
Confirm bookings with suppliers (accommodation, transport, guides, activities, meet-and-greet, restaurants, special services).
Manage release dates of services booked.
Address account queries within set deadlines.
Communicate professionally with suppliers to request availability, confirm rates and services, and obtain booking confirmation. 
Provide professional and timely communication with customers and respond to queries within the required turnaround time.
Assist with issuing travel documentation, vouchers, and final itineraries.
Assist with amendments, cancellations, and re-bookings.
Support emergency or after-hours operational requirements when required.
EDUCATION: Grade 12 essential. A related tertiary qualification in Travel and Tourism is highly preferable.
EXPERIENCE: Related experience in the Inbound Tourism, reservations, or tourism administration; exposure to reservations systems or travel booking platforms; relevant experience with the Japanese market will be highly beneficial.
KNOWLEDGE: Basic knowledge of travel products, destinations, and logistics in South and Southern Africa.
SKILLS: Computer and Systems Skills (UDMS, Microsoft Office/Word/Excel/Outlook/SharePoint; Strong administrative & organization; high attention to detail; good verbal and written communication skills; ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
ATTRIBUTES: Customer service orientated; willingness to learn and develop; team player; ability to work under pressure; professional and reliable.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Junior Reservations Consultant is primarily responsible for managing FIT and small group travel enquiries from initial quotation through to finalization, invoicing, and preparing necessary travel documentation. This role involves confirming bookings with various suppliers, managing service release dates, and addressing customer queries promptly.
ai_requirements_summary: Essential requirements include a Grade 12 qualification, with a tertiary qualification in Travel and Tourism being highly preferable. Candidates should possess related experience in inbound tourism or reservations, familiarity with booking systems, and basic knowledge of Southern African travel products, while experience with the Japanese market is a significant advantage.
linkedin_org_slogan: We pride ourselves in being South Africa’s most reputable family leisure travel brand with a history of excellence.
linkedin_org_locations: Chiapinni Square, Mechau Street, Cape Town, Western Cape 8001, ZA
linkedin_org_description:
Pentravel was established in 1983 and has for several years been part of the Cullinan Holdings group, a listed company specialising in travel and tourism companies in such areas as hospitality, tour operating, corporate retail travel and leisure travel.

With 20 shops in SA and expert product and destination knowledge, Pentravel is your trusted leisure travel partner. In addition to expertise in selling local, regional and international airfares, the retail travel group has a strong land product strategy in conjunction with key preferred suppliers worldwide, as well as the ability and expertise to package tailor-made breaks based on client preference and budget. From hotel accommodation and resort packages, to coach tours, ski and cruises, Pentravel offers more than a travel consulting service. Pentravel are the leisure travel experts. Foreign exchange, travel insurance and visa services are also provided.

At Pentravel we like to sell the difference; focusing on personal service, great advice and excellent value. We have a high rate of repeat and referral business and believe that knowledge and personal experience add value to your own experience. And it would be our privilege to guide them. Your holiday begins with us!

title: Commodity Operations - Metals
description_text:
About Javelin
Javelin Global Commodities is a leading global commodities marketing, trading, logistics services and investment firm with head offices in London and wider global offices in New York, Dallas, San Diego, St Louis, Canada, Switzerland, Poland, Dubai, India, Singapore, China and Australia. Javelin was founded in 2015 and now operates across six continents with respect to more than 20 different commodities across thermal coal, metallurgical coal, iron ore, steel scrap, metals, hydrocarbons, freight, agriculture, and renewables.
Established in 2015, Javelin employs over 200 people and is a unique place to grow your career. We take pride in our values, global reach, meritocratic culture and strong work ethic. Our success over the years has been built on our guiding principles of customer service, creativity, transparency, integrity, and responsibility. 
Whilst our footprint grows, we still foster a small-company atmosphere. We have a flat company structure and encourage collaboration across all seniority levels, teams and locations to generate the best ideas. Javelin has a dedicated strategy to support local and meaningful charities through volunteer days, donation matching, a ‘give as you earn’ scheme and other fundraising activities. We are committed to the wellbeing of our colleagues and organise various social evenings, sports clubs and lunchtime activities. Our London office has on-site gym facilities and a golf/sports simulator. 
The Role
The Operations team at Javelin manages the logistics of commodity products within Javelin. Working with the traders, finance, technology & legal, documents and settlements, operations ensure that risk is captured accurately and deliveries are made to customers as per the terms of our trades.
As the business continues to expand and grow, we are looking for a Logistics Senior Analyst or Associate (title subject to level of experience) to join the metals team in London. Dynamic and always challenging, this role will complement the responsibilities of the other team members and work as part of the global team to achieve the Operation Group’s objectives.
Key Responsibilities Include:
Responsible for physical operation for refined metals & metals concentrates in relation to Javelin’s domestic and international commodities businesses
Make sure scheduling systems are up to date and accurately reflect positions/inventory
Be responsible for documentation of cargos and prepare invoices on a timely basis
Take responsibility for reporting of programmes and respond to ad-hoc queries from the trading desk
Liaise with documentation team, and legal team to make sure contracts are accurate from a logistics perspective
End of month reporting – including stockpile reconciliation/management
Building relationships with clients/ports/warehouse & transport providers.
Prior experience managing physical base metal contracts 
Knowledge and understanding of title transfer documents and Incoterms
Experience with physical inventory management and supply chain optimisation/planning
Experience in container ocean freight import and export operations
A strong work ethic is required to learn on the job under guidance from senior team members
Can confidently working independently to manage daily tasks and report to the trading desk
Ability to work collaboratively across teams and geographic locations
Clear communication skills, both written and verbal
Ability to manage and prioritise workload across time zones and judge priority and urgency of requests and issues accordingly
Excellent attention to detail and ability to work well under pressure.
Annual discretionary bonus 
25 days holiday
Company pension scheme (10% company contribution)
Private medical scheme with a range of additional wellbeing benefits
Travel insurance
Income protection
Life insurance (5x salary)
Bike2Work scheme including secure storage and showers
Season ticket loan
Onsite gym.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves managing the logistics of refined metals and metals concentrates, ensuring accurate scheduling and documentation. Additionally, the position requires reporting and liaising with various teams to ensure smooth operations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have prior experience managing physical base metal contracts and a strong understanding of title transfer documents and Incoterms. The role also requires excellent communication skills and the ability to work independently and collaboratively.
linkedin_org_locations: 27 Eccleston Place, London, England SW1W 9NF, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Javelin Global Commodities is a global commodities marketing, trading, logistics, financing, and advisory company focused on bulk, steel, energy, green carbon, renewable fuels and soft commodities.
Javelin has unparalleled risk management expertise, extensive global network and broad market insight. We are forward thinking, dynamic and place the needs of our clients first. Our team at Javelin is unequivocally 'Moving Commodities Forward'.

Javelin was founded in 2015 by a team dedicated to deliver new, innovative, customer-led solutions to buy and sell commodities. 

Headquartered in London with offices in New York, San Diego, St. Louis, Puerto Rico, Bangalore, Zug, Singapore and Melbourne, we are uniquely positioned to provide superior solutions to our clients globally. 
Javelin operates across 100 countries and six continents with respect to more than 20 different commodities, where we lead the market in supplying commodities to the energy, steel and industrial sectors.

title: Supplier Quality Engineer
description_text:
Job Title: Group Supplier Quality EngineerLocation: Skelmersdale 
Our Vision & Why It Matters
At Alexander Dennis, our vision is to lead the evolution of sustainable on-road mass transportation and mobility.
We design, deliver, and support market-leading buses and coaches that help reduce emissions, improve air quality, and keep communities connected. Every role contributes to that mission, whether you’re engineering, driving sales, improving production, or supporting our customers.
What You’ll Do
As Group Supplier Quality Engineer, you’ll lead supplier concern investigations, manage supplier meetings, and validate supplier parts to ensure quality standards are met. You’ll qualify new suppliers, conduct audits, and drive continuous improvement initiatives across the supply base. You’ll be a key player in crisis management, supporting in-service and in-factory quality incidents, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to resolve issues and implement corrective actions.
You’ll also support reliability investigations, prepare performance data for supplier reviews, and lead RCCA and 8D activities. Your role will be instrumental in improving supplier process capability and ensuring consistent quality across all supplied components.
What We’re Looking For
We’re looking for a quality-focused engineer with experience in QA auditing within an automotive environment. You’ll have a strong understanding of ISO9001, IATF 16949, and supplier quality tools such as SPC, RCCA, PPAP, 8D, 5S, and FMEA. You’ll bring excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills, along with the ability to write clear and concise reports.
Bonus if you have:
HND/Degree in Mechanical, Electrical, or Quality Engineering
CQI certifications
Experience with APQP and QlikSense reporting tools
What We Offer
Because we know that to deliver on our vision, people have to feel supported and inspired:
Impact: Be part of something that’s changing cities, improving air quality, helping public transport evolve.
Development & Growth: Training programmes, mentorship, opportunities to take on new roles, broaden skills.
Culture & Belonging: An inclusive environment; your voice matters; we treat people fairly.
Perks & Benefits: Competitive salary, pension, wellbeing support, cycle to work scheme, enhanced maternity and paternity benefits.
Our Values – What Guides Us
These are more than words on a page, they shape how we work, what we stand for, and how we build trust with our customers, communities, and each other.
Safety: The health and wellbeing of our team members, and the safety of our products, are our top priority.
Quality: We strive for excellence in our products, services, and all that we do.
Integrity: We act with honesty, transparency and integrity, treating each other with respect in a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace.
Accountability: We take responsibility for our actions, seeking to build trust and earn a reputation for excellence and reliability.
Teamwork: We work with our team members, our supplier partners, and our customers to pursue mutual benefits.
Sustainability: We seek long-term success for our business, our communities, and the environment through responsible sourcing, lean manufacturing, and sustainable operations.
How to Apply
If you see yourself growing with Alexander Dennis and contributing to our vision, we’d love to hear from you.
Please complete our online application form and attach your CV!
We care deeply about being inclusive and we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
If you are an internal applicant, you have a responsibility to inform your current line/department manager and local human resources representative before applying for the role.
Due to the volume of vacancies and applications, we would prefer that you submit your application online. If however, you require an alternative method of applying, please give us a call on +44 1324 621 672 or send an email to careers@alexander-dennis.com
ai_core_responsibilities: The Group Supplier Quality Engineer will lead supplier concern investigations, manage supplier meetings, and validate supplied parts to ensure quality standards are met. This role involves qualifying new suppliers, conducting audits, driving continuous improvement, and supporting quality incident management across the supply base.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate is a quality-focused engineer experienced in QA auditing within an automotive environment, possessing strong knowledge of ISO9001, IATF 16949, and key supplier quality tools like RCCA, PPAP, and 8D. Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills are essential for resolving issues and implementing corrective actions.

title: Promoter | Kiosk
description_text:
The Real Estate Promoter is responsible for promoting properties, generating leads, and converting prospects into potential buyers. The role involves direct customer interaction, site visits, marketing support, and coordination with the sales team to achieve sales targets.
Key Responsibilities
Promote real estate projects to prospective customers through site visits, events, and direct interaction 
Explain property features, pricing, payment plans, and offers clearly to clients 
Generate leads through walk-ins, referrals and marketing campaigns 
Maintain accurate customer records and follow up with potential buyers 
Coordinate with sales managers and marketing teams to close deals 
Assist in organising promotional events, exhibitions, and property launches 
Provide market feedback and customer insights to management 
Ensure a high level of customer satisfaction and professional conduct 
Proven sales experience; secondary market real estate experience preferred
Strong negotiation, communication, and presentation skills 
Goal-oriented, self-motivated, and able to work independently
Ability to build long-term relationships with clients and investors
Unlimited Earning Potential with our Competitive Commission Structure
Rewards for Top Performers – Recognition and incentives for your outstanding achievements.
Career Growth & Progression – Clear paths to senior roles and leadership opportunities.
Employment Benefits – Full employment visa and medical insurance.

Fresh Leads Provided – Start your sales journey with pre-qualified client leads.
Exclusive Property Portfolio – Access to all developers and Dubai’s most lucrative off-plan projects.
Professional Training related to the Secondary Market.
Prime Office Location in Dubai Marina – Modern, centrally located office with a vibrant work environment.
Be Part of the Best – Join a top-performing team in one of Dubai’s top 10 real estate agencies and take your career to the next level.
Ready to take your real estate career to new heights! Apply now and become a key player in Dubai’s booming secondary Market property market!

title: Purchasing Intern
locations_alt_raw: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
locations_derived: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description_text:
Employment Type: Intern 
Division: Purchasing 
Department: Purchasing 
Salary Range: $23-$28/hr
The Purchasing Intern supports the Purchasing team in managing bids, evaluating cost comparisons, tracking budgets, issuing purchase orders, and maintaining vendor relationships. This role provides hands-on experience in subcontractor prequalification, bid analysis, and contract management.
Responsibilities / Essential Functions 
Prequalify subcontractors and vendors. 
Evaluate cost estimates and pricing proposals. 
Solicit bids from qualified vendors and subcontractors. 
Prepare and issue complete bid packages. 
Review, evaluate, and de-scope submitted bids. 
Award material purchase agreements (MPAs) and subcontractor packages. 
Draft subcontracts and MPAs in alignment with project requirements. 
Support timely and accurate project buyouts. 
Identify appropriate bid lists for each scope of work. 
Verify subcontractor insurance coverage, bonding capabilities, and EMR (Experience Modification Rate) before contract award. 
Perform other tasks as assigned by the team.
Key Skills 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Strong initiative and problem-solving abilities. 
Exceptional attention to detail. 
Effective time management with the ability to handle multiple responsibilities and meet deadlines.
Required Experience 
Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Engineering, or a related field. 
Preference given to students studying Civil Engineering or other construction-related disciplines. 
Demonstrated interest in construction procurement and the building industry.

Consigli Construction Co., Inc. is a leading construction manager in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic. Consigli services clients across all markets, including academic, healthcare, life sciences, institutional, energy, corporate and federal.

title: Freelance Senior Product Designer (Contract - 3 Months)
description_text:
Freelance Senior Product Designer - Start date beginning of March
We’re looking for a passionate, collaborative Senior Product Designer to temporarily join our two SaaS design teams and take a hands-on role in problem-solving and design execution. Treatwell’s SaaS teams focus on the business tools salons use to run their operations across our web and app platforms.
In this role, you’ll be actively designing across Treatwell’s end-to-end user journeys, contributing directly throughout the full design process - from research and ideation to prototyping, testing, and implementation. You’ll work day-to-day with the SaaS Design Lead, product managers, and engineers in an internationally distributed team, turning complex problems into clear, high-quality design solutions.
Above all, we’re looking for a proactive, organised self-starter who can hit the ground running from day one.
You will be responsible for: 
Collaborating with other designers, user researchers, product managers, and engineers to plan, design, build and evolve our products.
Influencing strategy for a product area by articulating a coherent UX vision based on clear, actionable problem / opportunity statements.
Advocating for the user and crafting UX solutions that meet critical business objectives.
Working on a range of projects on different platforms.
Helping facilitate idea generation with stakeholder.
Designing with scalability in mind and actively contributing to our design system.
Creating simple, intuitive experiences in a space with complex rules and workflows.
Consistently creating clear and compelling materials and narratives explaining design rationale to team and stakeholders using customer, business or competitive insights.
Actively seeking diverse feedback on your work and anticipating feedback to address it proactively.
About you: 
Detail-oriented and passionate about the craft
5+ years as a Product or UX Designer (or equivalent experience) with robust case studies that showcase deep interaction and problem-solving skills
Experience creating and using design systems
Experience working in a scale-up environment
Strong communication (both written and verbal) skills
Excellent understanding of design principles
An ego-free attitude that welcomes candid feedback
A willingness to fail and learn and to go beyond good enough
About Treatwell
Hi, we’re Treatwell. Nice to meet you.
We’re a team of 700 passionate and brilliantly talented people across Europe. We’re the industry’s smartest salon management software, all connected to Europe’s leading bookings platform, working with over 50,000 salon partners across 13 countries, processing over 8 million bookings every month. And….breathe.
But we’re not done yet. We’re on an exciting journey to digitally revolutionise the hair and beauty community.
If this excites you, we might just have the role for you.
We'll treat you with
A challenging role at one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe, with a fun company culture in the heart of Amsterdam.
Mental health support through our partnership with OpenUp
A delicious freshly prepared office lunch each day, available for a small contribution
Equal Ops
We are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to their condition.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Product Designer will collaborate with various teams to design and evolve products, influencing strategy and advocating for user needs. They will work on multiple projects, creating intuitive experiences while contributing to the design system.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of experience in product or UX design with strong communication skills and a detail-oriented approach. Experience in a scale-up environment and familiarity with design systems are also required.

linkedin_org_description:
At Treatwell, we empower industry growth to bring more beauty and wellness to everyone, everywhere. As Europe’s No.1 beauty and wellness booking platform, we connect millions of people with salons and professionals across 13 countries. With the industry’s smartest salon management software at our core, we help beauty and wellness professionals grow their business while making treatments more accessible than ever.

Our vision is simple: to create a world where everyone can treat themselves well. That means giving salons the tools, visibility, and support to thrive, while making it effortless for customers to book the treatments they love. Anytime, anywhere.

Behind it all is a team of passionate, ambitious people united by our values: Treat everyone well, Think like a salon owner, Make every appointment count, and Play to win together. These values shape how we work, drive our growth, and keep us focused on building a company that truly makes a difference for our people, partners, and industry.

Ready to be part of the journey? Explore our careers page, your next chapter could start here.

title: Marketing Manager - Subscriptions, 12 Month FTC
description_text:
The Subscriptions Marketing team are responsible for all direct-to-consumer marketing, promoting Future's portfolio of print and digital specialist titles to both existing and potential customers. The overall objective of the team is to sustainably increase subscription revenues through optimised marketing campaigns via a variety of online and offline channels.
What you'll be doing
Reporting to the Senior Marketing Manager, the Marketing Manager will operate in a strategic capacity. You will devise and deliver an effective plan that owns and drives decision-marketing for designated vertical acquisition performance, marketing trends, customer behaviours, pricing, previous performance, positioning, and CPA.
You will prioritise and co-ordinate workload effectively for yourself and your reports. You'll be accountable for best-practice and accurate execution and delivery of acquisition campaigns within your designated verticals, and across the wider portfolio.
You will also required to develop 3rd party opportunities to acquire new subscribers, to build/maintain working relationships with existing providers and vendors, and to actively explore new areas of opportunity.
Experience that will put you ahead of the curve
At least 4 years of experience in marketing and campaign management
Direct report management experience
Subscriptions marketing experience or marketing qualification
Management experience
Experience with campaign analysis
Budget management
Numerate and commercially aware (profitability, ROI, ARPU, LTV)
Previous experience using AI tools to increase productivity
What's in it for you
The expected range for this role is £30,000 - £42,000
This is a Hybrid role from our Bath Office, working three days from the office, two from home
… Plus more great perks, which include;
Uncapped leave, because we trust you to manage your workload and time
When we hit our targets, enjoy a share of our profits with a bonus
Refer a friend and get rewarded when they join Future
Well-being support with access to our Colleague Assistant Programmes
Opportunity to purchase shares in Future, with our Share Incentive Plan
Internal job family level P5
Who are we…
We're Future, the global leader in specialist media. With over 3,000 employees working across 200+ media brands, Future is a prime destination for passionate people worldwide looking to consume trusted, expert content that educates and inspires action - both online and off - through our specialist websites, magazines, events, newsletters, podcasts and social spaces.
We've got ambitious plans that further build on our growth momentum and unlock new opportunities – and we're looking for driven people who want to be a part of it!
Our Future, Our Responsibility - Inclusion and Diversity at Future
We embrace and celebrate diversity, making it part of who we are.
Different perspectives spark ideas, fuel creativity, and push us to innovate. That's why we're building a workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive.
When it comes to hiring, we keep it fair and inclusive, welcoming talent from every walk of life. It's not just about what you bring to the table — it's about making sure the table has room for everyone.
Because a diverse team isn't just good for business. It's the Future.
Find out more about Our Future, Our Responsibility on our website.
Please let us know if you need any reasonable adjustments made so we can give you the best experience!
#LI-Hybrid
#LI-HD1
ai_core_responsibilities: The Marketing Manager will devise and deliver an effective marketing plan to drive acquisition performance and optimize campaigns. They will also develop third-party opportunities to acquire new subscribers and maintain relationships with existing vendors.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least 4 years of experience in marketing and campaign management, with direct report management experience. Subscriptions marketing experience and familiarity with campaign analysis and budget management are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: The global leader in specialist media. Expertly produced original content across 200+ brands.

We are Future. The global platform for specialist media. We create expert original content that connects people to their passions across a portfolio of over 200 phenomenal brands.

Through our websites, magazines, events, and social spaces we reach 1 in 3 adults online in the UK and the US.

Founded in 1985, we have grown significantly to a global business with over 3,000 colleagues. We are the largest magazine publisher in the UK with a combined circulation of more than 3 million across our publications.

We innovate through our proprietary technology and diversify our audiences across new platforms every year. We’ve got an ambitious plan to build on our growth momentum and unlock new opportunities.

title: Freelance Statistics Expert with Python Expertise - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, Statsmodels, and Scikit-learn); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for statisticians with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Statistics or related fields, e.g. Probability Theory, Mathematical Statistics, Applied Statistics, etc. 
3+ years of professional mathematics experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PStat, CAP, SAS Certifications) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $11/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice and evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints. They will also validate calculations using Python libraries and improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be statisticians with professional experience in Python, ideally holding a degree in Statistics or related quantitative fields, and possessing 3+ years of professional mathematics experience. Strong written English proficiency (C1/C2) and a stable internet connection are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

title: Freelance Statistics Expert with Python Expertise - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, Statsmodels, and Scikit-learn); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for statisticians with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Statistics or related fields, e.g. Probability Theory, Mathematical Statistics, Applied Statistics, etc. 
3+ years of professional mathematics experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PStat, CAP, SAS Certifications) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $11/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.

title: GAIN Creative Studio - Videographer Editor
description_text:
Business Unit: Creative Studio: We bring big ideas and content to life, shaping brands that stand out, sparking conversations, and making a real impact.
Team: Production
Reporting to: Production Manager 
Location: Bournemouth / London 
ABOUT:

GAIN is a creative-led, insight-driven company that blends data, tech and creativity. We believe the best ideas emerge where intelligence and creativity unite, where insight sparks imagination, and where innovation turns possibility into progress. 

We are explorers of new frontiers, shaping bold strategies that move people, brands, and businesses forward. Individually and together, our specialist teams provide the vision, data, and confidence brands and organisations need to make braver, more impactful decisions.

Today as GAIN, we work as a united force, using data to fuel creativity, and technology to unlock new possibilities. As imagineers we don’t just embrace innovation – we engineer it, transforming information into action, and ideas into breakthroughs. 

This is where rebel thinking, smart technology, and data-driven creativity shape the future.
Through our five specialist teams: Creative Studio, Conversion, Customer Science, Experience and Performance. Individually, and together, we work to fuel your growth, and deliver measurable impact.
THE ROLE:
The role of a Videographer Editor is to produce exceptional production work that delights our clients and proves effective for their audiences. This role is ideal for someone who lives and breathes film – from shot form social content to campaign led films - and understands how storytelling, pacing and platform-native execution drive engagement.
In a nutshell you:
Manage and oversee assigned video and photography content from start to finish
Help to drive creativity and elevate production output to the next level
Manage and oversee assigned post-production and editing to realise creative vision
RESPONSIBILITIES:
Manage and oversee assigned video and photography production content from start to finish:
Collaborate with client services and lead creative to plan and develop content, specific to client needs including all technical aspects
Advise client services and lead creative from a photo/video perspective prior to filming, to help determine the most achievable outputs
Confidently use all video, lighting, audio and editing equipment/software and be adept at shooting on location and in studio
Assist in troubleshooting and providing technical support for any production related enquiries
Confidently direct cast and crew to ensure all content is captured and produced to the highest quality and to brief
Prioritise workload and proactively manage multiple projects to meet delivery deadlines
Maintain excellent relationships with colleagues across the agency and external stakeholders
Keep production manage/lead creative and client services up-to-date on project progress, as well as any creative or technical needs throughout the course of a project, and flag any concerns or delays promptly
Ensure your timesheets are completed accurately and on time in Productive
Help to drive creativity and elevate production output to the next level:
Use your experience and knowledge of the latest content and shooting techniques to elevate production value and quality on every project
Work collaboratively with others in the team to adapt creative/technical skills to accomplish different outputs, providing feedback, suggestions and new ideas where appropriate
Keep up-to-date with new and relevant techniques, proactively test and refine, and identify opportunities and projects where these new techniques could be incorporated
Be aware of current trends and openly share ideas to enhance the creative output of the team
Manage and oversee assigned post-production and editing to realise creative vision:
Manage the post production workflow from building and supplying selects through to final resizing and delivery of all content within any owned project and to deadline
Advise alternative solutions to amends with lead creative and producer/client service
Manage and maintain ingesting, transferring and quality checking files and footage onto our internal network and server
Ensure project files, RAW footage and assets of completed projects are backed up on internal servers
Adapt video assets for different formats, ratios and placements
Create platform native video for all channels
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Excellent time-management and organisational skills with the ability to work to tight deadlines
Flexible approach with the ability to remain calm and perform well under pressure
The ability to think creatively and support the delivery of clever and innovative concepts
Personable, and able to work effectively with a variety of different people at all levels
A proactive and enthusiastic approach to work
Be involved in creative ideation, concept development and working with key stakeholders
Have an excellent understanding of camera operation, lighting and editing software including BlackMagic camera systems and Adobe Creative Suite
Excellent technical acumen to be able to operate, configure, set up, and fix video, audio, lighting, or other production equipment
Mentor, advise and help guide more junior members of the team
HOW YOU’LL WORK:
Constant curiosity: You can think critically and understand the data, knowing where you need to take action. You’re analytical with the ability to investigate and resolve 
Pushing boundaries: You’ll make the most efficient use of technology, suggesting process improvements 
The power of our imagination: You're a problem solver who goes the extra mile. You’ll know the best approach to take with clients to get results. 
Making it happen: You’re proactive and you know you can make a difference. You use your sound judgement when making decisions and you take responsibility for your decisions and actions. 
Putting people first: You’ll build effective relationships with people, internally and externally, understanding both business and client needs.
Our benefits for UK employees include:
Private Medical Insurance 
BUPA Life Assurance 
Income protection 
Employee Assistance Programme 
Cycle to Work salary sacrifice scheme
Tech & Wearables salary sacrifice scheme
Octopus EV Scheme
Discounts and deals on a range of items from hotels, holidays and hormone testing to cinema, gyms and will writing 

GAIN is an equal opportunities employer and positively encourages applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marital status, or pregnancy and maternity
