title: Direct Care Worker
description_text:
Eminence Home Care is seeking dedicated and compassionate individuals to join our team as Direct Care Workers (DCWs). In this fulfilling role, you will provide essential support to clients who require assistance with daily living activities in their homes and in a facility. Your responsibilities will include helping with personal care, meal preparation, and companionship, all tailored to meet the individual needs of each client. Your contributions will play a key role in enhancing the quality of life for those you care for, making this a truly impactful position.
Key Responsibilities
Assist clients with personal hygiene, grooming, and bathing.
Prepare nutritious meals and assist with feeding when necessary.
Provide companionship and engage clients in social and recreational activities.
Monitor clients’ health and report any changes to the nursing staff.
Ensure a safe and clean living environment for clients.
Support clients with mobility needs and transportation if required.
Experience in a caregiving or direct care role is preferred but not required.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Empathy, patience, and a genuine desire to help others.
Strong ability to work independently as well as part of a team.
Detail-oriented with good organizational skills.
Availability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays.
About Us: 
Our mission is to provide and restore client dignity and independence in their homes through individualized care plans in an effort to reduce caregiver role and stress on family members while avoiding nursing homes and assisted living facilities. 
Eminence Home Care, an affiliate of Ultimate Care, is a licensed home care provider located in Connecticut, Maryland, New Hampshire, New Jersey & Pennsylvania. We offer comprehensive home care services where we lead with our hearts to offer top quality and empathetic home care services.
Why Work for Us?

At Eminence Home Care, we are dedicated to fostering a people-centric culture that is pivotal for success in the contemporary home care landscape. Our unwavering commitment begins with inspiring and empowering our employees to unleash their full potential. We actively pursue avenues to cultivate a dynamic learning environment, allowing our team members to explore and nurture their passions and talents. Our goal is to facilitate holistic growth, both personally and professionally, whether within the office or beyond. Join us on this journey of empowerment and achievement!
At Eminence Home Care and our affiliates, we're committed to creating a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're enthusiastic about the role but don't meet every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply. You could be the ideal candidate for this or other roles!
Eminence Home Care is an equal opportunity employer committed to non-discrimination in hiring, valuing qualifications over factors such as race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, and sexual orientation. Eminence Home Care is dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans to foster an inclusive and accessible work environment. If you require accommodation, please inform us Eminence Home Care does not accept resumes from unsolicited search firms nor recruiters.
EPA123

title: Head of Growth & Business Unit Lead (Early-Stage)
description_text:
The Opportunity
We are seeking a Head of Growth & Business Unit Lead (Early-Stage) to help shape and execute next chapter. This is a senior, hands-on role with meaningful ownership, combining growth execution, go-to-market development, and commercial leadership. You will act as the connective tissue between marketing, product, sales, and engineering, operating with a high-ownership mindset and growing into broader leadership responsibility as the business scales.
This role is based within a very small team. Structured training, hands-on support, and access to shared group resources will be provided, and the role may initially be embedded within another business to provide additional structure and mentorship. As the business grows, the scope, responsibility, and impact of the role will grow with it.
This role is ideal for a SaaS growth leader who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys building systems from first principles, and wants a clear path toward increased ownership over time.
What You’ll Be Doing
Business & Commercial Ownership
Own responsibility for growth performance and commercial outcomes, operating within agreed budgets and with support from senior leadership
Build and execute a milestone-based growth plan aligned with ARR expansion goals
Operate with increasing autonomy while leveraging shared infrastructure, capital, and expertise from the broader group
Go-To-Market & Growth Leadership
Design and execute early-stage GTM initiatives, including positioning, ICP definition, pricing, packaging, and channel strategy, in collaboration with experienced operators across the group
Establish and refine the initial sales motion, working closely with sales leadership and directly with customers
Lead growth initiatives across demand generation, lifecycle marketing, conversion optimisation, and expansion
Support the commercial development of early product verticals or use cases as the business evolves
Product & Technical Collaboration
Act as the primary bridge between product, engineering, marketing, and sales
Partner closely with technical leaders to ensure a strong developer experience, including APIs, integrations, documentation, onboarding, and reliability
Bring market and customer insight directly into product strategy and roadmap prioritisation
Team & Culture
Work hands-on within a small, fast-moving team across growth, marketing, and commercial functions
Lead and develop a small team as the business scales and headcount grows
Set the tone for execution, accountability, and customer-centric decision-making within the business unit
What You Bring
Significant experience in B2B SaaS growth, with exposure across marketing, sales, and product
Experience contributing to the growth or scaling of a SaaS business, product line, or growth function; ideally in an AI, data, or developer-facing product
Strong exposure to product management or a track record of working closely with product and engineering teams
Experience supporting early-stage or 0→1 GTM motions and helping optimise them for growth
Commercial understanding across pricing, packaging, funnels, and customer acquisition
A high-ownership mindset: resourceful, adaptable, and comfortable operating in an early-stage environment with evolving structure
Excellent communication skills, able to move fluidly between technical and non-technical stakeholders
Prior experience mentoring others or taking on informal leadership responsibility
What You’ll Own
Delivery against agreed growth and revenue outcomes
Execution of GTM initiatives across marketing, sales alignment, and product-led growth
Contribution to product-market fit evolution and early commercial scaling across use cases
Increasing ownership of team performance, culture, and execution quality as the business grows
This role offers a rare opportunity to join an AI SaaS business at an early stage, with meaningful responsibility, strong support, and a clear path to broader leadership as the company grows.
#FluentSoftware
ai_core_responsibilities: The Head of Growth & Business Unit Lead will own growth performance and commercial outcomes while executing a milestone-based growth plan. They will also design and execute go-to-market initiatives and collaborate closely with product and engineering teams.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have significant experience in B2B SaaS growth and a strong understanding of product management. A high-ownership mindset and excellent communication skills are essential for success in this role.

Headquartered in Montreal with a global presence, Valsoft is committed to building a legacy of excellence by prioritizing sustainable growth and operational efficiency. Our long-term vision, driven by entrepreneurial spirit and disciplined execution, ensures we create lasting value for our portfolio companies, their customers, and the industries they serve. For more information, visit valsoftcorp.com.

Nous gérons un portefeuille croissant d'entreprises qui bénéficient d'un leadership décentralisé et d'une autonomie opérationnelle. Nous soutenons activement nos entreprises en les accompagnant dans leur croissance et leur évolution en leaders de leur secteur. 

Basée à Montréal et présente à l'échelle mondiale, Valsoft s'engage à bâtir un héritage d'excellence en priorisant la croissance durable et l'efficacité opérationnelle. title: Senior Data Engineer
description_text:
About UBDS
UBDS Group empowers organizations to achieve remarkable digital transformations through innovative technology solutions We work with clients across the public and private sector to design and build products, platforms and services that genuinely make a difference to users.
We’re looking for a Senior Data Engineer who combines deep technical capability with a strong consulting mindset. Someone who enjoys ambiguity, cares about users, and can turn messy business problems into robust, production-grade data solutions.
The Role
As a Senior Data Engineer at UBDS, you’ll work closely with clients, designers, analysts and engineers to shape and deliver modern data solutions end to end. You’ll be trusted to lead technical thinking, make pragmatic design decisions, and support teams in delivering high‑quality outcomes that stand up in the real world.
This is a hands-on role with influence: you’ll be building, guiding others, and helping clients understand what good looks like.
What You’ll Do
Turn ambiguous business problems into clear data, analytical and technical solutions.
Design, build and operate production-grade data pipelines, models and products using SQL, Python and modern data engineering frameworks.
Architect and work across data warehouses, lakehouse and event-driven patterns, including strong semantic models for analytics and visualisation.
Deliver solutions on cloud data and AI platforms (AWS/Azure), applying solid software engineering, testing and CI/CD practices.
Embed analytics, experimentation, AI/ML integration, governance, security and data quality best practices by default.
Create clear data visualisations, narratives and consultant-grade materials that help clients understand and act on insight.
Work closely with users and stakeholders in Agile, client-driven environments, learning from live services and real-world feedback.
Apply strong critical thinking, attention to detail and relevant industry and government standards to deliver solutions that are easy to hand over, maintain and evolve.
What We’re Looking ForCore Data & Technical Skills
Ability to translate ambiguous business problems into clear data, analytical and technical requirements.
Strong SQL and Python skills for data modelling, transformation, automation and analysis.
Experience designing data models and architectures across data warehouses, lakehouse and event-driven patterns, or strong semantic modelling in Power BI or other visualisation tools.
Hands-on experience with modern data engineering frameworks such as Spark / PySpark, Pandas and orchestration tools.
Experience working with cloud data and AI platforms on AWS or Azure (e.g. Databricks, Redshift, Bedrock, Microsoft Fabric).
Solid software engineering fundamentals, including Git, testing and QA, CI/CD pipelines and modular, maintainable design.
Strong analytics and statistical reasoning, including metric design, experimentation and interpretation of results.
Practical AI/ML literacy, including integrating model APIs, evaluation approaches and prompt orchestration.
Experience applying data governance, security and quality best practices, such as access control, data catalogues, lineage and GDPR awareness.
Ability to communicate insight through data visualisation and storytelling using tools like Power BI, Tableau or Python-based charts.
Consulting & Delivery Mindset
A user-centred approach: curiosity about who will use the solution, why it matters, and the impact it will have.
Confidence communicating clearly and working effectively in ambiguous, client-driven environments.
Ability to produce high-quality, consultant-grade slides and written materials.
Strong critical thinking, with a clear and logical approach to defining and delivering data solutions.
Proven experience delivering production-grade solutions to real users, supporting live services and learning from real-world feedback.
High attention to detail, including readable code, consistent naming conventions and clear technical and user documentation.
A product-driven mindset, with experience delivering features and outcomes iteratively in Agile teams.
Familiarity with relevant industry and government standards, and a habit of seeking out appropriate standards at the start of delivery rather than reinventing solutions.
Why people choose to grow their careers at UBDS Group
Professionals choose to grow their careers at UBDS Group for its reputation as a dynamic and forward-thinking organisation that is deeply committed to both innovation and employee development. At UBDS Group, employees are given unique opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects across a diverse range of industries, exposing them to new challenges and learning opportunities that are pivotal for professional growth. The Group’s culture emphasises continuous improvement, offering ample training programs, mentorship, and the chance to gain certifications that enhance their skills and marketability. 
UBDS Group fosters a collaborative environment where creativity and innovation are encouraged, allowing employees to contribute ideas and solutions that have a tangible impact on the company and its clients. This combination of professional development, a culture of innovation, and the opportunity to make meaningful contributions makes UBDS Group an attractive place for those looking to advance their careers and be at the forefront of technological and operational excellence.
Employee Benefits
Training – All team members are offered a number of options in terms of personal development, whether it is technical led, business acumen or methodologies. We want you to grow with us and to help us achieve more
Private medical cover for you and your spouse/partner, offered via Vitality
Discretionary bonus based on a blend of personal and company performance
Holiday – You will receive 25 Days holiday, plus 1 day for Birthday and 1 day for your work anniversary in addition to UK bank holidays
Electric Vehicle leasing with salary sacrifice
Contributed Pension Scheme
Death in service cover
About UBDS Group
At UBDS Group our mission is to support entrepreneurs who are setting new standards with technology solutions across cloud services, cybersecurity, data and AI, ensuring that every investment advances our commitment to innovation, making a difference, and creating impactful solutions for organisations and society. 
Equal Opportunities
We are an equal opportunities employer and do not discriminate on the grounds of gender, sexual orientation, marital or civil partner status, pregnancy or maternity, gender reassignment, race, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, disability or age.
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Senior Data Engineer, you will design, build, and operate production-grade data solutions while leading technical thinking and supporting teams. You will work closely with clients and stakeholders to deliver high-quality outcomes that address real-world business problems.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should have strong SQL and Python skills, experience with data engineering frameworks, and a solid understanding of cloud data platforms. A user-centered approach and the ability to communicate insights effectively are also essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: We're in the business of confidence.

title: HVACR Technician
description_text:
James A. Wheat & Sons is looking for a skilled HVACR Technician to join our team, providing heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration solutions in Montgomery County and Washington DC. This role involves maintaining residential systems for optimal performance and requires strong technical skills and problem-solving abilities. Candidates will enhance their skills in a supportive environment, with competitive pay, benefits, and career advancement opportunities. If you are eager to advance your HVAC career with a customer-focused company, we invite you to apply.
Responsibilities
Install, maintain, and repair HVACR systems in residential settings.
Diagnose and troubleshoot HVACR equipment malfunctions.
Perform routine maintenance on HVACR systems to ensure optimal performance.
Respond to service calls and provide timely and effective solutions to customer issues.
Educate customers on system operation and maintenance best practices.
Keep detailed records of work performed and parts used on service calls.
Assist in training and mentoring junior technicians as needed.
High school diploma or equivalent; technical training in HVACR preferred.
Valid HVACR Journeyman or Masters license and certifications as required by local regulations.
Strong understanding of HVACR systems and components.
Ability to read and interpret blueprints, schematics, and technical drawings.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and customer service skills.
Paid Time Off and Holidays
Training & Development
Weekly Performance Bonus
Weekly Pay
Health Insurance - after 60 days employment
Dental Insurance - after 60 days employment
Vision Insurance - after 60 days employment
401k - after 6 months employment
401k 4% company match - after 6 months employment
Company vehicle to drive to and from work
Company iPad
Company Wi-Fi Card
Monthly Phone Stipend
Company Tool Loan Program to help you get the tools you need to excel in your career
Many Bonus and Commission opportunities 
Company Profit Share Program
3 Company Outings a year (Company paid events for the team to relax and have fun together)
Employee, Friends and Family discount
A Family Environment - NOT CORPORATE OWNED or OPERATED
ai_benefits: Company vehicle to drive to and from work
ai_core_responsibilities: The technician will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and repairing HVACR systems in residential settings, which includes diagnosing malfunctions and performing routine maintenance to ensure optimal performance. Key duties also involve responding to service calls, providing timely solutions, educating customers, and maintaining detailed work records.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a high school diploma or equivalent, with technical training in HVACR preferred, and hold a valid HVACR Journeyman or Masters license as required by local regulations. Essential qualifications include a strong understanding of HVACR systems, the ability to interpret technical drawings, and excellent problem-solving and customer service skills.
linkedin_org_slogan: Wheat & Sons is a family-owned business that strives to provide plumbing and HVAC services that exceed your expectations
linkedin_org_locations: 7839 Beechcraft Ave, Gaithersburg, Maryland 20879, US
linkedin_org_description: James A. Wheat & Sons, Inc. is a family-owned and locally operated business that has been serving residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Montgomery County, Maryland, and NW Washington, DC since 1978. For two generations, our plumbing and HVAC company has been striving to provide plumbing, heating, air conditioning, and fireplace services that exceed your expectations.

title: Charlotte Tilbury Beauty Expert
description_text:
Charlotte Tilbury Beauty Expert
Location: Various locations
About Charlotte Tilbury:
Founded by renowned makeup artist Charlotte Tilbury, our brands revolutionize the beauty industry with luxury, innovative, and effective products that inspire confidence. Our mission is to empower everyone to be the most beautiful version of themselves. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds to help drive our brand forward.
The Charlotte Tilbury Beauty Expert role is at the heart of our brand, where you will inspire and inform our customers about our exceptional product range, creating memorable experiences that leave them feeling fabulous.
Key Responsibilities:
Deliver an exceptional customer experience by applying your expertise in makeup artistry and product knowledge.
Drive sales through effective customer engagement, building relationships, and inspiring clients with your passion for beauty.
Maintain the highest standards of store presentation and cleanliness.
Be a brand ambassador, representing Charlotte Tilbury with enthusiasm and professionalism.
Stay updated on product knowledge, beauty trends, and industry insights to inform customers effectively.
Qualifications:
Previous experience in a beauty or luxury retail environment is preferred.
Passion for makeup and skincare, with an understanding of beauty trends.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to build relationships with customers.
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, achieving sales objectives while maintaining a positive attitude.
Flexible availability, including weekends and holidays.
Culture of the Team:
We win together as a team, supporting and developing each other through our careers. We embrace the challenge and work in an incredibly fast-paced environment with high expectations set by the business, shaking it up with our creative lead, Charlotte Tilbury, as we revolutionize the beauty industry. We love what we do, and we support each other while we share the magic with the world.
*OUR MISSION
‘TO MAKE EVERYONE FEEL THE MOST BEAUTIFUL VERSION OF THEMSELVES’
ai_core_responsibilities: The Charlotte Tilbury Beauty Expert will deliver an exceptional customer experience by applying makeup artistry expertise and product knowledge. They will drive sales through effective customer engagement and maintain high standards of store presentation.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have previous experience in a beauty or luxury retail environment and a passion for makeup and skincare. Excellent communication skills and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment are essential.
linkedin_org_locations: 8 Surrey Street, London, WC2R 2ND, GB
Lafayette Street, New York, US
17/F, Crystal Galleria , 68 Yuyuan Road, Jing’an Shanghai, Shanghai, CN
10/F, Lee Garden Five, 18 Hysan Avenue, Causeway Bay, Hong Kong, HK
linkedin_org_description:
Founded by British makeup artist and beauty entrepreneur Charlotte Tilbury MBE in 2013, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has revolutionised the face of the global beauty industry by de-coding makeup applications for everyone, everywhere, with an easy-to-use, easy-to-choose, easy-to-gift range. Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty continues to break records across countries, channels, and categories and to scale at pace.

Over the last 10 years, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has experienced exceptional growth and is one of the most talked about brands in the beauty industry and beyond. It has become a global sensation across 50 markets (and growing), with over 2,300 employees globally who are part of the Dream Team making the magic happen.

Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty is a truly global business, delivering market-leading growth, innovative retail and product launches fuelled by industry-leading tech — all with an internal culture of embracing challenges, disruptive thinking, winning together, and sharing the magic. The energy behind the bran­d is infectious, and as we grow, we are always looking for extraordinary talent who want to be part of this our success and help drive our limitless ambitions.

title: Tenant Management Associate
description_text:
About the Role
Are you a process-driven professional who loves creating seamless experiences? SmartCrowd is hiring a Tenant Management Associate to own the entire tenant lifecycle and ensure our operations run like clockwork. Based in Dubai, you will be the primary point of contact for our residents, managing everything from compliance and contracts to daily inquiries. This is a mission-critical role for someone who is exceptionally organized, firm yet empathetic, and ready to learn the ropes of a regulated FinTech platform.
About SmartCrowd
SmartCrowd is where fintech innovation meets real estate investment. Our mission is to make real estate investing accessible through fractional ownership. We create opportunities for investors of different backgrounds to participate in quality property investments. Our team combines technology, analysis, and creativity to redefine how people in the region build wealth.
What You'll Do
Onboarding & Compliance: Conduct thorough compliance checks for all incoming tenants and manage the preparation and signing of tenancy contracts.
Lease Administration: Execute Ejari and DIFC lease registrations and manage tenant-side operations on platforms like Keyper and Tern.
Financial Oversight: Manage cheque and payment collection, track rental payments rigorously, and ensure the collection of renewal admin fees.
Tenant Experience: Act as the main point of contact for tenant concerns, including maintenance requests, rent delays, and early terminations.
Utility & Logistics: Assist tenants with utilities registration and manage the complete move-in and move-out process.
Coordination: Manage notices, renewals, and coordinate with tenants for property viewings and inspections.
Communication & Records: Own the info@windsorestates.ae inbox and maintain meticulous records to support the Head of Operations.
What We're Looking For
Education & Experience: 0–2 years of experience. We encourage fresh graduates with a degree in Business, Real Estate, or a related field to apply.
Quick Learner: Ability to quickly grasp Dubai Land Department (DLD) regulations, Ejari systems, and DIFC lease requirements.
Tech Savvy: Comfortable learning and using property management software (Keyper/Tern experience is a major plus).
Communication: Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to handle conversations professionally and with empathy.
Detail-Oriented: A "zero-mistake" mindset when it comes to contracts and financial tracking.
Location: Must be based in Dubai, this is an on-site role.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Associate will manage the entire tenant lifecycle, including conducting compliance checks, executing lease registrations (Ejari/DIFC), and overseeing financial aspects like rent collection. They will also serve as the primary contact for tenant concerns, coordinate logistics like utilities and move-in/out, and manage renewals and property viewings.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 0-2 years of experience, preferably with a degree in Business or Real Estate, and must be quick learners capable of grasping DLD regulations and Ejari systems. A detail-oriented approach is crucial for contract and financial tracking, alongside excellent interpersonal skills for professional communication.

title: Freelance Statistics Expert with Python Expertise - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, Statsmodels, and Scikit-learn); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for statisticians with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Statistics or related fields, e.g. Probability Theory, Mathematical Statistics, Applied Statistics, etc. 
3+ years of professional mathematics experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PStat, CAP, SAS Certifications) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $34/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design rigorous statistics problems reflecting professional practice and evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints. They will also validate calculations using Python libraries and improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be statisticians with professional experience in Python, open to part-time, non-permanent projects, ideally holding a degree in Statistics or related fields. A minimum of 3 years of professional mathematics experience and strong written English proficiency (C1/C2) are required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

title: Application Support Engineer
description_text:
Join the Future of Technology with ZILO™
At ZILO™, we're redefining what’s possible in technology. ZILO™ is the UK-based FinTech specialising in global asset and wealth management software, designed to scale and transform businesses of all types using our own developed AI Technology. Our mission is to digitalise the future of the global asset management industry. 
We are a team of experts with decades of combined experience at leading firms globally, who thrive in fast-paced environments and want to shape the future of technology. Every individual plays a key role in driving progress and making a real impact. We continuously strive to innovate and improve.
Why work with us? At ZILO™, you'll be part of a dynamic and inclusive environment where creativity thrives. We offer the opportunity to work on cutting-edge technology, collaborate with talented individuals, and contribute to projects that have a real-world impact. We value continuous learning, personal growth, and providing our team with the resources they need to succeed.
Ready to shape the future? Let’s talk.
This role is hybrid and will require regular weekly attendance at our London office.
We’re looking for an Application Support Engineer to join our team — someone who thrives on solving complex production issues, understands how applications behave in the real world, and takes pride in keeping systems reliable and performant.
This is not a platform engineering role. You won’t just be spinning up Kubernetes clusters or building infrastructure — you’ll be deeply involved in understanding our applications, what they do and how they operate, troubleshooting real-world issues, and working directly on improvements that impact our customers every day.
What You’ll Do
Incident Response & Troubleshooting: Investigate and resolve incidents raised by clients, diving into logs, metrics, and application code to identify root causes. 
Application Debugging: Work across our core stack — Java, Golang, and Python — to trace and fix issues affecting reliability or performance. 
Data Fixes: Perform data investigation and fixes using Postgres. 
Operational Excellence: Patch and maintain Kubernetes clusters and other production systems. 
SRE Roadmap: Contribute to the continuous improvement of our observability, reliability, and automation initiatives.
Solid experience with application debugging in at least one of: Java, Golang, or Python. 
A good grasp of PostgreSQL — enough to run queries, analyse data, and perform safe fixes. 
Familiarity with Kubernetes and modern cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure). 
Understanding of incident management, observability tools (Grafana, Prometheus, etc.)

title: Senior Associate, Insights (Hedge Funds)
description_text:
About Dialectica
Dialectica is a B2B information services firm that serves the world's top business professionals, enabling them to gather real-time information and insights. We have been recognized as one of Europe’s fastest-growing companies by the Financial Times for 4 years in a row. Our culture is dynamic, ambitious, and highly competitive.
About the Role
As a Senior Associate / Manager, you will play a hands-on role in scaling operational excellence and driving product innovation for our Hedge Fund client base. You will have direct exposure to Hedge Fund traders and analysts.
Reporting directly to the Global Head of Insights, you will be responsible for the seamless delivery of our products, ensuring that our content meets the high-velocity demands of public market investors. You will bridge the gap between our expert recruitment teams and our product vision, leading the charge on developing new insight formats that deliver custom research and ultimately alpha to our public market clients.
What you will do
1. Strategy and Product Innovation
Commercial, Product Strategy and Delivery: Define the strategy (commercial and product) and guarantee delivery of high-frequency data products, such as quantitative channel checks, supply chain monitoring, and sentiment trackers that provide Hedge Funds with real-time market signals. 
Tech-Enabled Innovation: Lead initiatives to incorporate advanced technologies and innovative solutions into our product suite, such as leveraging AI to synthesize expert interviews into rapid-consumption formats for traders.
New Product Development and Delivery: Collaborate with product teams to enhance our custom research offering based on client needs and market trends, ensuring we are first-to-market with insights on volatile tickers or market events
2. Content Operations & Delivery
Operational Excellence: Develop and implement efficient operational processes to ensure the seamless delivery of insights. You will identify bottlenecks in how we source and package data to match the speed of the public markets.
Quality Assurance: Oversee daily operations, ensuring adherence to quality standards and timelines. You will implement quality control measures to ensure survey data reliability and compliance safety.
Supply-Side Enablement: Work with our expert recruitment teams to build specific expert pools relevant to liquid markets (e.g., distributors, buyers, and competitors for channel check surveys).
3. Commercial Support & Strategy
Sales Collaboration: Collaborate with the sales team to support business development efforts, arming them with unique channel check data and proprietary content to pitch to Analysts and Portfolio Managers.
Growth Opportunities: Identify and capitalize on growth opportunities within existing client accounts, recommending tailored solutions like recurring survey trackers.
Strategic Planning: Develop and execute strategic plans to drive the growth and success of our products.
4. Team Coordination & Leadership
Culture: Foster a collaborative and motivating work environment, encouraging team members to achieve their full potential.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Foster strong relationships with internal and client teams to optimize delivery of work products.
What we are looking for
Experience: 3–5 years of experience in consulting or financial services. Specific experience in Equity Research, Hedge Funds, or building primary research products (Surveys/Channel Checks) is highly preferred.
Innovation Mindset: A builder who can take a concept (e.g., "we need a semiconductor channel check") and turn it into a scalable product.
Commercial Acumen: Strong commercial acumen with a focus on driving growth and profitability.
Analytical Skills: Data-driven mindset with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Interpersonal Skills: Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
Adaptability: Proactive and results-oriented with a focus on continuous improvement.
What you will get out of it
Competitive compensation package
Learning and development opportunities 
Reward and recognition programs
Company sponsored team-bonding events
Weekly health and wellness activities (e.g. basketball, football, yoga, running)
Support and coaching from some of the most engaging colleagues around
A diverse and inclusive culture supported through a number of initiatives
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Associate/Manager will define the commercial and product strategy for high-frequency data products and lead initiatives to incorporate advanced technologies like AI into the product suite for Hedge Fund clients. They will also develop efficient operational processes, oversee quality assurance, and collaborate with sales teams to support business development efforts.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should possess 3–5 years of experience in consulting or financial services, with a strong preference for experience in Equity Research, Hedge Funds, or primary research product development. Essential qualities include a builder mindset, strong commercial acumen, data-driven analytical skills, and excellent interpersonal abilities.

linkedin_org_description:
Dialectica is a B2B information services firm that serves the world's top business professionals, by enabling them to gather real-time information and insights from industry experts across various markets, industries, and regions.

Driven by our mission to achieve unparalleled customer recognition, we are developing the most trusted and innovative knowledge-sharing platform in the world. 

Dialectica has been recognized as one of Europe’s fastest-growing companies by the Financial Times for 5 years in a row, a Top Employer for Recent Graduates by The Career Directory in Canada, a Great Place to Work in Greece, Vietnam and Canada and a Best Workplace in Europe.  

We believe in supporting our people to do their best work and grow, and building a dynamic, empowering, and respectful workplace is core to our purpose: Accelerate the shift to a prosperous society by empowering better decision-making

With a global presence spanning 6 offices across 3 continents and a highly-trained team of over 1,400 employees, we are always looking for individuals who share our belief that our work transcends individual and organizational benefits, contributing significantly to the collective advancement of society.

title: Finance Lead
description_text:
We are at a pivotal stage in our growth as a regulated healthcare / life sciences business, moving from early-stage foundations into a phase of greater scale, maturity, and operational rigour. As we move towards product-market-fit and grow the organisation, we are strengthening our team to ensure we have the financial insight, discipline, and processes needed to support good decision-making. 
The Finance Lead will play a central role in this next phase, helping to deepen the financial understanding of our business, mature our financial operations, and support future growth ambitions, including potential fundraising or investment activity. This trusted role will work closely with the executive team to ensure the company is financially robust, compliant, and well positioned for the next stage of growth.
The Role 
The Finance Lead is responsible for the overall financial health of the company, with end-to-end ownership of financial processes, planning, analysis, budgeting, and reporting. The role oversees outsourced accounting services, while ensuring that financial processes, controls, and insights are fit for a growing, regulated organisation. 
As a key contributor to the company’s strategic direction, the Finance Lead supports data-driven decision-making across the business, works closely with other functions and the senior management team (including the CEO on fundraising activity), and drives continuous improvement in financial operations. This is a hands-on role, combining strategic leadership with practical execution in a lean environment. 
The role reports to the COO and will be part of the broader Ops team. It is supported by outsourced bookkeepers and by a part-time contractor. This role will not be right for you if you want to manage a large team. 
The role is hybrid (Leeds) or fully remote. It is a full-time role, but we are open to candidates who would prefer 80% FTE. We cannot sponsor visas to work in the UK. 
What you will do:
Running an effective, scalable and compliant finance function 
Own day-to-day financial operations, including payroll, invoicing, A/P and A/R, bookkeeping, VAT and tax, ensuring transactions are accurate, timely, and well controlled across both the UK business and the US subsidiary. 
Ensure financial, corporate, and regulatory compliance, including timely statutory reporting, adherence to UK and US regulatory requirements, and oversight of audits, tax planning, and financial risk management. 
Lead cash flow, working capital, and treasury management, maintaining liquidity, forecasting cash requirements, and ensuring the business is well positioned to fund operations and strategic investment. 
Manage outsourced bookkeeping partner and external advisors (as needed) 
Improve finance processes, systems and controls, improving the quality, speed, and usefulness of financial information as the business scales. 
Supporting business strategy, growth and reporting 
Own financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and analysis, providing forward-looking insight to support strategic planning, growth initiatives, and operational decision-making. 
Deliver high-quality financial reporting and analysis, including monthly management accounts, board reporting, financial modelling and scenario planning, improving understanding of unit economics and key financial levers. 
Act as a trusted finance business partner to the senior management team and departmental leaders, translating financial data into clear insights, challenging assumptions where appropriate, helping teams grow their financial understanding and ensuring cost controls. 
Support capital strategy and fundraising activity, working closely with the CEO and COO on fundraising (including financial modelling, and investor due diligence support), investor reporting, and debt or equity financing. 
Required 
Proven experience in a senior finance role (e.g. Finance Lead, Head of Finance), with end-to-end responsibility for the finance function in a growing business at a similar stage (Start-up Series A / B) 
Strong experience in financial planning, forecasting, management reporting, budgeting, and cash flow management, with clear ownership of outputs and quality 
Experience supporting a B2B business, ideally with exposure to both recurring revenue and project-based income models 
Demonstrated ability to define, analyse, and improve profitability, including developing a clear understanding of margins, unit economics, and financial performance across different parts of the business 
Track record of producing high-quality board-level reporting, including clear narrative, insight, and recommendations to support strategic decision-making 
Professional accounting qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) or equivalent experience 
Desirable 
Experience operating across multiple entities and jurisdictions, particularly UK and US 
Experience working as finance leader for a PE/VC portfolio company 
Exposure to fundraising, investor reporting, or financial (and customer) due diligence, supporting senior leaders through investment processes 
Track record of improving finance processes and reporting in a scaling business 
Background in healthcare, life sciences, SaaS, or adjacent B2B sectors 
Capabilities and behaviours we are looking for 
Living our values
Highly collaborative, working effectively across teams and functions to build strong relationships and trust 
Excellent communicator, able to explain financial concepts clearly to non-financial colleagues and adapt style for different audiences 
Credible and confident with senior stakeholders, including the Board and investors, with the ability to challenge constructively and influence outcomes 
Comfortable moving between strategic leadership and hands-on execution, taking ownership of outcomes and stepping into the detail when needed in a lean environment 
Detail-oriented, pragmatic, and resilient, maintaining accuracy and sound judgement while operating at pace and managing competing priorities 
Able to introduce appropriate structure, controls, and discipline, improving clarity and robustness without creating unnecessary complexity 
Gross salary: £85-92K per year for FTE 
28 days annual leave pro-rata (plus public holidays) on fulltime FTE 
£500 annual training budget 
£200 home working budget (one-off) 
Flexible working with core hours 
Enhanced (family & parental) leave policies 
Up to 6% pension match (salary sacrifice) 
About Little Journey 
Little Journey was founded by Dr Chris Evans, a paediatric anaesthetist and researcher who witnessed first-hand the impact that procedural anxiety, poor preparation, and inconsistent communication can have on patient experience, engagement, and clinical outcomes. Partnering with Sophie Copley, an expert in human-centred product design, the healthcare platform was developed and demonstrated significant benefits in improving care efficiency, compliance and patient experience. It was rapidly adopted across the NHS supporting children undergoing surgery, before being expanded to support a wide variety of common procedures. 
Building on this initial success, Little Journey expanded into clinical trials, applying the same principles of personalised education, emotional support, and engagement to research settings. Our Life Science platform supports trial participants and their families across therapeutic areas, to alleviate the burdens they face as they navigate participating in a clinical trial. The platform is now a global solution, and we work with major pharmaceutical companies. 
Little Journey is a Series A HealthTech start-up, and we are a highly collaborative, mission-driven organisation with a team of about 30 (including Product & Tech, Sales & Marketing, Operations & Delivery). We aim to change the future of personalised paediatric care, one empowered family at a time. We value honesty and integrity, trust and accountability, reflection and learning, proactive thinking, and a deep commitment to helping others. 

Diversity & Inclusion 
We encourage people from all walks of life to apply and strive to eliminate unconscious bias in our recruitment process. We do not discriminate on ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran or parental status or disability. We encourage candidates from underrepresented groups to apply. If you need adjustments made to our application process, to help accommodate any disabilities, please let us know on hr@littlejourney.health
ai_core_responsibilities: The Finance Lead is responsible for the overall financial health of the company, owning end-to-end financial processes including planning, analysis, budgeting, and reporting while overseeing outsourced accounting services. This role supports strategic direction by providing data-driven insights and working closely with the executive team, including on fundraising activities.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have proven experience in a senior finance role with end-to-end responsibility in a growing business, strong expertise in financial planning, reporting, and cash flow management, and hold a professional accounting qualification or equivalent experience. Experience supporting a B2B business, ideally with recurring revenue models, and demonstrating profitability analysis skills is required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Little Journey supports children & their families undergoing healthcare procedures or participating in clinical trials
linkedin_org_locations: Discovery Way, Nexus - University of Leeds, Leeds, England LS2 3AA, GB
London, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Little Journey is a digital eSupport platform designed to psychologically prepare, support and inform children and families before, during and after healthcare interactions - both those occurring as part of a clinical trial and within routine care.

Our mission is to support all children to better health. We believe that, through the provision of engaging, interactive and age-appropriate content, we can improve healthcare experiences and health outcomes for all.

The Little Journey smartphone app enables families to access information and support tailored to their child’s age, procedure type and hospital at a time and location convenient to them. Virtual tours using 360-degree images of the actual rooms families will visit explain what will happen and who children will meet during their hospital journey. Relaxation animations and therapeutic/distraction games further help to reduce anxiety and improve experiences. A parent information section includes articles, checklists and hospital guides – all of which can be configured by healthcare organisations via a web portal. 

We ensure a truly patient-focused user experience by co-creating content, using a continuous feedback loop with children, families and key stakeholders.

Little Journey fundamentally believes in the power of familiarisation and desensitization through immersive play to facilitate individual coping strategy development, reducing peri-procedural anxiety and improving healthcare experiences for all.

title: Real Estate Agent (Secondary Market)
description_text:
Tanami Properties is a premier real estate agency in Dubai, renowned for its expertise in both the secondary market and luxury properties. We provide clients with access to high-demand resale properties across Dubai, helping them buy, sell, and invest smartly. Join our team and take part in shaping Dubai’s thriving real estate market.
Role Overview:
We are looking for a driven and results-oriented Secondary Market Real Estate Agent to join Tanami Properties. You will focus on the resale of residential and commercial properties, guiding clients through smooth transactions, and connecting buyers with the best investment opportunities in Dubai’s secondary market.
Key Responsibilities:
Buy, sell, and lease resale properties across UAE.
Build and maintain strong relationships with buyers, sellers, and property investors.
Advise clients on market conditions, pricing trends, and investment potential.
Conduct property viewings, negotiate deals, and manage contracts.
Generate and manage leads through referrals, digital marketing, and networking.
Achieve and exceed individual and team sales targets. 
Proven sales experience; secondary market real estate experience preferred
Strong negotiation, communication, and presentation skills 
Goal-oriented, self-motivated, and able to work independently
Ability to build long-term relationships with clients and investors
Unlimited Earning Potential with our Competitive Commission Structure
Rewards for Top Performers – Recognition and incentives for your outstanding achievements.

title: Destination Operations Manager
description_text:
About us
Founded in 2015 in Athens, Greece, Welcome redefines the way people travel by going above and beyond the commoditized transfer service and being the first company to deliver a complete, personalised, in-destination travel experience. From the moment a traveler arrives at a new destination, until their return journey home, Welcome accommodates all their travel needs, including transfers, sightseeing trips, and local information, in the easiest, friendliest, and most personalised way possible. Welcome's drivers are experts in the area and share their local know-how to make travellers feel at home wherever they are. The company has also introduced contactless rides, thorough cleaning protocols, and protective equipment to make every journey safe.
Being a travel tech startup, Welcome continues to grow and scale its operations and is quickly becoming a global category leader for in-destination travel services.
One of the highest-rated global transportation companies with a rating of 4.9/5 stars.
Expanded from 200 destinations last year to 350, achieving our ambitious 2024 growth target.
Over 4,000 travel partners including 2,500 hotels, numerous vacation rentals, and travel agents, adding 50+ new ones every month.
Over 2.5 million happy travellers every year.
⭐️ If you want to dive deeper into the awesomeness of Welcome's culture, click on to check our TikTok account.⭐️
The Team
We are a group of vibrant, diverse people who love travelling and never settle on quality. Each one of us didn’t join Welcome by chance and believes deeply in what Welcome is trying to achieve, so we work relentlessly to make that happen. We challenge common logic, focus on design, put simplicity and usability first, and create memorable experiences. We keep learning and exploring better ways to serve our community and grow personally and professionally in our respective fields. We stay humble along the way, with a “pay it forward” mentality, but with big and bold goals.
As a Destination Operations Manager, you'll own the operational performance, driver engagement, and local health of one or more high-priority destinations. This is a senior operational role where you'll be fully accountable for results—ensuring your destinations deliver exceptional service while maintaining close connections with the driver community and gathering critical insights from the field.You'll work closely with your Regional Manager and central teams to ensure destinations run smoothly, are correctly configured, and reflect local realities in pricing and capacity decisions. This role requires a strong blend of analytical thinking, hands-on execution, and stakeholder management.
Responsibilities
Destination Performance & Operational Execution
Own end-to-end operational performance for your assigned destinations, tracking transfer success rates, driver utilization, and service quality metrics
Execute routine data analysis to ensure key operational performance indicators (KPIs) are met and take corrective action when thresholds are breached
Assist in forecasting demand surges and seasonal patterns, ensuring proper resource allocation and driver availability
Report inefficiencies and suggest improvements in driver scheduling, dispatching, and allocation models
Conduct weekly reviews of operational data, ensuring continuous optimization of destination workflows
Maintain destination configurations including cutoff times, vehicle limits, and pricing strategies based on live data analysis
Demand-Supply Balancing & Driver Allocation
Track driver availability across your destinations, ensuring proper service capacity during peak and off-peak periods
Assist in the optimization of incentive programs to keep drivers engaged and aligned with operational targets
Work closely with the Acquisition & Recruiting team to highlight geographic or time-slot-based driver shortages
Conduct basic data analysis on operational trends, supporting proactive decision-making on driver allocation
Monitor and refine bookings allocation for peak-hour stability, ensuring high service reliability
Transfer Success & Service Reliability
Track and work towards reducing last-minute cancellations, ensuring transfer fulfillment aligns with service commitments
Monitor service-level disruptions, identifying patterns that require further operational intervention
Communicate emerging service risks to Live Operations team to trigger rapid corrective measures
Work with Quality & Compliance teams to flag recurring driver issues impacting service efficiency
Local Market Intelligence & Community Engagement
Capture reliable intelligence on pricing, driver costs, competitor pay, local regulations, and community sentiment
Feed structured insights to central teams (Pricing, Strategy) to inform decision-making
Build visibility and trust with local drivers through regular engagement activities
Identify and manage driver ambassadors, collect feedback, and run initiatives to improve satisfaction
Organize incentive campaigns, driver quests, or reactivation pushes—track results and share learnings
Cross-Functional Collaboration & Continuous Improvement
Collaborate with Live Operations team to provide real-time visibility on driver availability and operational risks
Partner with Acquisition & Recruiting to proactively communicate driver shortages or surpluses
Work closely with the Pricing team to ensure operational decisions align with cost efficiency and budget targets
Coordinate with Business Intelligence and Data Analytics teams to support performance tracking and data-driven operational decisions
3+ years of experience in operations, logistics, mobility, or marketplace management.
Proven track record of managing performance and executing improvement actions based on data insights.
Strong analytical skills — comfortable using dashboards, reports, and KPIs to guide decisions.
Hands-on problem solver with a proactive approach to spotting and addressing operational challenges.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management, able to build relationships across drivers, partners, and internal teams.
Understanding of local market conditions, including supply/demand dynamics, competitor landscape, and regulatory requirements.
Fluent in English, with local language skills considered an advantage.
Energy and ownership mindset — you take responsibility for results and don’t wait to be told what to do.
Vibrant and fresh work environment
Flexible work-from-home policy
The tools you need to perform your daily tasks successfully
L&D personal budget
Private Insurance Plan
+4 extra PTO days annually
The unique opportunity to join “the next big thing” at ground level
ai_core_responsibilities: The Destination Operations Manager will own the operational performance and driver engagement for high-priority destinations, ensuring exceptional service delivery. This role involves data analysis, resource allocation, and collaboration with various teams to optimize operations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3+ years of experience in operations or logistics, with strong analytical skills and a proven track record in performance management. Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities are essential, along with an understanding of local market conditions.

Welcome redefines the way people travel by offering the most personalized and wholesome travel experience from the moment they start planning their trip until the moment they return home. Welcome helps you make the most of your travels by providing transfer services, essential products, pre-ordered tickets to top attractions, tips and tailor-made experiences led by locals.

title: Sales Support Coordinator
description_text:
The Sales Support Coordinator will support the Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) and the wider Sales Leadership team. This is a fast-paced commercial environment and requires someone who can confidently manage executive coordination, highimpact PowerPoint presentations, sales analytics, meeting operations, travel planning, and professional stakeholder communication. The successful candidate will be a trusted “right hand” to the CRO office — ensuring smooth execution of leadership priorities, exceptional meeting readiness, and consistent, executive-level output across communications, reporting, and coordination.
Responsibilities:
CRO & Sales Leadership Support
Provide day-to-day administrative and business support to the CRO and sales leadership team. 
Act as a coordination hub between Sales, Marketing, Finance, HR, Delivery, and executive stakeholders. 
Maintain strict confidentiality, professionalism, and discretion in all communications and documents.
PowerPoint & Executive Presentation Development
Build, edit, and refine executive-level PowerPoint decks (CRO updates, board reports, QBRs, leadership presentations). 
Ensure presentations are aligned with brand standards, consistent formatting, and clear executive storytelling. 
Convert raw data and inputs into clean slides, visuals, charts, and summaries. 
Sales Analytics & Reporting
Support the CRO office with sales performance analytics and reporting (weekly/monthly dashboards). 
Prepare reports and summaries on pipeline, forecast, performance trends, and key sales KPIs. 
Consolidate inputs from sales teams into structured, decision-ready formats. 
Meetings, Calendars & Leadership Operating Rhythm
Own scheduling and organization of CRO meetings (internal leadership, customer meetings, QBRs, GTM sessions). 
Prepare meeting agendas, briefing packs, talking points, and minutes/action logs. 
Track follow-ups and ensure closure on action items across teams. 
Maintain a structured cadence for recurring leadership meetings and reporting cycles. 
Travel, Logistics & Event Coordination 
Manage travel planning and logistics for the CRO and sales leadership (flights, hotels, visas, ground transport). 
Prepare travel itineraries, meeting schedules, and travel packs. 
Coordinate sales offsites, leadership workshops, and internal events when required. 
Corporate Communication & Stakeholder Coordination 
Draft professional communications on behalf of the CRO office (emails, announcements, followups). 
Coordinate internal alignment and ensure timely updates across stakeholders. 
Support preparation of executive summaries, memos, and formal business correspondence.
3+ years of experience in a similar role (Sales Support, Executive Support, Sales Operations, Business Support, EA for Sales Leadership, CRO office support, etc.). 
Experience in telecom, ICT, technology services, cloud, cybersecurity, data/AI, or B2B enterprise sales environments.
Experience supporting senior executives (CRO, CEO, VP Sales, GM, etc.). 
Familiarity with CRM tools (Salesforce or equivalent). 
Basic Power BI understanding (or strong interest in reporting and dashboards). 
Experience supporting QBRs, forecast calls, and pipeline governance.Advanced proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint (executive deck building is a must). 
Strong analytical skills and confidence working with Excel-based reporting, KPIs, and dashboards. 
Excellent calendar management and meeting coordination capability. 
Experience handling complex travel arrangements and logistics. 
Strong written English and corporate communication skills (Arabic is a plus). 
Strong attention to detail, formatting, and high standards of output quality. 
Ability to work under pressure, manage multiple priorities, and deliver quickly without sacrificing accuracy.
ai_core_responsibilities: The coordinator will provide day-to-day administrative and business support to the Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) and sales leadership, acting as a central coordination hub between various departments like Sales, Marketing, and Finance. Key duties include developing executive-level PowerPoint decks, preparing sales performance analytics and reports, and managing the leadership's operating rhythm including complex scheduling and travel logistics.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess at least 3 years of experience in a similar support role, preferably within technology or B2B enterprise sales environments, and have supported senior executives like a CRO or VP of Sales. Essential technical requirements include advanced proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint for executive deck building, strong analytical skills with Excel reporting, familiarity with CRM tools like Salesforce, and experience supporting QBRs and forecast calls.

ZainTECH is a regional integrated digital solutions provider, unifying Zain Group’s ICT assets to offer a unique value proposition of comprehensive digital solutions and services under one roof. The company is positioned to drive the transformation of enterprise and government customers in the MENA region by providing a center of excellence and managed solutions across the ICT stack, including cloud, cybersecurity, modern infrastructure, big data, IoT, AI, smart cities, drones and robotics, and emerging technologies. 
 
ZainTECH leverages Zain’s global reach, unique regional footprint, and infrastructure across its operations in Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Jordan, Iraq, and the United Arab Emirates, as well as in other key markets in the Middle East.

ZainTECH forms a key pillar in the evolution of Zain’s core telecom business to maximize value and build on the company’s many strengths to selectively create and invest in growth verticals beyond standard mobile services. This ultimately supports Zain’s vision of becoming a leading ICT and digital lifestyle provider.

title: Influencer Marketing Intern
description_text:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients
Min Experience: 0 years
Location: Gurgaon
JobType: full-time
We’re looking for a proactive and driven Influencer Marketing Intern to support creator partnerships and campaign execution in a fast-paced content commerce environment. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with creators, managing multiple conversations, and collaborating across teams to deliver impactful influencer campaigns.
Key Responsibilities
Identify, scout, and onboard creators onto the platform through structured outreach and follow-ups.
Act as a point of contact for influencers, handling campaign-related queries with clarity and speed.
Coordinate closely with internal teams to ensure smooth campaign planning, execution, and timely delivery.
Support creators with onboarding, tech-related issues, and campaign requirements by providing practical solutions.
Track influencer campaign performance, maintain reports, and share insights to help optimise future campaigns.
Ensure clear and consistent communication between creators, internal stakeholders, and external partners.
What Makes You a Great Fit
Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to build rapport quickly.
Comfortable multitasking and managing multiple creators, campaigns, and deadlines at the same time.
Quick learner who can adapt to new tools, platforms, and influencer-related challenges.
Problem-solving mindset with the ability to think on your feet and handle ambiguity.
Collaborative team player who enjoys working cross-functionally and contributing to shared goals.
Interest in influencer marketing, creator economy, and digital commerce is a strong advantage.
ai_core_responsibilities: The intern will be responsible for identifying, scouting, and onboarding creators, acting as the main point of contact for influencers, and coordinating internal teams for smooth campaign execution. Key duties also include supporting creators with technical issues and tracking campaign performance to provide optimization insights.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should possess strong verbal and written communication skills, be comfortable multitasking across multiple campaigns and deadlines, and demonstrate a quick learning ability to adapt to new platforms. A problem-solving mindset and a collaborative spirit are essential, along with a strong interest in influencer marketing and digital commerce.

title: Freelance Energy Engineer with Python Experience - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation isproject-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous energy engineering problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for energy engineers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Energy Engineering or related fields, e.g. Electrical Engineering, Power Systems Engineering, Renewable Energy Engineering, Electronics etc. 
3+ years of professional energy engineering experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PE, CEng, EMP, CEM) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $12/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will be responsible for designing rigorous energy engineering problems that reflect professional practice and evaluating AI solutions for correctness, constraints, and assumptions. They will also validate calculations using Python libraries and improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be energy engineers with professional experience and strong proficiency in Python for numerical validation, holding a degree in Energy Engineering or related fields. A minimum of 3 years of professional energy engineering experience and strong written English (C1/C2) are required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

title: Suomalainen asiakaspalvelija Kreikkaan
description_text:
Asiakaspalvelutyöntekijä – Kreikka
Haemme asiakaspalvelutyöntekijöitä erilaisiin asiakasprojekteihin. Tässä tehtävässä pääset työskentelemään monipuolisissa asiakaspalvelutehtävissä kansainvälisessä ympäristössä.
Työtehtävät
Asiakkaiden yhteydenottojen käsittely eri kanavissa (puhelin, chat, tiketöinti) 
Asiakaskysymysten ja -tilanteiden selvittäminen sekä ratkaisujen tarjoaminen 
Ammattimainen ja empaattinen asiakaspalvelu kaikissa palvelukanavissa 
Valmiiden ohjeiden ja laatukäytäntöjen noudattaminen 
Haastavampien tai herkkien tapausten eskalointi tarvittaessa 
Muut projektiin liittyvät tehtävät 
Aloituspäiviä on useita eri, riippuen projektista. 
Odotamme sinulta
Erinomaista suomen kielen taitoa 
Sujuvaa englannin kielen taitoa (kirjallinen ja suullinen) 
Aikaisempi asiakaspalvelukokemus ei ole pakollista, mutta katsotaan eduksi 
Hyviä kirjallisia taitoja ja sujuvaa näppäimistön käyttöä 
Perusvalmiuksia tietokoneen käyttöön ja kykyä työskennellä useissa järjestelmissä samanaikaisesti
Tarjoamme sinulle
Täyden tuen Kreikkaan muuttoon (lennot, hotellimajoitus alussa sekä apua asunnon etsintään – kaikki ilman kustannuksia sinulle) 
Kilpailukykyisen kuukausipalkan sekä 2 ylimääräistä palkkaa vuodessa 
Kaverisuositteluohjelman ja houkuttelevat bonukset 
Terveydenhuoltoedut ja muita henkilöstöetuja sekä alennuksia 
Kattavan koulutuksen sertifioitujen kouluttajien toimesta 
Mahdollisuuksia ammatilliseen kehittymiseen ja uralla etenemiseen 
Yhteisöllisiä tapahtumia sekä vastuullisuushankkeita 
Ilmaiset online-kreikan kielen kurssit 
Mahdollisuuden työskennellä Great Place to Work -sertifioidussa yrityksessä

title: LLM Engineer / EU Project Manager
description_text:
The LLM Engineer / EU Project Manager is a multidisciplinary role that combines hands-on development of Large Language Model (LLM)–based solutions with the coordination and management of European-funded research and innovation projects (e.g., Horizon Europe, Erasmus+, Digital Europe). The role focuses on designing AI-driven systems while ensuring effective project planning, reporting, and collaboration across international partners, aligning technical outcomes with EU project objectives and deliverables.
Note: The Greek-based position assumes a hybrid mode of work, whereas the Swiss-based one a remote (in Switzerland).
Key Responsibilities
LLM Engineering & Technical Development
Design, develop, and deploy applications leveraging Large Language Models (prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, evaluation workflows, and integrations). 
Contribute to the technical architecture and implementation of AI components within company and EU-funded projects. 
Develop prototypes, proof-of-concepts, and production-ready solutions aligned with project goals. 
Evaluate model performance, reliability, and ethical implications, ensuring compliance with EU AI and data protection standards. 
Collaborate with researchers, developers, and data scientists across partner organizations. 
EU Project Management
Coordinate technical work packages, tasks, and deliverables within EU-funded projects. 
Support proposal writing, especially in AI/LLM-related sections (technical approach, innovation impact, methodology). 
Track milestones, timelines, and deliverables to ensure compliance with grant agreements. 
Prepare technical reports, documentation, and contributions for periodic reviews. 
Facilitate communication and collaboration among international consortia partners.
Bachelor's or MSc degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong experience with LLM technologies, NLP, prompt engineering, and AI system integration. 
Proficiency in Python and experience working with APIs, cloud services, and data pipelines. 
Experience participating in or managing EU-funded projects (Horizon Europe, H2020, or similar). 
Understanding of project lifecycle management, deliverable planning, and reporting requirements. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English. 
Ability to work effectively in international, multidisciplinary teams.
Competitive remuneration package adjusted to proven skills and experience;
Excellent working conditions;
Exposure to training and professional development capabilities, including the ability to engage in cutting-edge research;
Exposure to international clients and collaborators.
ai_benefits: Exposure to training and professional development capabilities
Ability to engage in cutting-edge research
Exposure to international clients and collaborators
ai_core_responsibilities: This multidisciplinary role involves designing, developing, and deploying Large Language Model (LLM)-based solutions, including prompt engineering and RAG pipelines, while also coordinating technical work packages and deliverables for European-funded research and innovation projects.
